title: Waterfall clipping regression in 0.7.8-beta.r11
description:
User observed waterfall clipping regression after upgrading to 0.7.8-beta.r11. Tabled during khsa investigation — pick this up once khsa lands (or sooner if r11 is the actual culprit and the swizzle change needs reverting).

Symptom: not yet characterized (tabled before details captured). User will describe when work resumes.

Bisect range to investigate when picked back up:
- Good (suspect): r6 (093637e1c) — last build before khsa diagnostic chain started
- Bad: r11 fix (ce9280b49) — current head

Candidate commits in range:
- f3f09e732 r7  — diag dump only (additive, unlikely to break rendering)
- b7a14f097 r8  — r_alias_minlight cvar, lighting floor in R_DrawAliasModel (alias only, not water)
- 8ab6bf9bf r9  — gl_solid_format/gl_alpha_format changed from 3/4 → GL_RGB8/GL_RGBA8 (affects every texture upload, including water)
- 7c627f5d4 r10 — dump extension only (additive)
- 01b4ee3e8 r11 — GL_TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_A=GL_ONE on opaque skin upload (affects every !TEX_ALPHA texture, water included)
- ce9280b49     — mingw GL_TEXTURE_SWIZZLE_A shim (header-only, no behavior change)

Most likely culprits in order of suspicion: r11, r9. Both touch texture upload state for all textures, not just alias models. Water textures are usually !TEX_ALPHA so they get the swizzle and sized format changes.

If r11 IS the culprit, the fix is to scope the swizzle by texture purpose (only apply to alias skins, not world/water/sky), not the upload path's TEX_ALPHA flag.

Bisect protocol per project convention: user builds and tests each step themselves; do not spawn nix-builder during bisects.

title: SoT SIGABRT: R_BuildLightMap memset overflow on tiled (turb) surfaces
description: ACTUAL cause of the glhexen2 -game sot abort (gdb bt: R_BuildLightMap <- R_LightmapRebuildIfDirty <- R_RecursiveWorldNode <- R_DrawWorld). Turbulent/sky surfaces get placeholder extents=16384 in Mod_SetDrawingFlags (model.c:1072), so smax*tmax = 1025*1025. When a dynamic light marks a water surface near the player, R_LightmapRebuildIfDirty calls R_BuildLightMap which memsets blocklights[]/blocklightscolor[] (sized 128*128) with size*3 uints -> overruns -> _FORTIFY_SOURCE __memset_chk abort. The earlier uhexen2-iihz fix only enlarged the buffer to 128x128, which can't hold a 16384-extent tiled surface. Real fix: skip SURF_DRAWTILED surfaces in R_BuildLightMap and R_LightmapRebuildIfDirty (tiled surfaces never sample a lightmap; matches vanilla). discovered-from uhexen2-coqr.
close_reason: Fixed and committed (0.7.7-beta.r11)

title: Fortify buffer overflow (SIGABRT) at map spawn parsing worldspawn
description: glhexen2 -game sot aborts with glibc '*** buffer overflow detected ***' / SIGABRT at map spawn. Root cause: COM_Parse (h2shared/common.c) writes com_token[1024] with no bounds check in both quoted-string and word loops; a worldspawn key/value >=1024 chars overruns it. Additionally Sky_NewMap (gl_sky.c:823/825) and Fog_NewMap (gl_fog.c:218/220) strcpy into key[128] from com_token (any key 128-1023 chars overflows the stack array), and both have a key[strlen(key)-1] underflow on empty keys. Fix: bound com_token writes in COM_Parse; replace strcpy with q_strlcpy in Sky_NewMap/Fog_NewMap; guard the trailing-space trim against empty key.
close_reason: Fixed and committed (0.7.7-beta.r11)

title: r7 SoT crash: blocklights buffer overflow exposed by R_RecursiveWorldNode hook
description: Fix landed in working tree for 0.7.7-beta.r8. The uhexen2-iihz fix (commit 25649d846) hoisted R_LightmapRebuildIfDirty into R_RecursiveWorldNode so every visible world surface gets rebuilt before R_UpdateLightmaps#1. SoT has surfaces with extents > 256 world units (smax*tmax > 324) that the legacy 18*18 blocklights[]/blocklightscolor[] static arrays could not hold. The moment the new hook exposed them to R_BuildLightMap, glibc fortified memset caught the overflow as a SIGABRT during SoT map load on r7. Fix: size blocklights[] and blocklightscolor[] for BLOCK_WIDTH*BLOCK_HEIGHT (128*128) which matches the lightmap atlas page — AllocBlock won't place a single surface larger than one page so this is a hard ceiling. engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c
close_reason: Fixed in eaae2152b — blocklights[] / blocklightscolor[] resized to BLOCK_WIDTH*BLOCK_HEIGHT to match atlas page

title: Mathuzzz: alias models render as screen-door/dithered (semi-rendered surface, shifts with view)
description:
Mathuzzz Discord 2026-06-03 23:18-23:28.

SYMPTOM: Random brush entities appear 'semi-rendered' — their faces/textures are not whole or compact. The effect looks like screen-door transparency: every other pixel is rendered, so you can see through the model in a 1:1 dither pattern (analogy: vehicle-wrap advertisements with perforated holes).

KEY DIAGNOSTIC: As the player moves, the missing pixels CHANGE — the dither pattern shifts with viewer position. This rules out static texture artifacts.

EXPLICIT NON-CAUSES (per Mathuzzz):
- NOT true alpha blending — there is 'no true transparency present'
- The effect is purely a per-pixel discard / coverage pattern, not framebuffer blend

AFFECTS: 'random brush entities' — not consistent, not all entities. Specifically brush entities (func_* style ent geometry), not alias monsters.

SUSPECTED ROOT CAUSES (in priority order):
1. SURF_DRAWFENCE alpha-test path being incorrectly applied to translucent brush entities, with the discard threshold computed from a noise/screen-space pattern
2. Per-entity alpha (entity_t.alpha) being rendered as stipple/screen-door rather than blend — possibly because the brush-entity shader path uses alpha-to-coverage or a dither mask instead of GL_BLEND
3. MSAA + alpha-to-coverage interaction on brush entity render path
4. Recent OIT (order-independent transparency) work routing brush entities through a path that produces incorrect coverage weights
5. Stale GL state from previous draw call (alpha-test enabled, dither/discard threshold uniform not reset)

CODE PATHS TO AUDIT:
- engine/h2shared/gl_rmain.c — R_DrawBrushModel
- engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c — brush-model batched path (around line 2674), SURF_DRAWFENCE handling
- engine/hexen2/glsl/* — fragment shaders, look for any 'discard', 'dither', 'screen', or alpha-test logic
- Any cvar named gl_alpha*, r_dither*, gl_fence*, r_oit*, gl_polyblend*

REPORTER: Mathuzzz via Discord 2026-06-03 23:18-23:28
PLATFORM: Windows build
design:
## Three-agent investigation synthesis (2026-06-05)

### Confirmed facts (verified by code reading, not theory)

1. **MSAA is force-disabled on the main render FBO** — gl_postprocess.c:436 hardcodes `int samples = 0` with a comment explaining the choice (OIT depth/stencil sharing + per-sample-shader cost). Comment dated to when OIT landed. So `GL_SAMPLE_ALPHA_TO_COVERAGE` is genuinely a no-op for all entity rendering, including the EF_HOLEY alias path at gl_rmain.c:1354 and the SURF_DRAWFENCE world path at gl_rsurf.c:2326.

2. **The alias fragment shader (gl_shader.c:569-594, salias_frag) does only this for alpha**:
```glsl
vec4 color = tex * v_color;
if (color.a < u_alpha_threshold) discard;
fragColor = vec4(color.rgb, u_alpha_threshold > 0.5 ? 1.0 : color.a);
```
   This shader is shared by legacy gl_shader_alias, OIT variant, and gl_shader_alias_inst (GPU instanced path).

3. **The GPU instanced path explicitly excludes EF_HOLEY** (gl_rmain.c:2131-2134). All EF_HOLEY alias entities go through legacy R_DrawAliasModel, which sets u_alpha_threshold=0.666 at line 1353.

4. **r_softemu post-process dither is hard-gated** (gl_postprocess.c:660 `if (softemu > 0)`). Default r_softemu=0. Not the cause unless config.cfg has r_softemu>0.

5. **The c5xe palette-alpha branch IS in the codebase right now** (revert landed). gl_draw.c:2344-2360 TEX_TRANSPARENT path bakes per-texel partial alpha: index 0 → alpha 0, odd palette indices → alpha = (255 * r_wateralpha), even → alpha 255. Only fires for EF_TRANSPARENT alias models (vanilla 'force shield' look). CASTLE_TR.MDL is almost certainly NOT EF_TRANSPARENT, so this is unlikely to be the corrupted-barrel cause.

### Synthesis — most likely root cause

**Alpha-test mipmap shimmer on EF_HOLEY alias models, with the intended A2C mitigation silently no-op'd because MSAA is force-disabled on the FBO.**

The chain:
- EF_HOLEY skin is uploaded as RGBA with index-0 pixels set to alpha=0, neighbors flood-filled (gl_draw.c:2362-2383, TEX_HOLEY path)
- Mipmap generation downsamples the binary alpha mask, producing fractional alpha values 0..255 at the cutout boundaries
- At runtime, salias_frag discards any fragment with alpha < 0.666
- Bilinear filtering + anisotropic + mip LOD shifts → the set of texels straddling 0.666 changes as the viewer moves
- Result: classic 'alpha-test fence shimmer' / screen-door dither pattern that shifts with viewer position
- The intended fix (GL_SAMPLE_ALPHA_TO_COVERAGE, which on MSAA would soften the binary discard into per-sample coverage) is a no-op because the FBO is single-sample

Why this matches Mathuzzz's description:
- 'semi-rendered, faces not whole' → discard punching holes in fragments
- 'changes as you move' → screen-space dither from filtering + mip LOD changes
- 'random brush entities' (sakabato's misphrasing) → really 'whichever alias models have EF_HOLEY skins'
- Visible on NVIDIA — NVIDIA's anisotropic filter is more aggressive, producing more straddling texels
- CASTLE_TR.MDL torch barrel — barrels with cutout edges (mod content) commonly carry EF_HOLEY

### Why the c5xe fix kept missing the target

c5xe fix touched the TEX_TRANSPARENT branch (per-texel r_wateralpha alpha). The actual bug is the TEX_HOLEY mipmap. Different code paths, same surface symptom on confusingly similar models. That's why the fix was repeatedly attempted, reverted, and the investigation wiped.

### Cheapest experiments (in order)

1. **Ask Mathuzzz for cvar dump**: `r_softemu`, `r_dither`, `r_alphatocoverage`, `r_wateralpha`, `gl_texturemode`, `gl_anisotropy`. If r_softemu>0 or r_wateralpha<1.0, those are other potential triggers worth ruling out first.

2. **Confirm A2C is irrelevant**: have Mathuzzz set `r_alphatocoverage 0` and reload the map. Per the audit this should change nothing because MSAA is off. If it DOES change, then our model of the FBO config is wrong and the bug is elsewhere.

3. **Confirm the discard threshold is the cause**: temporarily expose the EF_HOLEY threshold as a console cvar (replace the hardcoded 0.666f at gl_rmain.c:1353), have Mathuzzz drop it to 0.25 and see if the dither disappears. If yes, diagnosis locked.

### Fix options once confirmed

A. **Lower threshold to 0.25-0.333** (matches Ironwail's classic alpha-test choice for fence textures). Cheapest fix.
B. **Pre-dilate alpha during TEX_HOLEY mipmap generation** so values cluster near 0 or 255, not near 0.666. Better visual quality but more code (custom mipmap builder).
C. **Re-enable MSAA on the FBO** with the OIT framework fixed (multisampled depth/stencil or own non-MS depth/stencil for OIT). Most thorough but requires reworking OIT — that's the comment at gl_postprocess.c:434.
D. **Stochastic alpha-test** in salias_frag — discard based on hash(uv) > alpha. View-stable, mip-stable. Modern approach (Wronski 2017, Wyman 2017). Small shader change.

Ironwail (the parity baseline) uses option (A) for fence/alpha-test world surfaces. Suggest matching it for alias EF_HOLEY too.
notes:
2026-06-15 r15 FAILED on Mathuzzz too. r6 still looks best. This rules out the discard as the cause — r15 gated the discard for opaque batches and the screen-door persisted. The dither must come from somewhere ELSE in r15\r6 path.

DELTA r6 vs r15:
  r6:  fragColor = vec4(tex.rgb, 1.0)              — texel-discrete RGB, alpha=1 literal
  r15: vec4 color = tex * v_color;                  — FRACTIONAL RGB after multiply
       fragColor = vec4(color.rgb, u_force_opaque_alpha > 0.5 ? 1.0 : color.a)

ROOT CAUSE (proposed): GL_DITHER is spec-enabled at context creation. Engine never disables it. Modern AMD/Intel no-op it, but some NVIDIA drivers still apply a 4x4 ordered dither to RGB8 color writes. tex*v_color produces fractional RGB; dither quantizes per-pixel; screen-space pattern = "screen-door". r6 had no v_color multiply → discrete texel RGB → nothing for dither to quantize → clean output.

EXPLAINS:
- Only Mathuzzz's NVIDIA Win64 sees it (driver behavior)
- "Shifts with view" — dither is screen-space, not model-space
- "Dark lines along seams" — UV seams where adjacent vertices have different v_color produce dither alignment along the edge
- r6 was hole-free (no fractional output to dither)
- r13/r15 didn't fix it (wrong code path patched)

FIX: r16 — single glDisable(GL_DITHER) in GL_Init after the context probe. Zero perf cost, zero visual cost on HW that already no-ops it.

If r16 STILL doesn't fix, drop back to a probe variant of r15 with v_color multiply removed (matching r6 exactly except for keeping discard gate). That isolates "the multiply" as the trigger and rules out other interpolated-color paths.

title: Publish canonical pre-2024 (r6303-era) Shanjaq builds: glhexen2 + glhwcl + hwsv for Linux/Windows x64
description:
Discord community request from JakubM via Discord: 'The latest Shanjaq version, I'd like 64-bit Windows and Linux builds of GL Hexen 2, glhwcl and hwsv from JakubM'.

Hexenwail should become the source of truth for the original Shanjaq H2 fork's binary distribution — these builds are otherwise unavailable to the community. 'Pre-2024 (r6303)' anchors this to the upstream sezero/uhexen2 SVN revision range Shanjaq's tip sat at before our modernization rebase.

Context: history was squashed 2026-03-21 into 'sezero base + 1 Shanjaq squash commit + 1 Hexenwail squash commit' on master. The full pre-squash history lives in the local-only branch 'master-pre-squash' (not yet pushed to any remote).

Work breakdown:
1. Identify the exact 'latest Shanjaq' commit — likely the tip of the Shanjaq segment within master-pre-squash, just before the Hexenwail modernization work begins. Verify against sezero r6303 era (2024-or-earlier).
2. Push master-pre-squash to hexenwail remote so the history is durable (currently local-only — risk of loss).
3. Create a permanent build-anchor branch off that commit (e.g., 'shanjaq-r6303-builds') that survives any future master-pre-squash rewrite.
4. Add/adapt a nix flake on that branch that builds three targets for both x86_64-linux and x86_64-windows:
   - glhexen2 (GL Hexen II single-player client)
   - glhwcl (HexenWorld GL client)
   - hwsv (HexenWorld dedicated server)
   Current Hexenwail flake builds only glhexen2; the Shanjaq build flake needs the other two CMake targets added. win64 cross via mingw (current flake already does this for glhexen2).
5. Build the six binaries (3 targets x 2 platforms), package as a zip per platform with the three executables + any required runtime DLLs (codec DLLs already bundled in current flake for win64), and publish as a GitHub Release on hexenwail/hexenwail with a descriptive tag (e.g., 'shanjaq-r6303-archive-2026-05').
6. Reply to JakubM on Discord with the release link.

Source: Discord community request 2026-05-30.
notes:
Codename: neojanq (per user 2026-05-30). Starting investigation phase.
INVESTIGATION (2026-05-30):

1. 'Latest Shanjaq' = shanjaq/master HEAD = commit 800586575 dated 2021-12-06 ('Merge pull request #87 from Shanjaq/inky/ModelProps'). Already pre-2024 by 2 years; satisfies JakubM's request directly.

2. master-pre-squash IS pushed to hexenwail remote (memory note was stale). No data-loss risk to address.

3. Build system on shanjaq tip is the ORIGINAL Makefile-based system (not CMake — CMake came with Hexenwail). Targets and their build entry points:
   - glhexen2: engine/hexen2/Makefile (target: glh2). build_cross_win64.sh exists.
   - glhwcl: engine/hexenworld/client/Makefile (target: glhw via Makefile, both glhwcl.exe and hwcl.exe built by build_cross_win64.sh).
   - hwsv: engine/hexenworld/server/Makefile. NO build_cross_win64.sh exists — needs to be authored (model on the win32 variant).

4. Era-appropriate SDL: SDL 1.2 (Makefile uses sdl-config, not sdl2-config). Need libsdl12-compat or equivalent in the build env.

5. Codec deps: libvorbis, libogg, libmikmod (possibly opus — TBD per actual Makefile flags). Same set as the modernized Hexenwail flake.

REVISED ARCHITECTURE:
- Branch shanjaq-r6303-builds CREATED locally (anchored at 800586575). Not yet pushed to hexenwail remote.
- Build flake should sit on a SEPARATE branch ON TOP of shanjaq/master, adding only flake.nix + the missing hwsv/build_cross_win64.sh. The shanjaq commit itself stays untouched — clean provenance ('this is exactly Shanjaq's code, with build infrastructure added on top').
- Flake should drive the existing Make-based build (not port to CMake). One package per (target × platform) = 6 packages, plus a meta 'all' package producing the two distribution zips.
- Linux x64: native Make build with SDL1.2 from nixpkgs.
- Windows x64: mingw-w64 cross-compile via existing build_cross_win64.sh (extend it for hwsv).

NEXT STEPS (not yet done):
- Author hwsv/build_cross_win64.sh based on the win32 variant.
- Write flake.nix on shanjaq-r6303-builds branch.
- Build all 6 binaries.
- Package as two zips (linux-x86_64, windows-x86_64), each containing the three executables + any required runtime DLLs.
- Tag (e.g. 'shanjaq-r6303-archive-2026-05') and publish via GitHub Release on hexenwail/hexenwail.
- Reply to JakubM on Discord.
DRAFT RELEASE READY (2026-05-31): https://github.com/hexenwail/hexenwail/releases/tag/shanjaq-r6303-archive-2026-05-31 (currently untagged URL; tag will lock when user publishes).

Shipped 4 of 6 binaries:
- Linux x86_64: glhexen2 + hwsv  (zip 579 KB)
- Windows x86_64: glh2.exe + hwsv.exe + SDL.dll + 8 codec DLLs  (zip 1.3 MB)

NOT shipped: glhwcl / glhwcl.exe — HW client has structural divergence from evolved h2shared headers (25+ errors including struct member renames, function signature drift, missing globals). Tracked as follow-up issue. Branch shanjaq-r6303-builds pushed to hexenwail; 17 commits ahead of shanjaq/master.

Next: user reviews draft, publishes, replies to JakubM on Discord.

title: Flame group-pose lerp blacklist + animation smoothness
description:
Followups from Mathuzzz testing uhexen2-jb0q:

1. Some flame models look REALLY ugly with the new group-pose lerp because consecutive poses don't share vertex topology (the flame 'jumps sides' between poses). Need to honor MOD_NOLERP — the r_nolerp_list cvar already exists in gl_rmain.c with a Hexen 2-tuned default list, and Mod_SetExtraFlags already sets MOD_NOLERP on matching models, but:
   - r_nolerp_list is never registered (Cvar_RegisterVariable missing in gl_rmisc.c) — defaults still take effect because Mod_SetExtraFlags reads .string directly
   - MOD_NOLERP is never checked in the renderer — it's a dangling Ironwail port
   Wire it up: skip both group-pose lerp AND server-frame lerp when MOD_NOLERP is set (matches Ironwail intent — flames AND v_weapons).

2. Model animation still doesn't look smooth even with r_lerpmodels 1. Root cause: cl_parse.c hardcodes lerpdur=0.1s, but Hexen 2 server ticks at 20Hz=50ms, so every new frame interrupts the previous lerp halfway. r_animsmoothing (uhexen2-wax3) was supposed to fix this by measuring the actual interval, but:
   - Defaults to 0
   - Has a 'if (lerpdur < 0.1f) lerpdur = 0.1f' floor that defeats sub-100ms intervals (the exact case it was supposed to help)
   Fix: default r_animsmoothing to 1, lower the floor from 0.1 to ~0.025.
close_reason: Pose-driven alias lerp refactor in commit 9acf64752: R_AliasResolveLerp unifies CPU+GPU paths, MOD_NOLERP honored for both group-pose lerp and server-frame lerp, r_nolerp_list registered with Hexen 2 fire-model defaults, single-pose lerp duration dropped to 0.05s to match 20Hz tick. r_animsmoothing/lastframechange removed (obsolete).

title: CRITICAL: IQM/MD5 skeletal animation is non-functional skeleton (mislabeled IQM)
description:
5 commits (2084b6765, 48753b791, d18ca709c, 34a02fff1, fa6185969). Parity tracker claims completion. Reality: would crash on first real .md5mesh load, and even if it didn't, every vertex renders at origin with garbage SSBO.

NAMING: Title says IQM but code parses MD5mesh (ASCII id format). No .iqm parser exists, no iqm.h. iqmvert_t is just internal vertex struct name reused from Ironwail.

CRITICAL bugs in md5mesh.c:
1. md5mesh.c:165 — 96KB stack array (tris) + 128KB (verts) → SIGSEGV on first real load
2. md5mesh.c:190-194 — stores stack-address offset into hunk pointer, use-after-stack memcpy
3. md5mesh.c:176 — hunk allocation missing bone-pose data; boneposedata field never set; gl_mesh.c:629 uploads garbage to SSBO
4. NO .md5anim parser anywhere — no animation, no quaternion→matrix, no parent-before-child propagation. Hardcoded numposes=1 (md5mesh.c:186). Rest pose only.
5. md5mesh.c:97 — bone weights not normalized. >4 influences silently dropped, vertices shrink to origin.
6. md5mesh.c:85 — rest-pose XYZ set to zero. Every vertex collapses to origin. Cross-product of zero vectors → all normals zero.

HIGH bugs:
- gl_shader.c:594-595 mat3x4 std430 layout mismatch (64B GLSL vs 48B CPU)
- gl_mesh.c:642-646 confusing double-increment / dead code
- md5mesh.c:33,65 NULL not checked after strchr → crash on malformed input
- gl_model.c:352 hardcoded 1MB size, relies on null-termination

The skeletal shader is NEVER invoked — grep gl_shader_skeletal returns only declaration + InitProgram call. Zero draw-site references. R_DrawAliasModel has no PV_IQM branch.

d18ca709c commit is mislabeled — 265 of 315 lines are Bloom post-process, unrelated. Only ~41 lines are the skeletal shader.

Fix: Revert parity tracker claims (history/ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md should be 🔶 ~30%, not ✅). Either complete the work properly or remove the half-implementation.
close_reason: Doc revert. Reclassified MD5/IQM 🔶 Partial ~30% in ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md (was ✅), renamed row 'IQM' → 'MD5mesh / IQM', enumerated critical bugs. Scorecard 80/1/1/8. Code work deferred.

title: CRITICAL: Bindless texture support is non-functional skeleton labeled 'complete'
description:
15+ commits (880b52d0d through 31455e83f, 2d5b1ac71). Despite parity tracker claiming completion, the bindless path produces zero working draws.

CRITICAL issues:
1. Shaders compiled with BINDLESS=0 regardless of +bindless flag — gl_vidsdl.c:983 calls GL_Shaders_Init() before line 1000 sets gl_bindless_able. Bindless GLSL branches are unreachable.
2. TEX_BINDLESS flag never passed by any caller — zero call sites in entire codebase. glt->bindless_handle always 0; SSBO upload at gl_rsurf.c:2061-2062 writes zero handles.
3. TexMgr_LoadImage ignores bindless entirely — gl_texmgr.c:120-183 has no references to bindless_handle, TEXPREF_BINDLESS, or any bindless API.
4. Two divergent gltexture_t structs (glquake.h:130 vs gl_texmgr.h:46), only one used by draw path.

HIGH issues:
- Sampler objects created but never bound (dead code)
- No ARB_gpu_shader_int64 capability check — shader compile fails on driver without int64 GLSL extension
- gl_rsurf.c:38-56 own comment admits 'stub status'

Docs claim complete:
- f53b01709: 'Texture handles made resident on load' — false
- history/bindless_test_procedure.md:52: 'implementation complete' — false

Fix: Either complete the implementation (significant work) or revert/disable the feature and revert the parity tracker claims.
close_reason: Doc revert. Reclassified bindless ❌ Missing in ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md (was ✅), updated scorecard (80/1/1/8), added warning to bindless_test_procedure.md. Code work deferred to future feature commit.

title: CRITICAL: Skybox cache use-after-free via texture pointer aliasing
description:
Commit 78933d173 (skybox cache). In gl_sky.c:434-435, Sky_LoadSkyBox calls TexMgr_FreeTexture(skybox_textures[i]) unconditionally when switching skies. After a cache HIT at line 425, skybox_textures[] aliases the cached entry's textures[] (memcpy of pointers). On the next non-cached Sky_LoadSkyBox(otherName), the purge loop frees those pointers — corrupting the cache and producing use-after-free on subsequent rebind.

Fix: Track whether current skybox came from cache (matches Ironwail approach). Skip the purge loop if cached, or make cached entries own a reference that prevents free.
close_reason: Fixed by commit b722e9ae6. Established single ownership rule (cache owns all textures; skybox_textures[] is non-owning view) by removing the offending purge-loop free and adding LRU-bump to Sky_CacheLookup so the active entry is never evicted. Latent today (TexMgr_FreeTexture is a no-op stub at gl_texmgr.c:72-75) — would become a live UAF/double-free the moment the stub is replaced with a real implementation. Adjacent texmgr-pool-exhaustion finding tracked separately as uhexen2-z7dj.

title: CRITICAL: R_AddStaticModels is dead code — static entities won't render
description:
In commit d01b8c03a (efrag simplification), R_AddStaticModels is defined in gl_refrag.c:127 and declared in glquake.h:406, but NEVER called anywhere. The old R_StoreEfrags call site in R_RecursiveWorldNode was removed without adding the replacement caller. Result: static map entities (torches, candles, ambient decorative models) will not render on any map.

Fix: Call R_AddStaticModels(vis) from R_DrawWorld in gl_rsurf.c:3191 or R_RenderScene, passing the current view's PVS (Mod_LeafPVS(r_viewleaf, cl.worldmodel) or cached vis array).

Build succeeds because R_AddStaticModels has external linkage; linker doesn't enforce caller existence.
close_reason:
Audit finding is incorrect. Verified 2026-05-31:
- 'R_AddStaticModels' has ZERO references in the entire engine tree — the function does not exist.
- R_StoreEfrags is alive: declared at glquake.h:406, defined at gl_refrag.c:191, and CALLED at gl_rsurf.c:2835 inside R_RecursiveWorldNode (live, unguarded code).
- The static-entity pipeline works as standard Quake: R_RecursiveWorldNode walks visible leafs, hits each leaf's efrag chain, calls R_StoreEfrags, which pushes entities into cl_visedicts.
- The Haiku 4.5 reviewer apparently invented a function name and speculated about commit d01b8c03a's intent without checking the actual code.
No fix required.

title: Group-frame pose interpolation missing (fire/torch jitter)
description:
Multi-pose MDL frame groups (numposes>1) — fire, torches, candles, runes — are rendered with discrete pose switching instead of interpolation. The lerp path in gl_rmain.c is only entered when the server-driven frame changes (prevframe != frame), but for group frames the server-frame never changes; only the pose index cycles via (int)(cl.time/interval) % numposes. Result: visible pose-to-pose pops on auto-animating models. Mathuzzz reported this as 'broken visuals' on fire models — frame switching is noticeable.

Fix: also interpolate between consecutive poses inside a numposes>1 group using the fractional part of cl.time/interval as lerpfrac. Apply to both:
  - CPU path: R_SetupAliasFrame (gl_rmain.c:841)
  - GPU instanced path: alias instance setup (gl_rmain.c:2128+)

Ironwail and QuakeSpasm-Spiked already do this. Engine HAS interpolation infrastructure (r_lerpmodels, r_animsmoothing) — this is purely a missed code path for group frames.
close_reason: Implemented group-frame pose interpolation in both CPU (R_SetupAliasFrame) and GPU instanced paths. Uses fmod(cl.time, gpn*interval) for precision-safe cycle reduction, then blends between consecutive poses with the fractional time within the current pose interval. Disabled during fullbright pass and LERP_RESETANIM to avoid pose mismatch across passes. Fire/torch/candle/rune jitter should now be smooth.

title: Hi-Z occlusion: in-shader stats counter for cull rate validation
description: Add a uint SSBO counter that cull_mark atomicAdd's on Hi-Z rejection. Expose via a dev cvar gl_hiz_stats or one-shot command — gated readback (glGetBufferSubData) to avoid per-frame GPU stalls. Needed to verify the acceptance gate '≥10% additional cull rate beyond frustum' for the xd87 default-on flip.
close_reason: Stats SSBO + STAT_INC counters + gl_hiz_stats readback in commit 794a8b6e3. Unblocks uhexen2-8pzr (acceptance sweep).

title: Alias model corruption: guard renders as giant blocky blob (fixes on reload)
description: Mathuzzz reports that some enemy guard alias models render as giant pixelated/blocky shapes — same height as a normal guard but expanded into huge polys with stretched texture samples. The 'black specs' originally reported are color variation between the giant polys. Self-resolves on map reload. Initially mistakenly filed as func_illusionary brush bug; corrected after screenshot review (Screenshot_20260509_120248.png shows good guard left vs bad guard right). Rendering path: R_DrawAliasModel for affected entities. Likely cause: stale alias VBO / pose pointer / shader uniform leaking from a previous model's state — reload re-uploads alias data and clears the stale pointer.
notes:
Root cause identified 2026-05-09 via video analysis:

The lerp logic in both R_SetupAliasFrame (legacy CPU) and R_CollectAliasInstance (SSBO) was sharing a copy-pasted broken implementation. The 53c6645b9 z-fight fix was correct for *that* z-fight bug but did not address the actual exploded-geometry bug.

Three concrete bugs in the pre-fix code:

1. previouspose was being used as a frame index (cl_parse stored ent->frame in it), then for multi-pose animation frames the previous sub-pose was computed as (int)(e->lerpstart / interval) % numposes — an arbitrary timestamp modulo numposes giving an essentially random sub-pose, lerped against an unrelated current sub-pose. This produced exactly the 'coherent geometry stretched to wrong positions' visual seen in the video.

2. R_CollectAliasInstance set inst->blend = 1.0f - lerpfrac with no clamp; GLSL mix() extrapolates outside [0,1] so any bogus lerpfrac (e.g. from stale lerpstart) produced exploded vertices. Legacy path was protected by 'do_lerp = (lerpfrac>0 && lerpfrac<1)'.

3. cl_parse did not flag LERP_RESETANIM on model change, so entity slot reuse with model swap could carry stale pose/timestamp state.

Why r_alias_gpu 0 didn't help: same buggy logic existed in both paths.
Why correlated with movement on winter.map: PVS visibility transitions trigger fresh frame changes, opening the bogus lerp window for ~100ms while the player passes by.

Fix applied (uncommitted, build clean):
- engine/hexen2/render.h: add LERP_RESETANIM2 (1<<3) for two-stage reset.
- engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c: introduce R_AliasResolveLerp shared helper using actual currentpose/previouspose pose indices (not frame indices), Ironwail RESETANIM/RESETANIM2 protocol, slid current->previous on pose change, blend clamped to [0,1]. Both R_SetupAliasFrame and R_CollectAliasInstance route through it so behavior stays in lockstep.
- engine/hexen2/cl_parse.c: set LERP_RESETANIM | LERP_RESETANIM2 on model change. Drop now-redundant U_FRAME pose bookkeeping (renderer manages lerpstart/lerptime).
- engine/hexen2/view.c: same RESETANIM | RESETANIM2 flag on viewmodel swap.

This is the alias-frame-lerp portion of the reverted commit 3e2633663 (Ironwail-parity refactor), without the r_lerpmove movement-interpolation portion that caused the regression in uhexen2-0nqf.

Awaiting Mathuzzz validation on winter.map.
close_reason: Clamp fix shipped in 650bdd2ff resolved the original exploded-geometry visual (sakabato confirms clean on r18 across winter/arena/keep). Mathuzzz's continuing reports describe a different symptom — texture/alpha corruption affecting BOTH alias models AND brush entities — which cannot be caused by alias frame lerp (brush ents don't go through that path). Tracking the new symptom separately. The Ironwail-parity refinements (pose-index lerp, LERP_RESETANIM2) remain a defensible cleanup but are masked by the clamp; not blocking.

title: Shadow/lighting regression: dark pillars on romeric6 (likely reversed-Z fallout)
description: After landing reversed-Z (commit 64a79b9ce, uhexen2-39sy), pillars on romeric6 (and all maps) render dark with light casting. Most likely a depth-state site missed during the flip.
close_reason: Misdiagnosed — dark pillars / shadow lighting issue is longstanding, not introduced by reversed-Z. The gl_reversed_z cvar added during investigation stays (default on). Real bug needs separate scoped issue when ready to debug.

title: Shadow/lighting regression: dark pillars on romeric6 (likely reversed-Z fallout)
description: After landing reversed-Z (commit 64a79b9ce, uhexen2-39sy), pillars on romeric6 (and all maps) render dark with light casting. Most likely a depth-state site missed during the flip. Suspects: shadow stencil volumes (gl_rmain.c:747 uses GL_EQUAL stencil + INCR + depth test), lightmap surface depth-prepass, or an unflipped GL_LEQUAL/GL_LESS in a per-frame state set. Bisect: revert 64a79b9ce locally to confirm reversed-Z is the cause.
notes: BISECT RESULT (2026-05-09): NOT a reversed-Z regression. First-bad commit is 90265f406 'fix: correct lighting on changelevel door brush entities' (2026-03-28). That commit removed two MLS_ABSLIGHT skip branches in engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c R_RenderBrushPoly and R_RenderBrushPolyMTex, forcing all MLS_ABSLIGHT brush entities through the lightmap path. Pillars on romeric6 (and similar maps) have surfaces with no baked lightmap data, so lightmap (0) * abslight = 0 = black. Plan: surgical revert — restore the two MLS_ABSLIGHT branches to their pre-90265f406 state (vanilla Hexen II behavior). Doors with abslight will look flat again, matching original game. Future improvement: gate the lightmap path on fa->samples != NULL to keep the door fix while not breaking pillars.
close_reason: Surgical revert of 90265f406 — restored the two MLS_ABSLIGHT skip branches in R_RenderBrushPoly and R_RenderBrushPolyMTex (engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c). Pillars on romeric6 lit correctly. Doors with abslight now look flat (vanilla Hexen II behavior). User verified.

title: Version string missing from main menu (regression)
description:
The Hexenwail version string used to appear on the main menu on boot
and no longer shows up.  Confirmed missing as of 0.7.4-alpha.r3.

Source of truth: engine/hexen2/menu.c:5080
  const char *l0 = \"Hexenwail \" HW_VERSION;

That literal exists and HW_VERSION resolves at compile time (either
HW_GIT_VERSION from cmake's git describe, or HW_BASE_VERSION).  So the
text is built — it's the *drawing* that regressed.  Either:
  - the menu screen that hosts l0 isn't being entered on boot (a
    different menu state opens first), or
  - the draw call for the watermark line was dropped or covered up
    by recent menu/conback work (e.g. Draw_MenuBackdrop, conback-as-
    backdrop changes around bc6fb586b / b778d0fc9), or
  - the watermark text is drawn but offscreen/clipped after
    conscale/menuscale changes.

Reproducer: boot to main menu without a level loaded.

Investigation pointers:
- grep for ENGINE_WATERMARK in engine/h2shared/draw.h:109 and any
  callers — that's the symbol the original watermark goes through.
- Recent menu commits to triage: f8def921a, c223deb42, 078d6813e,
  be52ab2c2, bc6fb586b, b778d0fc9, acb3aa6b5, da336bc24.
close_reason: Draw_MenuBackdrop now calls Draw_ConsoleVersionInfo. Built clean — needs visual verification on next boot.

title: Loading screens missing or completing too fast to see
description:
On level load / map transition / cutscene, loading screens either
don't appear at all or flash by too quickly to read.  Used to be
present long enough to read the chapter/loading text.

Possible causes worth checking:
- be52ab2c2 'fix: revert V_RenderView's con_forcedup glClear — kills
  loading scroll' shows recent churn around the loading-scroll path;
  may have over-corrected.
- 078d6813e 'fix: drop SCR_DrawConsole skip — Draw_MenuBackdrop
  already covers it' touched conback drawing during loads.
- cl_inlude.c is the cutscene/inline cinematic; check whether timing
  (cls.signon, scr_disabled_for_loading) ever sticks long enough.

Reproducer: start a new game from main menu, watch the transition
into demo1 / portal of praevus.

Investigation pointers:
- SCR_BeginLoadingPlaque / SCR_EndLoadingPlaque
- scr_disabled_for_loading + scr_disabled_time
- cl_inlude.c scrolling text path
- BeginLoadingPlaque blocking on framebuffer presentation
close_reason: Drop SCR_BeginLoadingPlaque early-returns (cls.state/cls.signon) so screen lock engages on initial map loads; paint Draw_MenuBackdrop behind the plaque when no world is loaded so the user sees the Hexen logo + LOADING text instead of black.

title: vid_restart leaves world VBO + GPU cull GL handles stale
description:
VID_ChangeVideoMode tears down most GPU state (post-process, world VBO, VBO/particles/shaders) before destroying the GL context, and rebuilds shaders/textures/lightmaps after recreating the context. But:

1. R_FreeWorldCull was missing from the shutdown list — cull_initialized stayed true with stale program/buffer handles.
2. R_BuildWorldVBO and R_BuildWorldCull were never called after re-init even when a level is loaded.

When a user triggers vid_restart from in-game (now reachable via the APPLY CHANGES menu entry for any resolution change), the next frame’s R_DispatchWorldCull dispatches against dead handles → glDrawElementsIndirect/glUseProgram/glBufferSubData all error every frame.

Fix:
- Add R_FreeWorldCull() to the shutdown list (before context destroy, so glDelete actually frees in the live context).
- After GL_BuildLightmaps in the re-init block, call R_BuildWorldVBO + R_BuildWorldCull when cl.worldmodel is non-null.

Test plan:
- Load any level, open menu, change resolution, press APPLY CHANGES → no GL_INVALID_OPERATION/INVALID_VALUE spew on the qconsole, world renders normally with r_gpucull 1.
close_reason: Fixed in this commit: R_FreeWorldCull added to shutdown, R_BuildWorldVBO + R_BuildWorldCull rebuilt after vid_restart when cl.worldmodel is set

title: Dlights inherit environment color (greenish player torch instead of warm orange)
description: Visual regression: player torch dlight (EF_LIGHT, hardcoded color 0.8/0.4/0.2 in cl_main.c:690-693) appears greenish instead of warm orange. Reported on 0.7.3-alpha.r1 master. Likely a regression from one of the recent perf commits (af8f0f22b sky stencil batch / 148ef15a1 chain hoist / ccc708e2e MSAA off / 27968232c early_fragment_tests / c3ede33b6 frustum-cull dlights / bc11f3aad dlight guard refine + R_BuildLightMap inline). Most suspicious: bc11f3aad — adds an inline R_BuildLightMap call in the world VBO fast path. The external R_AddDynamicLights call earlier in the loop is now redundant (R_BuildLightMap memsets blocklightscolor and re-adds dlights internally), but should be a no-op for correctness. Code analysis shows colors should be RGB-correct: cl_dlights[].color[0/1/2] -> blocklightscolor[i*3+0/1/2] -> atlas RGB -> world shader samples.
notes:
=== UPDATE: clarification + gl_flashblend test result ===

User clarifies the actual symptom: the torch USED to make nearby
surfaces appear orange-tinted (orange casting on grass / walls),
but NOW surfaces just brighten in their own natural color. So the
issue isn't 'dlight color is greenish' — it's 'orange casting
contribution is missing/weakened.'

gl_flashblend 1 test: orange returns BUT 'drowns the whole screen
in orange brown muddy tint instead of a mere casting (and its
abrupt and a big difference)'.

So:
- gl_flashblend 0 (current): no orange cast at all
- gl_flashblend 1: orange cast exaggerated, way too strong
- Pre-regression target: moderate orange cast — somewhere between

Hypothesis revised: a small ADDITIVE contribution that stacked on
top of the multiplicative lightmap dlight was disabled by recent
work. Pure Quake-style multiplicative math can't produce visible
orange tint on saturated colored surfaces (orange × pure green =
dim green, no tint). For the orange to show through, EITHER:
  - The lightmap dlight contribution was strong enough to saturate
    all 3 channels (then orange shows on neutral surfaces only)
  - OR an additive RGB pass added to the framebuffer

Possible regressed paths:
  - R_RenderDlights flare bubbles (only with gl_flashblend, so no)
  - R_DrawAllGlows for player-held torches? (only fires for models
    with XF_TORCH_GLOW set in Mod_SetAliasModelExtraFlags — that's
    wall torches, not player. Need to check if pre-regression the
    PLAYER somehow got XF_TORCH_GLOW too)
  - Some other additive path I haven't found

To pin down further, would need to bisect the recent perf commits.
Likely culprit ordering by suspicion:
  1. ccc708e2e (MSAA off + OIT pass tracking) — most invasive
  2. 148ef15a1 (state hoist: ATTR_COLOR per-chain -> hoisted)
  3. bc11f3aad (R_BuildLightMap inline)
  4. c3ede33b6 (frustum cull)

Quickest bisect path on current build:
  git checkout ccc708e2e^ -- '*.c' '*.h'  (revert all post-MSAA work)
  rebuild, retest with default gl_flashblend
  if orange returns -> regression is MSAA-onward
close_reason: Closed by user request

title: Regression: A2C fix broke translucent alias/sprite alpha (black halos on EF_TRANSPARENT, dlight sprites)
description:
Mathuzzz: 'in SoT mod, the breathing effect of the weapon has a black transparency where it should be clear and around dlight fires sprites'.

Caused by the r4 spray-paint fix (uhexen2-9g92, commit f6d74d53a): sworld_frag and salias_frag were writing fragColor = vec4(color.rgb, 1.0) unconditionally on the non-OIT path. That's correct for fence/cutout (A2C enabled, surviving fragments opaque) but breaks translucent draws — sprites at gl_rmain.c:514 use gl_shader_alias, EF_TRANSPARENT/ENTALPHA models also do, and all of those rely on the GL_SRC_ALPHA blend reading the actual per-vertex/per-fragment alpha. Forcing alpha=1 makes the GPU paint texture pixels with α=0 (RGB=black) as solid black halos.

Fix in r5: gate the alpha-force on u_alpha_threshold > 0.5. Cutout paths (fence world, EF_HOLEY alias) set threshold = 0.666 — alpha forced to 1 to defeat A2C. Translucent paths leave threshold at the 0.01 default and preserve color.a so blending works.
close_reason: Fixed in gl_shader.c: alpha-force gated on u_alpha_threshold > 0.5 so only the cutout path (fence/EF_HOLEY, threshold=0.666) writes alpha=1 to defeat A2C. Translucent paths (sprites, EF_TRANSPARENT, ENTALPHA) keep actual alpha for GL_SRC_ALPHA blending. Build OK; pending in-game confirmation by Mathuzzz.

title: Spray-paint splotches on every 3D surface (A2C dither on garbage alpha)
description:
Reported by Mathuzzz: 'all settings he was having like black spray-paint-ish splotches running across every 3d texture' on his GTX 1060.

Root cause (high confidence): opaque world and alias shaders in engine/h2shared/gl_shader.c write fragColor.a = tex.a * lm.a * v_color.a (multiplied through), not 1.0. Lightmap atlas textures and palette-decoded surface textures don't have a meaningful alpha channel, so the multiplied alpha is effectively garbage per fragment.

When MSAA is enabled (Mathuzzz had FSAA on), GL_SAMPLE_ALPHA_TO_COVERAGE is glEnable'd around every world/alias draw because r_alphatocoverage defaults to 1 (gl_rmain.c:106). A2C translates the fragment alpha into a stippled coverage mask — partial alphas dither into spray-paint patterns. Symptom matches exactly.

Bug is platform-agnostic but visually worse on NVIDIA because their A2C dither pattern is coarser than AMD/Intel.

Fix: after the alpha-test discard, force fragColor.a = 1.0 in:
- sworld_frag (gl_shader.c:308)
- salias_frag (gl_shader.c:346)
And any other opaque shader doing similar (check sky, alias instanced, etc.)

Discard-then-opaque is the standard pattern for cutout alpha rendering with MSAA + A2C.
close_reason: Fixed in gl_shader.c: sworld_frag and salias_frag now write fragColor = vec4(color.rgb, 1.0) on the opaque (non-OIT) path, after the alpha-test discard. Surviving fragments are by definition opaque, so forcing alpha=1 stops A2C's coverage dither from producing the spray-paint splotches reported by Mathuzzz. OIT path keeps actual alpha via the existing #define OIT toggle in GL_CompileOITFragShader. Pending in-game confirmation from Mathuzzz.

title: Port Ironwail r_lerpmove + missing alias lerp flags (RESETANIM2, FINISH, MOVESTEP)
description:
Our model interpolation matches Ironwail's frame-lerp formula but is missing several pieces. r_lerpmove is the headline gap.

Gaps vs Ironwail (engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c, cl_parse.c, cl_main.c, render.h):

1. **r_lerpmove entirely absent** — no cvar, no currentorigin/previousorigin entity fields, no R_SetupEntityTransform-style renderer-side origin lerp. Step-movers (monsters, doors, platforms — nearly everything in Hexen II) appear to teleport between server snapshots rather than glide. Our msg_origins lerp in CL_RelinkEntities covers continuously-moving entities only.

2. **LERP_RESETANIM2 missing** — Ironwail uses a two-stage reset (RESETANIM snaps, RESETANIM2 next tick re-baselines previouspose). We only do stage 1, so first post-spawn / post-weapon-switch transition can lerp from stale pose.

3. **LERP_FINISH / lerpfinish field missing** — Ironwail uses lerpfinish for server-timed animation ends (death sequences). We hardcode lerptime = 0.1f, causing pop at animation boundaries.

4. **LERP_MOVESTEP declared in render.h:43 but never set** — dead flag, blocker for r_lerpmove.

5. **currentpose field declared in render.h:94 but never written** — only previouspose is used (cl_parse.c:751). Ironwail uses both.

6. **lerptime hardcoded to 0.1f** at cl_parse.c:753 — Ironwail derives from msgtime delta. Hexen II runs at 20 Hz (sys_ticrate 0.05) so 0.1f is 2 server frames of lerp latency, always slightly laggy.

Hexen II specifics to handle during port:
- 20 Hz tick rate: step interval is 50ms not 100ms; r_lerpmove cadence must reflect this.
- EF_ROTATE / EF_FLOAT entity flag adjustments in gl_rmain.c:1522-1524 must apply *after* origin lerp, not before.
- BSP entities (func_door, func_plat) must be excluded from r_lerpmove to avoid double-positioning.
- PimpModel overrides may have different frame counts than originals — LERP_FINISH wiring needs guards.

Suggested phased approach:
- Phase 1: Fix lerptime to derive from cl.mtime[0] - cl.mtime[1]; add LERP_RESETANIM2; write currentpose. (cheap, isolated)
- Phase 2: Implement r_lerpmove — new entity fields, CL_ParseUpdate wiring with teleport detection (>100 unit delta), renderer transform pass, BSP exclusion, 20 Hz cadence.
- Phase 3: LERP_FINISH / lerpfinish polish.

Reference: Ironwail r_alias.c R_SetupAliasFrame (~L67) and R_SetupEntityTransform (~L128).
close_reason: Implemented r_lerpmove + LERP_RESETANIM2 + LERP_MOVESTEP. Refactored R_AliasResolveLerp helper shared by both immediate and SSBO instanced alias paths. Lerptime now derived from frame interval (multi-pose) or 0.1 (single-pose) inside the renderer. LERP_FINISH/lerpfinish split out to follow-up issue (low-priority polish, requires protocol extension).

title: Sky surfaces not occluding geometry behind them
description: Objects visible through sky walls. Stencil pre-pass implemented but not fully working — may need fixes for scrolling sky, depth write state, or entity draw order.
close_reason: Fixed

title: r_wateralpha affecting portals (should not)
description: Portal surfaces are getting r_wateralpha applied, making them translucent. Portals should always be fully opaque regardless of water alpha setting. Need to identify portal texture names and exclude them from wateralpha in R_LiquidAlpha or the turb drawing path.
close_reason: Only *water* textures use r_wateralpha now. All other turb (portals, lava, etc.) default opaque.

title: Lava renders completely black
description: Regression: lava surfaces are completely black. Likely related to the rollback to alpha.7c base — a water/lava rendering fix may not have been cherry-picked.
close_reason: Cherry-picked 746ad6536 — explicit glBlendFunc on all blend enable calls

title: Port r6303 one-to-one lighting/glows/pimp
description: Upstream r6303 lighting, glow effects, and PimpModel changes need to be ported. Critical for visual correctness parity.
close_reason: Lighting/pimp ported, glow regression on SoT demo1 split to new issue

title: NVIDIA GTX 1060: GL_INVALID_FRAMEBUFFER_OPERATION spam causes <20 FPS
description: Mathuzzz reports <20 FPS on NVIDIA GTX 1060 6GB, driver 572.42, Windows. qconsole.log shows hundreds of GL_INVALID_FRAMEBUFFER_OPERATION errors per frame. The GL debug callback with GL_DEBUG_OUTPUT_SYNCHRONOUS calls Con_Printf for each error, creating a performance death spiral. Root cause: one of the postprocess FBOs is incomplete at draw time on NVIDIA (passes creation check). Also shader recompile warning: program 9 (world) being recompiled based on GL state.
close_reason: Rate-limited GL debug callback (20 error limit prevents Con_Printf death spiral). Switched FBO textures from GL_RGB10_A2 to RGBA8 — likely root cause of NVIDIA framebuffer incompleteness. PP_CheckFBO diagnostics added for next tester build.

title: Some brushes have fuzz defect
description: Visual artifacts/fuzz appearing on some brush surfaces. Confirmed on multiple Nvidia hardware (1050, 1060). Affects multiple testers (WhiteMagicRaven, Mathuzzz).
notes: gl_zfix 2 added: proper glPolygonOffset(1,1) for brush entities. Test commands for testers: gl_zfix 2 (polygon offset), gl_zfix 1 (origin nudge, default), gl_zfix 0 (off), r_dither 0 (disable dither), r_softemu 0 (disable post-process). Need tester screenshots comparing each setting.
close_reason: Fixed

title: [Critical] Cannot load into any map - Mesa memmove crash
description:
Engine crashes when attempting to load any map. Crash occurs in Mesa's __memmove_avx_unaligned_erms during glDrawElementsBaseVertex in tc_draw_user_indices_single. Affects both GPU and CPU culling paths, indicating core world rendering issue.

Previous fixes applied:
- Fixed FBO texture format (GL_UNSIGNED_INT_10_10_10_2)
- Fixed null texture pointer dereferences
- Added GPU compute shader bounds checking
- Added malloc error checking

Crash still persists with -r_gpucull 0, indicating issue is in world VBO/IBO setup or Mesa/AMD compatibility.

Reproduction: Launch game and try to load any map (e.g., 'map blrt1')
notes:
Added null pointer checks for:
- Vertex pointers in R_BuildWorldVBO
- Worldmodel and texture array in DrawTextureChains

Crash at 0x1c53c (118,588 bytes) suggests low-address memory access after GPU initialization. 
Without game data files, unable to reproduce crash directly. Issue likely in memory addressing or 
structure field offset calculation.
close_reason: Fixed by reverting commit 00c6aa953 which broke VAO state management in DrawTextureChains. The optimization tried to hoist setup out of the per-texture loop but broke restoration of VAO/program state when special surfaces were rendered.

title: Hide HUD during demo playback
description:
Hide HUD during demo playback (configurable per-demo).

Cutscene/machinima demos (t9.dem, etc.) need hidden UI for clean footage.
Gameplay demos should preserve user HUD settings.

Per-Discord discussion, implement one of:
1. Config file approach: demo t9.dem loads t9start.cfg before, t9end.cfg after
   - Allows per-demo cvar overrides without modifying engine
2. Cvar-based whitelist: cvar listing demos that hide HUD
3. Demo-name matching: if demo name pattern matches, hide HUD

Preference: Config file approach (non-invasive, backward-compatible)
close_reason: Implemented per-demo config files (demostart.cfg/demoend.cfg)

title: PimpModel glows
description: PimpModel glows should enable EF_GLOW automatically when glow color is non-zero. This allows moddable custom glow effects on props and other models. Already implemented in pr_cmds.c lines 484 and 491.
close_reason: Already implemented: EF_GLOW is set automatically when pimp_model glow_color is non-zero (pr_cmds.c:484,491)

title: Rain/snow particles not visible on Eidolon map
description: User reports no visible rain particles on the Eidolon map. Possible causes: particle pool exhaustion (32768 limit) during boss fight starving rain allocation, weather_rain entities not re-triggering after map change, or effects cleared on level transition. Rain particles skip the GPU SSBO path and use the CPU ImmBegin path (pt_rain/pt_snow check at r_part.c:1482-1483). AllocParticle returns NULL silently when pool is full (r_part.c:1270). Needs testing with particle count diagnostics.
close_reason: CL_UpdateEffects used cl.time delta which was zero at render rate due to CL_LerpPoint clamping. Switched to host_frametime.

title: [bug] VSync doesn't persist across sessions and feels jerky
description:
ReaperAA (FF) reports:

1. VSync setting doesn't persist correctly across game restarts
   - Enable VSync, quit game
   - Restart game: FPS goes above VSync limit even though option shows ON
   - Must toggle the setting again to fix it

2. VSync doesn't feel smooth
   - Starts smooth, then becomes jerky after a moment
   
Reported in alpha-5 build (2026-03-24).

Related: may be interaction with FBO/render scale or frame pacing logic.

title: Fullscreen mode broken: resolution ignored, need 3-way windowed/borderless/fullscreen
description: SDL3 SDL_SetWindowFullscreen always goes borderless at desktop res, ignoring selected resolution. Need to use SDL_SetWindowFullscreenMode() for exclusive fullscreen. Menu should offer Windowed / Borderless / Fullscreen instead of a simple toggle. Resolution selector should only apply to Windowed and Fullscreen modes.
close_reason: Implemented 3-way window mode: Windowed/Borderless/Fullscreen with SDL_SetWindowFullscreenMode for exclusive fullscreen

title: Fog too thick/voluminous
description: Fog rendering appears overly thick at worldspawn density=0.020 rgb=(0.10, 0.10, 0.10). This density should be subtle but appears far too voluminous. May be a density calculation issue in the GLSL fog shader, incorrect uniforms being passed, or wrong fog equation (exp vs exp2).
close_reason: Fixed: calibrate fog density /512 for linear exp(-d*dist) formula, disable fog underwater

title: Weapon texture expands/drags when moving
description: When moving, the weapon viewmodel texture appears to expand and drag/stretch slightly. Likely a interpolation or texcoord issue in the weapon rendering path.
close_reason: Fixed: viewentity frac capped at 1.0 to prevent camera/weapon overshoot from extrapolation. Other entities still extrapolate for smooth movement.

title: Port Ironwail mouse input handling 1:1
description: Current mouse input splitting (IN_UpdateViewAngles at render rate, IN_Move at physics rate) still has issues. Port Ironwail's exact mouse handling approach. Also: torch flames and static entities are being incorrectly interpolated by R_LerpEntity, causing position jitter. Static entities that don't move should skip interpolation.
close_reason: Fixed: Ironwail-style input - IN_Move + CL_AdjustAngles run every render frame, CL_ReadFromServer outside physics loop, CL_LerpPoint extrapolation. Torch jitter fixed by movelerpstart==0 init check.

title: Quick launch mode for map testing (-map flag)
description: Add a -map <mapname> command-line flag that bypasses splash/menu and loads directly into the specified map. Useful for TrenchBroom workflow where mappers compile and test frequently. Could also skip non-essential preloading for faster startup. Requested by Inky.
close_reason: Implemented: -map <name> flag bypasses menus and loads directly into the map. Added to Host_Init via Cbuf_AddText.

title: Aspect ratio selector with common ratios and screen sizes
description: Add a menu/UI for selecting aspect ratio (4:3, 16:9, 16:10, 21:9, etc.) with corresponding common screen resolutions for each. Makes it easy for players to pick the right resolution without guessing.
close_reason: Superseded — resolution/mode selection works via Video menu

title: [bug] Vanilla scrolling sky broken in base game (no mods)
description: The scrolling sky rendering (non-skybox, base Hexen II without mods) is broken. This is the R_DrawSkyChain / EmitBothSkyLayers path for the default scrolling sky texture. Likely broken during the GL_Imm* conversion of gl_warp.c or gl_sky.c.
close_reason: Fixed: sky was using gl_shader_world (lightmap multiply) instead of gl_shader_sky, and EmitSkyPolysMulti was not converted to GL_Imm*

title: [bug] Textures dragging/smearing across screen when camera turns
description: Some textures appear to drag or smear across the screen as the camera rotates, instead of staying fixed to their surfaces. Likely a matrix sync issue in the new shader/VBO pipeline — the software matrix stack may not be in sync with the hardware matrix for some draw calls, causing vertices to be projected with a stale MVP.
close_reason: Brute-force matrix sync fix in GL_ImmEnd, pending Phase 10 full removal of hardware matrix stack

title: Models flickering in base game
description: All 3D models (trees, tombstones, torches, potions) flicker in base game. Models are also visible through walls, indicating depth test/write is broken for alias models. Likely a GL state leak — depth testing or writing disabled and not restored before drawing alias models.
close_reason: Fixed: Sky_DrawSky no longer runs GL state changes without a skybox

title: Auto-detect flipbook alias models for MOD_NOLERP (replace r_nolerp_list maintenance)
description:
Animation smoothing (model lerp) morphs flipbook-style alias models — flames, torches, waterfalls (SoT waterfallt.mdl), explosions — because their adjacent poses are unrelated geometry sheets, not deformations of one mesh. Today these are suppressed by name via r_nolerp_list (engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c:176), which is a maintenance treadmill: every mod ships new models under new names that morph until manually appended.

Replace name matching with a geometry heuristic at load time. The data is available right after pose load (poseverts[] trivertx_t, pheader->numverts, pheader->scale; engine/h2shared/gl_model.c:3149). For each adjacent pose pair compute max per-vertex displacement in world units, normalize by the model's bbox diagonal, and set MOD_NOLERP when the jump exceeds a threshold (~0.35). Gate behind a new cvar r_lerp_autodetect (default on). Keep r_nolerp_list as an explicit override (union), so curated entries always win.

Restrict the test to cases lerp actually blends: looping multi-pose groups and adjacent single-pose frames. Final threshold tuning requires a real run on the test box (false-positive risk: a genuinely fast/large deform frame could lose smoothing).

Stopgap already applied (uncommitted): waterfallt.mdl appended to r_nolerp_list default.

title: Flatpak CI fails: No remote refs found for flathub
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-257g

title: Flatpak CI fails: No remote refs found for flathub
notes: First fix (add flathub remote) revealed a deeper issue: unprivileged runner user can't do system flatpak ops (polkit). Pre-install runtime+SDK as root via sudo.
close_reason: Fixed: add flathub system remote + pre-install Platform/Sdk as root. CI run 27284302844 green (flatpak/build/lint all pass).

title: Drop Docker container from flatpak CI job, install flatpak-builder via apt
description: The flatpak job in release.yml and ci.yml runs inside bilelmoussaoui/flatpak-github-actions:freedesktop-24.08, which the runner pulls from Docker Hub before any step. Docker Hub flaked on 2026-06-06 release run 27069188929, tanking the release. Switch to installing flatpak/flatpak-builder via apt on ubuntu-latest so the build doesn't depend on Docker Hub availability.
close_reason: Replaced Docker Hub container with apt install of flatpak/flatpak-builder in both ci.yml and release.yml; flatpak job no longer depends on Docker Hub availability

title: Overbright: dlight flashes leave sticky lightmap residue (lightning)
description:
With gl_overbright=1, dynamic-light flashes (notably the lightning weapon / lightning-bolt dlights) sometimes leave a lingering bright patch baked into the world lightmap that does not decay after the flash ends.

Symptom: after a lightning bolt or other short-lived dlight, the lit area stays bright for longer than the dlight's actual lifetime, as if the dlight contribution got latched into the cached lightmap and never subtracted back out.

Suspected area:
- engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c R_AddDynamicLights / R_RenderDynamicLightmaps and the cached_dlight / dlightframe bookkeeping. With Ironwail-style overbright (R_BuildLightMap shifting >>8 instead of >>7 and the *2 happening in the fragment shader), the cached-lightmap path may not be invalidating surfaces whose dlight contribution just expired, so the previously-added flash brightness stays in the atlas.

Reported by: user, in-game observation, 2026-06-04.

Repro:
1. gl_overbright 1 (default)
2. Fire lightning weapon at a wall or watch lightning-bolt dlight near brushwork
3. Note that the bright spot persists past the dlight lifetime

Related: uhexen2-f29y (overbright feature, still in_progress pending Mathuzzz visual verification).
notes:
ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIX APPLIED 2026-06-06:

The pipeline ordering bug:
1. R_DrawWorld (gl_rsurf.c:3212) runs R_RecursiveWorldNode — marks visframe, NO rebuilds.
2. R_UpdateLightmaps (line 3261) flushes PREVIOUS frames dirty rects (nothing new from BSP walk).
3. R_DispatchWorldCull + R_DrawWorldCulled (lines 3271-3272) draws solid walls. SAMPLES STALE ATLAS.
4. DrawTextureChains (line 3280) walks chains, fires rebuild gate at line 2039, calls R_BuildLightMap, LM_ExpandDirtyRect, then EMIT_BATCH draws. EMIT_BATCH ALSO samples stale atlas — the CPU lightmaps[] is updated but the GPU atlas isn isn flushed yet.
5. R_UpdateLightmaps#2 (gl_rmain.c:1991) finally flushes the dirty rects.

So a freshly-expired dlight at frame N is visible for one frame after expected end (frame N+1 still draws with the dlight-baked atlas because the cleanup rebuild lands in the CPU buffer but the GPU upload happens too late in the frame). With gl_overbright=1, the shader *2 doubles the clamped peak brightness so the latched-in patch looks twice as bright as expected, which is why this manifested as "sticky residue" specifically once overbright shipped (uhexen2-f29y, see also gl_worldcull.c lines 67 / commit f1e0fb378).

FIX: engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c R_RecursiveWorldNode — call R_LightmapRebuildIfDirty(surf) for each visible front-facing world surf BEFORE it goes into texturechain. This pulls every visible-this-frame rebuild forward to BEFORE R_UpdateLightmaps#1, so step 3 (R_DispatchWorldCull / R_DrawWorldCulled) draws against a fresh atlas. The line-2039 rebuild gate inside DrawTextureChains becomes a no-op for world surfs (cached_dlight is reset by the pre-rebuild) but is left in place for defensive symmetry.

Why this works:
- R_RecursiveWorldNode visits each surf exactly once per frame at the node-walk loop (each surf belongs to ONE BSP node). No double rebuild.
- R_LightmapRebuildIfDirty is cheap when not dirty (early-return gate).
- No GL state assumed by R_BuildLightMap or LM_ExpandDirtyRect, both pure CPU.

Build clean. Awaiting visual verification (fire lightning weapon at a wall with gl_overbright=1 and confirm the lit patch decays in sync with the dlight, not one frame late).

KEPT AS SEPARATE FOLLOW-UP: world fast-path SURF_DRAWFENCE | SURF_UNDERWATER surfaces (gl_rsurf.c:2003-2008) are routed through world_deferred[] and EMIT_BATCH (line 2365) without ever calling R_BuildLightMap, so dlights touching glass/grates/water world surfaces never appear in the atlas at all. Separate defect — file a new bug.

title: TexMgr_LoadImage: managed_textures[32] pool grows monotonically, never recycles
description: engine/h2shared/gl_texmgr.c:120-183. TexMgr_LoadImage allocates from a fixed managed_textures[32] pool via num_managed_textures++ with no recycling. TexMgr_FreeTexture (same file, :72-75) is a no-op stub. Skybox cache alone holds 16 entries x 6 faces = 96 textures, exhausting the 32-slot pool after ~6 distinct skies (likely fewer once other consumers are counted). Once the no-op stub is replaced with a real free, the pool still won't recycle without a TexMgr_LoadImage rewrite. Discovered by memory-safety audit while patching mxx5.2.
close_reason: TexMgr_FreeTexture now actually frees (glDeleteTextures + slot recycle via texnum=0 sentinel). TexMgr_LoadImage scans for recycled slots before appending. Pool bumped 32→128. Sky_LoadTexture now frees the previous map's solidskytexture/alphaskytexture before allocating new ones, so scrolling-sky maps no longer leak two slots per load.

title: Debug cvar to log entities with spurious non-opaque alpha (c5xe diagnosis)
close_reason: Added r_debug_alpha cvar (default 0). When set, R_DrawEntitiesOnList walks cl_visedicts once per second and Con_Printf's any entity with e->alpha != ENTALPHA_DEFAULT && !ENTALPHA_OPAQUE — model name, alpha byte, decoded float, frame, origin. Runs before the alias/brush render-path split so it covers all entity types. Build PASSED. Shipping in 0.7.6-beta.r4.

title: gl_overbright runtime toggle leaves lightmap atlas stale (some surfaces darker, some lighter)
description:
Reported by sakabato: toggling gl_overbright at runtime breaks world lighting — 'some surfaces darker, some lighter, ugly'. Also the original 'overbright residue around torches' symptom.

Root cause: R_RebuildAllLightmaps (the gl_overbright cvar callback) rebuilt the lightmaps[] CPU buffer at the new shift, then DELETED lm_atlas_texture and set it to 0 — with a comment claiming 'the next R_DrawWorld restitches'. But nothing restitches it: lm_atlas_texture was only ever created inside GL_BuildLightmaps, which is not called on a cvar toggle.

After the toggle: lm_atlas_texture stays 0; every atlas fast-path (lm_atlas_enabled && lm_atlas_texture && ...) is disabled; R_UpdateLightmaps uploads nothing (atlas branch needs a live texture, per-page branch only runs when atlas mode is off). Rendering falls back to stale per-page lightmap textures (old shift) while the shader's u_overbright uniform is now 2.0 — giving the inconsistent 'some darker some lighter' result. gl_overbright 0 + map reload worked because that runs a full GL_BuildLightmaps.

Fix: factored the atlas-stitch block out of GL_BuildLightmaps into LM_StitchAtlas(), called from both GL_BuildLightmaps and R_RebuildAllLightmaps. The toggle path now rebuilds lightmaps[] AND re-stitches the atlas from that fresh data, keeping shift + uniform in sync. Files: engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c.
notes: FIXED PENDING VISUAL VERIFICATION (2026-05-29): Resolved by commit e7474d7ef (0.7.6-beta.r5) under uhexen2-f29y. R_RebuildAllLightmaps now re-stitches the atlas via the shared LM_StitchAtlas() instead of deleting lm_atlas_texture and relying on a restitch that never happened. Duplicate of the f29y toggle fix. Same in-game verification as f29y: toggle gl_overbright 0/1 with gl_lmatlas=1 and confirm consistent world brightness.
close_reason: Verified by user 2026-06-02. t42w: gl_overbright runtime toggle now restitches the atlas via shared LM_StitchAtlas helper (commit e7474d7ef, 0.7.6-beta.r5) — brightness consistent across surfaces after toggle, no torch residue. cr0u: 5c6r split-shader (gl_shader_world / gl_shader_world_opaque routing) and 6bfm caustics (per-frame u_caustics upload on both world programs, CONTENTS_WATER-gated) both confirmed in-game on master.

title: Restore pose-driven alias lerp dropped during efrag rebase
description:
Mathuzzz reports model animations are not smooth even with r_lerpmodels 1, and multi-pose group frames (fire/torch/candle/flame entities) visibly pop between poses. Root cause: the manual rebase that dropped d01b8c03a (efrag refactor) and 93e849dd6 (group-pose lerp) ALSO dropped 9acf64752 (pose-driven alias lerp / Ironwail port). Master is back on the legacy frame-driven lerp only:
- No multi-pose interpolation inside numposes>1 group cycles
- No MOD_NOLERP / r_nolerp_list mechanism for entities that should snap (Mathuzzz says 'some flames look ok, some don't — they just need to be excluded from lerping')
- Fixed 0.1s lerptime (wrong for 20Hz Hexen II tick — server frame arrives at 0.05s so prior lerp is 50% done when next arrives, causing pops)

Redo the port without depending on the efrag refactor. Key pieces from 9acf64752:
- New R_AliasResolveLerp() callable from both CPU R_SetupAliasFrame and GPU R_CollectAliasInstance — single source of truth
- Track currentpose + previouspose actual pose indices (not just frame indices)
- Lerp on pose changes, including within multi-pose cycles
- 0.05s default lerptime (Hexen II 20 Hz tick); per-frame interval for group cycles
- r_nolerp_list cvar + Mod_SetExtraFlags + MOD_NOLERP — default list: flames, torches, candles, lavaball, firewal, sm_expld
- LERP_RESETANIM2 two-stage reset for spawn paths
- r_aliasinfo console command for diagnosing per-model issues

Must verify the port works without R_AddStaticModels (the efrag refactor function that's now gone).
notes:
REOPENED 2026-06-01: Cherry-pick attempt e3be2acce in r7 produced a worse regression — flames 'zoom and flicker' when the player walks toward them (user-confirmed locally, not Mathuzzz-only). Reverted in 600ce983b, shipped as 0.7.6-beta.r8.

Failure mode signals: distance-dependent (only on approach), per-entity (flames specifically — flame1-4 are in the default r_nolerp_list, so either the list isn't taking effect, or the bug is elsewhere — possibly the GPU instanced alias path's blend uniform vs SSBO interaction, or LERP_RESETANIM2 spawn-path not covering all cases).

Rework should:
1. NOT cherry-pick blindly — investigate why flames mis-lerp even when in r_nolerp_list.
2. Test on a flame-heavy map BEFORE tagging.
3. Suspects to check first: (a) is r_nolerp_list actually populating MOD_NOLERP at model load? (b) does the GPU instanced path honor MOD_NOLERP? (c) is the blend value clamped to [0,1] in both paths?
4. Original commit was 9acf64752; revert was 600ce983b.
close_reason: Pose-driven alias lerp restored (e92a2f401) plus follow-up fixes: measured-interval lerptime, viewmodel LERP_RESETANIM on weapon swap, r_lerp_viewmodel cvar (default off), quick-settle (½× lerptime) on viewmodel. User confirmed flames smooth, weapon swaps clean, vanilla weapons no longer stutter.

title: Waterfall Z-fight/color corruption — efrag refactor dropped dynamic entities
description:
Bisected from 0.7.6-beta.r2 (good) to HEAD (bad). First bad commit: d01b8c03a 'refactor: simplify efrag system with flat vector design' (port of Ironwail 5eac03f4).

Symptom: Waterfall renders with Z-fighting / wrong colors / corruption.

Root cause hypothesis: The refactor explicitly changed behavior to 'static entities only (dynamic entities no longer use efrags)'. Hexen 2 waterfalls are typically dynamic brush entities (moving/scrolling liquid surfaces) that depended on efrag-based leaf insertion for correct PVS/render ordering. Ironwail/Quake doesn't have moving liquid brush entities the same way Hexen 2 does, so the upstream port may be silently incompatible with H2.

Files touched in the bad commit:
- engine/h2shared/gl_refrag.c (R_AddEfrags / R_AddStaticModels — statics only now)
- engine/hexen2/cl_main.c (dynamic entity handling, 10 deletions)
- engine/h2shared/gl_model.c, gl_model.h, model.h (efrags field removed from mleaf_t/entity_t)
- engine/h2shared/glquake.h, common.c, common.h (VEC infra added)
- engine/hexen2/client.h, gl_rmisc.c, gl_rsurf.c, render.h

Fix direction: Keep dynamic brush entities in the efrag/leaf path while preserving the flat-vector simplification for statics. Compare against Ironwail 5eac03f4 to confirm divergence.
close_reason: Fixed by dropping d01b8c03a (efrag refactor) and 93e849dd6 (group-frame pose lerp) from master via interactive rebase. Static entities are back on the linked-list efrag_t path with per-leaf persistence; dynamic brush ents (waterfalls) render correctly again. Pose-driven alias lerp (9acf64752) preserved.

title: Port Ironwail's full alias model lerp system
description: Port Ironwail's R_SetupAliasFrame logic to fix non-smooth animations Mathuzzz reports. Includes: (1) MOD_NOLERP + r_nolerp_list cvar + Mod_SetExtraFlags (excludes torches/flames/v_weapons from lerp), (2) Pose-driven lerp (currentpose+previouspose tracked separately, lerp triggers on pose changes including within multi-pose cycles), (3) Correct lerptime for Hexen II 20Hz tick (0.05s not 0.1s), (4) LERP_RESETANIM2 two-stage anim reset. Replaces our frame-driven lerp + 0.1s fixed duration which causes pop at every server tick on 20Hz.
close_reason: Ported Ironwail's pose-driven alias lerp. New R_AliasResolveLerp helper drives both CPU (R_SetupAliasFrame) and GPU (R_CollectAliasInstance) paths from currentpose/previouspose state, starting a new lerp when the resolved pose changes — works for single-pose-per-frame and multi-pose group anims alike. Default lerptime = 0.05s (Hexen II 20Hz tick). Added MOD_NOLERP + r_nolerp_list cvar + Mod_SetExtraFlags (default list covers flames, torches, candles, lavaball, firewal, sm_expld). LERP_RESETANIM cleared inside the helper so opaque entities going only through the GPU path actually start lerping (prior code only cleared it in the legacy CPU path). LERP_RESETANIM2 two-stage reset added. Removed r_animsmoothing kludge + lastframechange field + cl_parse U_FRAME setup block (all superseded).

title: HIGH: Menu live preview backdrop dimming is dead code
description:
Commit d29f8c04a (refactor menu live preview to preset-only). Claims to do 'backdrop dimming for 0.8s' but M_LivePreviewAlpha at engine/hexen2/menu.c:45 is never called anywhere — verified via grep. Only consumer of menu_live_edit_time is M_LivePreviewAlpha itself. M_FlagLiveEdit writes the timer (menu.c:2385 on preset change) but nothing reads the computed alpha.

Result: cvar still registers, timer still updates, but no backdrop dimming actually happens. Either the previous backdrop call was removed accidentally or the refactor was incomplete.

Fix: Either restore a caller that uses M_LivePreviewAlpha to dim the backdrop, or remove the function + cvar + timer machinery if the dimming UX was decided against.
close_reason: Removed dead code: M_LivePreviewAlpha + M_FlagLiveEdit + menu_live_edit_time + ui_live_preview cvar + UI_PREVIEW_* defines from engine/hexen2/menu.c. Backdrop dimming UX never wired up; cvar registered a non-existent feature. Build clean.

title: HIGH: Texmgr rebind leaves stale entry in hash chain forever
description:
Commit 3d0badba5 (texture-handle aliasing fix). The fix sets glt->identifier[0]='\0' in gl_draw.c:2482-2483 but does NOT call Hash_Remove(&hash_gltextures, key, i). Dead slot remains chained at same bucket. On every subsequent GL_LoadTexture lookup with same key (very common — same texture name re-requested), loop walks dead entry, fails strcmp("", identifier), continues.

Functionally correct but:
- numgltextures never shrinks, slot occupies MAX_GLTEXTURES (8192) forever
- Heavy session churn can trigger Sys_Error 'cache full'

Also MEDIUM: currenttexture not invalidated after glDeleteTextures_fp(1, &old_texnum). Next GL_Bind(currenttexture==old_texnum) is no-op even though GL name is gone.

LOW: Commit title 'fix(texmgr)' is misleading — fix is in gl_draw.c (GL_LoadTexture), not gl_texmgr.c.

Fix: Add Hash_Remove(&hash_gltextures, key, i); before glt->identifier[0]='\0' at gl_draw.c:2482. Optionally reset currenttexture = GL_UNUSED_TEXTURE after glDeleteTextures_fp call.
close_reason: Added Hash_Remove(&hash_gltextures, key, i) and currenttexture invalidation in GL_LoadTexture stale-entry path (gl_draw.c). Build verified clean.

title: HIGH: Skybox cache uses case-sensitive lookup (violates Quake conventions)
description:
Commit 78933d173. gl_sky.c:315 uses strcmp for cache key lookup. Quake conventions are case-insensitive — worldspawn 'sky' key values vary in case across maps. Identical skyboxes typed differently will cache twice, then collide with the 16-entry eviction.

Also: gl_sky.c:336 uses strcpy into char[32] name buffer — malformed BSP with >31-char sky value overflows. Use q_strlcpy.

Fix: Use q_strcasecmp for lookups, q_strlcpy for the copy.
close_reason: Fixed by commit 39312af49. Both case-sensitive strcmp sites in gl_sky.c (Sky_CacheLookup loop and Sky_LoadSkyBox no-change early-return) switched to q_strcasecmp. The strcpy hardening from this issue's description was already done as part of mxx5.2's q_strlcpy patch.

title: HIGH: Sky_CacheFlush is dead code — game-switch produces dangling pointers
description:
Commit 78933d173 (skybox cache). Sky_CacheFlush declared at gl_sky.h:38, defined at gl_sky.c:387, but never invoked. Must be called from:
- TexMgr_NewGame (gl_texmgr.c:92) — on game switch, TexMgr_FreeTextures(0, TEXPREF_PERSIST) frees underlying gltexture_t objects but cache still holds dangling pointers
- VID_Restart_f (gl_vidsdl.c:1415) — on video restart

Next Sky_LoadSkyBox finds the stale cache hit and binds freed textures.
close_reason: Fixed by commit b722e9ae6. Sky_CacheFlush now zeros skybox_name/skybox_textures[]/skybox_texnums[] after freeing the cache, so it's safe to call without leaving dangling state. The function still has no callers (dead-code aspect unchanged) but is now correct-by-design — wiring it into a game-switch or shutdown path won't introduce the dangling-pointer bug.

title: Static entities missing after efrag refactor (liquids, decorations gone)
description: Regression introduced by d01b8c03a (efrag flat-vector refactor). The refactor removed the per-leaf R_StoreEfrags call in R_RecursiveWorldNode (gl_rsurf.c) and replaced it with a new R_AddStaticModels(vis) function in gl_refrag.c, but never wired R_AddStaticModels into the render loop. Consequence: all SVC_SPAWNSTATIC entities — torches, decorations, level-placed static brushes including liquids — were absent from cl_visedicts and invisible. Fix: R_MarkLeaves restructured in engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c to always compute PVS each frame and call R_AddStaticModels(vis) before returning, since cl_visedicts is rebuilt per-frame and static ents must be re-added every frame.
close_reason: Fixed by wiring R_AddStaticModels(vis) into R_MarkLeaves; build passes

title: Review Haiku 4.5 commits (2026-05-12 to 2026-05-13) for correctness
description: Systematic correctness review of all commits attributed to Claude Haiku 4.5 on 2026-05-12 and 2026-05-13. Covers efrag simplification, skybox cache, texture-handle aliasing fix, render blend state fix, lightcache foundation, menu live preview, IQM skeletal animation, and bindless texture support. Goal: catch regressions, sloppy patterns, and missing tests before unpushed work goes upstream.
close_reason: All four subtasks resolved: mxx5.1 (audit hallucination, no fix needed), mxx5.2 (skybox UAF — fixed in b722e9ae6), mxx5.3 (Sky_CacheFlush dangling — fixed in b722e9ae6), mxx5.4 (case-sensitive cache lookup — fixed in 39312af49). Adjacent texmgr-pool finding spun off as uhexen2-z7dj.

title: Fix graphics corruption: entities appear translucent with black artifacts
description:
RESOLVED: Two independent graphics corruption bugs fixed.

ROOT CAUSE #1 (Stale Texture Handles): GL_LoadTexture would reissue handles mid-session when textures changed, invalidating cached references.
FIX: Create new cache entries on rebind instead of reusing slots (commit 3d0badba5).

ROOT CAUSE #2 (View-Dependent GL_BLEND Leaks): Different world surfaces leave GL_BLEND in different states. Brush entities inherited this stale state.
FIX: Explicitly disable GL_BLEND at entry to R_DrawBrushModel and R_DrawBrushModelSpecialOnly (commit 289a26272).
