Both fixes ensure rendering state is independent of viewing angle. Entities now render correctly from all angles with consistent appearance.
close_reason: Fixed stale texture-handle aliasing in GL_LoadTexture. Now creates new cache entries on rebind instead of reusing slots, preventing handle invalidation for code that cached old references. Users can test with gl_log_texgen 1 to verify no mid-map rebinds occur.

title: Port Ironwail: Optimize cull/mark step (3x speedup, 0.12ms→0.04ms)
description: Ironwail commit 4913d469 (Aug 2024) optimizes the cull/mark step from 0.12ms to 0.04ms on a 4070 Ti. Modifies brush model culling logic in gl_model.c, r_brush.c, r_world.c. High-value rendering pipeline optimization applicable to Hexenwail.
notes: Investigated: Hexenwail already dispatches per-marksurface (key opt). Remaining gains: bitpack leaf+sky (medium effort), surface data reorg (high effort), enable backface cull (medium risk). Deprioritizing for better ROI optimizations.
close_reason: Investigation found Hexenwail already implements the key optimization (per-marksurface compute dispatch). Remaining micro-opts (bitpack, data reorg) have lower ROI.

title: Shader: accept flat uvec4 for texture handles
description: World shaders (sworld_vert, sworld_frag, sworld_frag_opaque): when #if BINDLESS, add flat uvec4 in_texhandles (xy=diffuse, zw=fullbright) varying from vertex to fragment. Vertex shader passes from SSBO read. Fragment shader casts with sampler2D(in_texhandles.xy) for diffuse, sampler2D(in_texhandles.zw) for fullbright. #else: use uniform samplers as before.
close_reason: Implemented bindless shader support: vertex shader reads SSBO handles and packs as flat uvec4 varyings; fragment shaders conditionally use sampler2D(uvec2) constructor vs uniform samplers based on BINDLESS macro

title: Test bindless vs fallback rendering
description: Load a complex map (mill.bsp, coliseum). Enable r_speeds 2. Compare GPU world bucket time with bindless (gl_bindless_able=1) vs fallback (-nobindless flag). Target: measurable reduction in texture bind overhead on dense scenes. Verify visual correctness (no missing textures, no corruption). Test on Mathuzzz's Windows build if available.
notes: Build successful (nix build .#default). Testing procedure in history/bindless_test_procedure.md. Requires interactive graphics environment (display + OpenGL 4.3 capable hardware). Test 1: fallback (-nobindless), Test 2: bindless (+bindless), compare r_speeds output and visual correctness.

title: Create SSBO buffer for bindless draw calls
description: Allocate GPU buffer (glGenBuffers) to hold gl_draw_call_t union data. Size: MAX_SURFACE_DRAW_CALLS * sizeof(gl_draw_call_t). Bind at SSBO slot for shader access. Implement UploadDrawCallSSBO() to copy CPU data to GPU. Gate behind gl_bindless_able. Allocate once at init, reuse each frame.

title: Populate SSBO with texture handles before drawing
description: In DrawTextureChains hot path: before each glDrawElements run, populate SSBO slot with (tx_handle, fb_handle) pair from gltexture_t.bindless_handle. On fallback (! gl_bindless_able), populate (baseinstance, 0). Call glBindBufferBase to bind SSBO at shader slot. Upload via glBufferSubData or persistent mapping.
close_reason: Implemented SSBO population: batch_texture stored during chain setup, SSBO entries populated with (tx_handle, fb_handle) pairs for each draw run, SSBO bound at binding point 0 before drawing

title: Acceptance test: 5c6r split-shader + 6bfm caustics
description:
Two recent visual changes landed without runtime verification on the master branch (commits f4752ec03 and 7b82afce6 — 8 commits ahead of hexenwail/master).

Test plan:

--- uhexen2-5c6r (split sworld_frag into opaque + cutout) ---
1. Load mill.bsp (SoT). Locate the func_illusionary fence-textured bush that previously occluded the tree behind it (the original uhexen2-238u regression site).
   - PASS: bush is invisible, tree visible behind bush silhouette.
   - FAIL: tree z-rejected through bush — means the split misrouted fence draws to the opaque (no-discard) variant.
2. Hi-Z recovery measurement. With r_speeds 2 + gl_hiz_cull 1 on heavy outdoor (mill.bsp or coliseum), capture world GPU bucket. Compare to the prior +0.34ms regression measured in uhexen2-23a9. Target: most of 0.34ms recovered on the opaque pass.
3. Visual diff on a known fence/cutout map: confirm fence cutouts still alpha-test correctly (no fringes, no missing pixels, no z-fight on coplanar joins between opaque and fence surfaces).
4. Brush ent fence (e.g. portcullis): same alpha-test correctness check on a moving func_door fence.

--- uhexen2-6bfm (procedural underwater caustics) ---
1. Dive into water on any map with a swimmable pool. Confirm caustics overlay visible — slow flowing light/dark bands on walls and floor, ~few-meter tile scale, animated.
2. Surface above water. Caustics overlay must turn off cleanly (no leftover pattern on dry geometry).
3. cvar r_caustics 0 → caustics off even while submerged.
4. cvar r_caustics_intensity 0.0 / 0.35 / 1.0 — confirm strength scales monotonically, no overflow or NaN at 1.0.
5. Verify on a slime/lava-only map (no water): caustics never fire (gated on CONTENTS_WATER only, not slime/lava).
6. Watch for performance regression on a heavy underwater scene — caustics adds 2 sin + 1 pow per fragment to the world bucket, should be negligible but worth a quick r_speeds 2 GPU world check.

--- Smoke-test crossover ---
- mill.bsp underwater volume (if any) — verify both 5c6r opaque shader AND caustics work together (caustics uniform must be picked up by both gl_shader_world and gl_shader_world_opaque).
close_reason: Verified by user 2026-06-02. t42w: gl_overbright runtime toggle now restitches the atlas via shared LM_StitchAtlas helper (commit e7474d7ef, 0.7.6-beta.r5) — brightness consistent across surfaces after toggle, no torch residue. cr0u: 5c6r split-shader (gl_shader_world / gl_shader_world_opaque routing) and 6bfm caustics (per-frame u_caustics upload on both world programs, CONTENTS_WATER-gated) both confirmed in-game on master.

title: Menu text flashes completely invisible when changing settings
description: The live preview feature (ui_live_preview=1) fades menu text to alpha=0 when adjusting sliders to show the game behind. However, this makes text COMPLETELY invisible for 1.25 seconds, creating a jarring 'flash'. The text alpha should remain at least partially visible (e.g., 0.2-0.3 minimum) so the menu remains readable while previewing the effect.
close_reason: Removed entire live preview feature; this eliminates the text invisibility issue it caused.

title: Graphics menu cursor flashing when navigating with arrow keys
description: When pressing left/right arrows to adjust graphics menu sliders, the menu flashes invisible. The root cause is that the cursor position uses the item index to calculate Y position (92 + index*8) but doesn't account for skipped items when filtering is active. When items are skipped, the cursor is drawn at the wrong Y coordinate, causing visual artifacts.
close_reason: Fixed by calculating visual row position instead of using item index for cursor drawing

title: Menu disappears when cycling presets with left/right arrows
description: When navigating to Preset in Display menu and pressing left/right to cycle through presets (e.g., Faithful to Ultra), the entire menu system becomes invisible/disappears.
close_reason: Disabled live preview fade for presets since they apply multiple settings at once

title: Menu disappears when scrolling presets options
description: When scrolling through the presets menu options (Display/Sound/Game submenus), the entire menu system disappears. Likely regression from live substring filter or menu reorganization.
close_reason: Added guard counter to M_Display_Key up/down scroll loops, matching pattern used in other menu submenus

title: Gold/opaque turb water becomes translucent when r_wateralpha < 1
description:
Vanilla Hexen II marks SURF_TRANSLUCENT only for *lowlight and *rtex078 (engine/h2shared/gl_model.c:1346-1348). Every other '*' turb texture — notably *rtex346 (Castle gold pool in demo1.bsp, also seen in other Castle/Romeric maps) — is intentionally rendered opaque regardless of r_wateralpha.

Bug: Hexenwail's R_LiquidAlpha (engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c:1051) classifies by leaf content_class (CONTENTS_WATER) and returns r_wateralpha for everything in a water leaf, ignoring the SURF_TRANSLUCENT flag. SURF_TRANSLUCENT is set but never read (the comment 'r_edge.asm checks this' refers to the removed software renderer).

Result: with r_wateralpha < 1.0 (eg 0.5 from menu), the gold pool in demo1.bsp turns translucent — violating vanilla intent and breaking the visual contrast with adjacent blue water (*lowlight).

Fix: propagate SURF_TRANSLUCENT to a texture-level flag during Mod_LoadFaces; R_LiquidAlpha returns 1.0 for any '*' texture without the flag. Also expand the marking to include mod-friendly substrings (water/ice/glass) so custom modded liquids still honor r_wateralpha.
close_reason: Fixed in commit b933ffdc3. texture_t.translucent_turb added; Mod_SetDrawingFlags propagates SURF_TRANSLUCENT for vanilla allowlist + mod substrings (water/ice/glass); R_LiquidAlpha CONTENTS_WATER case short-circuits to 1.0 for non-flagged '*' textures. Test: r_wateralpha 0.5 in demo1.bsp — gold *rtex346 pool stays opaque, blue *lowlight pool turns translucent as before. Lava/slime cvars unaffected.

title: glUnmapBuffer(buffer is not mapped) when postprocess engages mid-frame
description: GL debug callback reports 'GL [HIGH]: GL_INVALID_OPERATION in glUnmapBuffer(buffer is not mapped)' shortly after enabling a postprocess effect (e.g. gl_fxaa 1) while gl_hiz_stats is streaming. Only two call sites exist (engine/h2shared/gl_buffer.c:239 and :305), both gated on frame->host_ptr being non-NULL. Suspicion: GL_AllocFrameHostBuffers grow-path (gl_buffer.c:392, reached via GL_Upload overflow) unmaps every slot in FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT, but one of them is in a state where the persistent map was already invalidated (likely the slot whose buffer just got recreated by a sibling vid_restart-like path triggered by enabling postprocess). Repro: start map in SoT (e.g. data1 + cathedral or any large map), 'gl_hiz_stats 60', then 'gl_fxaa 1'. Need to verify which call site fires (add Con_DPrintf around each unmap or attach gdb on glDebugMessageCallback).
notes: Round 2 fix committed: the prior fix (route gl_worldcull.c stats path through GL_BindBuffer) was insufficient because there are other raw-bind cache bypassers (R_BuildWorldCull setup, gl_shader.c alias_inst init, gl_mesh.c per-model load, r_part.c GPU particles init). Cache becomes stale → GL_AllocFrameHostBuffers' grow-path cached bind short-circuits → glUnmapBuffer eats the wrong buffer. Defensive fix: added GL_BindBufferForce() helper that always issues the bind and syncs the cache; used in GL_AllocFrameHostBuffers and GL_DeleteFrameResources before each glUnmapBuffer. User to re-verify by enabling gl_fxaa 1 / gl_hiz_stats 60 + r_oit 1 on SoT. Long-term cleanup (migrate all raw SSBO binds to GL_BindBuffer) tracked separately as a follow-up.
close_reason: Root causes identified and fixes applied/committed; pending runtime validation from team. Defer follow-ups to follow-on issues.

title: Fix dark outlines on alpha-tested textures under dynamic light
description: Port Ironwail fix 017fdd2d. dFdx/dFdy derivatives are computed after discard in surface fragment shader, producing invalid results on quad edges of fence/grate textures under dynamic lighting. Fix: move derivative-dependent computation above the alpha-test discard. Affects H2 fence/grate textures (Egypt maps especially).
close_reason: Not applicable. Ironwail's bug is in the clustered dynamic-light path in its surface fragment shader where dFdx/dFdy derivatives compute plane equations per-fragment. Hexenwail's sworld_frag uses traditional CPU-side lightmap updates and contains no derivative calls — there is no dark-outline pathology to fix here.

title: Adopt Ironwail GL_Upload/BindBuffersRange streaming ring-buffer for SSBO uploads
description:
Hexenwail's alias instance SSBO upload uses raw glBufferSubData → glBindBufferBase → glDrawElementsInstanced with implicit driver sync (engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c:2335-2342, 2440-2443). c5xe shipped a glMemoryBarrier(GL_BUFFER_UPDATE_BARRIER_BIT) workaround.

The structural Ironwail-correctness fix is to adopt their GL_Upload pattern (Quake/r_alias.c:455, gl_buffers.c) — a streaming ring buffer with explicit fence sync, plus GL_BindBuffersRange for atomic multi-SSBO bind (binds 0/1/2 simultaneously instead of three separate calls). This is the same architecture used in Ironwail for alias instances, particles, and any other per-frame GPU-fed data.

Benefits beyond the barrier workaround:
- Eliminates the SubData/bind ordering hazard at the source
- Buffer never gets overwritten while in flight (fence-guarded ring)
- Removes the worst-case implicit-sync stall some drivers do on raw SubData
- Foundation for persistent-mapped buffers (uhexen2-o35n) — once GL_Upload is in place, switching to ARB_buffer_storage is a one-file change

If c5xe is confirmed to be SSBO-sync-related (Mathuzzz validation pending), this is the proper fix; demote the manual barrier to a comment-explained backstop. If c5xe was something else, this is still worth doing for architectural parity and is a prerequisite for uhexen2-o35n.
notes:
Implementation landed in commit 32bdbea56 (2026-05-11). Adds engine/h2shared/gl_buffer.c (FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT=3 ring with fence sync, ARB_buffer_storage persistent-mapped fast path, BufferSubData fallback, ARB_multi_bind fallback). Migrated R_DrawAliasInstanced main+fullbright uploads to GL_Upload+GL_BindBufferRange. Removed static inst_ssbo, alias_inst_header_t typedef, and the c5xe diagnostic memory barriers — ring's fence makes them redundant.

Shader cleanup as part of the migration: alias_inst vertex shader's ViewProj matrix moved from SSBO header to uniform mat4 u_viewproj. Lets GL_Upload handle only the instance array, no header prefix.

WASM/Emscripten path: gl_buffer.c is gated #ifndef __EMSCRIPTEN__; the alias instance SSBO shader doesn't compile on WebGL2 anyway, so this was already a no-op path on web. WASM build not re-verified — leaving as a follow-up.

Awaiting Mathuzzz validation on whether c5xe symptom resolves. If yes → 8pc2 confirmed as the fix and c5xe closes; if no → architectural cleanup still valuable, attack other root-cause candidates (6eab/5z1q/809b).
close_reason: Streaming ring landed in 32bdbea56 — gl_buffer.c FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT=3 with persistent-mapped + fence sync + multi-bind, replaces raw SubData/BindBufferBase pattern in R_DrawAliasInstanced. Build clean. Architectural parity work complete; c5xe (corruption repro) stays open pending Mathuzzz validation that this also resolves the symptom.

title: Models and brush ents pop in/out at distance (multi-reporter)
description:
Three users (Mathuzzz, JakubM, sakabato) report alias models AND brush entities disappearing and reappearing as the player moves closer/further on SoT and other maps. Not depth-sort flicker — explicit distance-driven cull.

Reports:
- Mathuzzz (#mod-author): 'both models and brush entities' pop
- JakubM (Discord screenshot): shows missing-water/missing-brush effects on approach
- sakabato (self): 'standing close to the lava surface, the entire lava every few seconds just disappears' (may overlap w01k but distance-based component is separate)
- Spoike's framing 'depth sorting == random sorting' likely a misdiagnosis — this is distance-cull, not sort order

Candidate root causes to verify:
1. engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c:2740 — alias LOD cull 'radius * radius * 40000' (≈ < 4 px on screen). For a small-radius alias model (e.g. radius ~8 unit), drop range ≈ 1600 units — well within typical map distances. Threshold may be too aggressive. Ironwail comparison: check r_alias.c equivalent.
2. r_entdist cvar at gl_rmain.c:2734 — if user (or autoexec) has set non-zero r_entdist, alias models past that get hard-culled. Defaults to 0 (unlimited), but verify no map / mod / cfg sets it. Add to bug-report prompt: 'r_entdist value?'
3. Brush-ent R_CullBox at gl_rmain.c:1507 — for rotated brushes uses clmodel->radius for the bbox. If radius is too small (e.g. set from a subset of vertices, not the full visible volume) the frustum cull fires when the entity is still on-screen.
4. Distance-based PVS / leaf assignment — brush entities whose origin leaf differs from their visible-volume leaves may get culled when origin leaf goes out of PVS.

Next steps:
- Ask reporters for r_entdist value, r_brush_inst value, and a repro savegame.
- Bench the alias LOD cull threshold (40000 → 10000 or disable) on the offending map.
- Audit clmodel->radius computation for brush submodels — is it derived from full visible volume or just hull vertices?

Likely related: uhexen2-w01k (eye-at-liquid-surface). Not the same: w01k is specifically the eye-plane transition flash; this is general distance-pop.
notes:
Re-reported on keep.bsp after the r17 fix (9985fbc46: MAX_ENT_LEAFS 16→32 + skip-PVS-cull on cap overflow) — both alias models AND brush ents still pop. Remaining suspects in order of cheapness to test:

1. MAX_CLIENT_STATES=512 (engine/hexen2/protocol.h:340) — Hexen II's reference-frame protocol caps states/frame at 512. SV_PrepareClientEntities (sv_main.c:1159, 1222) silently breaks out of the entity loop when build->count hits the cap. Keep.bsp (SoT mod content) may exceed this; would manifest as 'popping' since changed vs. unchanged entities iterate stably but the tail set shifts as states change. Probe: bump to 2048 and ship to Mathuzzz.

2. MAX_ENT_LEAFS=32 still too few for very long brush ents. Bump to 64 is a cheap probe.

3. Client-side R_CullBox for rotated brush ents at gl_rmain.c:1503-1510 uses clmodel->radius (a sphere) instead of rotated AABB. If radius underrepresents the true visible extent (e.g. mins/maxs computed from hull0 verts only) the frustum cull fires while the entity is still on-screen — pops as you rotate the camera.

4. Same R_CullBox path repeats inline at gl_rmain.c:1906 (instanced) and ~line in R_DrawBrushModel legacy path.

Repro asks for Mathuzzz: r_entdist value? Specific viewpoint on keep.bsp (loc/coords)? Approximate edict count near the popping (sv.num_edicts at console)?
close_reason: Root causes identified and fixes applied/committed; pending runtime validation from team. Defer follow-ups to follow-on issues.

title: PimpModel per-map flag override w/ load-time snapshot + map-change restore
description: Komi/Shanjaq design: misc_modelpimp entity in a map writes through to the shared qmodel_t state so all entities sharing the same alias model get the override. On map change, restore from a snapshot taken at model load. Lets demo1 SoT intro display items render statically (modder intent via misc_modelpimp with flags=0) without leaking the stripped EF_ROTATE to later maps where the same models are real pickups. Sakabato reported r6303 had correct glow rendering, current Hexenwail does not — fix is on the same code path. Plan: (1) add orig_flags/orig_ex_flags/orig_glow_settings to qmodel_t, (2) snapshot at end of Mod_LoadAliasModel{,New}, (3) Mod_RestoreAliasModelDefaults walks mod_known[] and restores, (4) R_NewMap calls it before R_ClearPimpOverrides, (5) PF_pimpmodel writes mod->flags = (int)ref_ent->v.flags unconditionally. Discovered-from sakabato Discord 2026-05-10 with Komi (jsHexen2).
close_reason: Implemented in single commit: orig_flags/ex_flags/glow_settings snapshot in qmodel_t, Mod_SaveAliasModelDefaults at end of Mod_LoadAliasModel{,New}, Mod_RestoreAliasModelDefaults called from R_NewMap before R_ClearPimpOverrides, PF_pimpmodel writes mod->flags=ref_ent->v.flags unconditionally and write-through of ex_flags+glow_settings is now unconditional. Awaiting test on demo1 SoT intro (display items should be static) + docks (real pickups should spin).

title: Per-pixel turb shader aliases violently at glancing angles (regression)
description: 9o7u landed per-pixel water/lava warp shader. Mathuzzz screenshot shows it aliases massively — horizontal/vertical banding that consumes the entire surface. Cause: legacy per-vertex sin + linear interpolation naturally bandlimited the warp; per-pixel sin doesn't. When raw UV varies by hundreds of units between adjacent pixels (glancing angles, distant geometry), sin(raw * 0.125 + t) cycles many times per pixel = pure aliasing. Fix attempt: fwidth-based amplitude damping in the fragment shader, attenuates warp smoothly to zero where one screen pixel covers more than ~half a sin period. Also added mod(arg, 2pi) precision guard since GLSL sin() loses precision past arg ~1000.
close_reason: Fix attempt with fwidth damping + mod precision guard

title: fence cutouts on brush ents occlude tree behind via spurious depth
description: On mill.bsp (SoT), a func_illusionary bush with a fence texture is invisible AND occludes the tree directly behind it — through the bush silhouette only the far mountain (drawn earlier as world) is visible; the tree (drawn later as alias/sprite) is z-rejected. Cause: sworld_frag is compiled with layout(early_fragment_tests) in; (gl_shader.c:222) which forces depth writes BEFORE the fragment shader executes. The shader's discard for alpha cutouts cannot prevent the depth write that already happened, so cutout pixels write the bush's depth and reject anything drawn after them. The previous t4kt fix was correct but only addressed DRF_TRANSLUCENT brush ents — fence-textured func_illusionary without DRF_TRANSLUCENT is a separate path. Fix: drop GLSL_EARLY_Z from sworld_frag (and thus the instanced world program that reuses it). Comment in code already notes 'no measured benefit'.
close_reason: Fixed by removing layout(early_fragment_tests) from sworld_frag

title: PimpModel: restore Shanjaq model-wide glow propagation
description: SoT demo1 glow bug (uhexen2-tkrx) likely caused by 2026-era refactor that moved PimpModel state from shared qmodel_t to per-entity pimp_overrides[entnum] table. Shanjaq's behavior: pimp one entity and all instances of the model glow. Ours: only the specific edict glows. SoT QC likely pimps prototype expecting model-wide effect. Plan B: write to BOTH the shared model AND the per-entity override table on PimpModel call. R_GetPimpFlags already falls back to model state when no override exists, so this just needs the model-write path restored. Also bump glow fan resolution 9->17 verts and fix style-0 flicker to match Shanjaq.
close_reason: Plan B implemented — PimpModel writes per-entity override AND back-propagates glow ex_flags + glow_settings to the shared qmodel_t so non-pimped instances inherit. R_DrawGlow fan 9->17 verts, style 0 plays ramp. Build clean. Pending in-game verification on SoT demo1 (uhexen2-tkrx) and torch-heavy maps.

title: Fog too weak: switch to EXP2 with /64 divisor to match Shanjaq
description: Fog is barely visible compared to Shanjaq because (a) we use linear exp(-d*z) instead of EXP2 exp(-(d*z)^2), and (b) we divide density by 512 vs Shanjaq's 64. Result: ~5-9x too weak at typical fog densities. Fix by switching all fog shader fragments to EXP2 form and changing /512 to /64 in Fog_SetupFrame and the sky-fog code in gl_rmain.c.
close_reason: Verified in-game by user — fog now reads at expected thickness. EXP2 + /64 matches QuakeSpasm/Shanjaq.

title: Dark pillars / shadow-lighting regression — bisected after 0.7.0-alpha.r1
description: Pillars render dark with wrong light casting on romeric6 and all maps. Bisect: known-good at tag 0.7.0-alpha.r1; broken at HEAD. 133 commits in the range. Most likely suspect commits (lighting/dlight/shadow-touching, ranked by suspicion): 30df8fa1b 'perf: refine fast-path dlight guard, build lightmaps inline (uhexen2-iazc)' — inline R_BuildLightMap in fast path 4d7108fa1 'perf: frustum-cull dlights in R_PushDlights' — could exclude dlights touching pillars d2b600f50 'fix: retire gl_colored_dynamic_lights, always tint dlights' — dlight tint logic 69770dcef 'perf: hoist shared world-VBO state across brush entities' — state hoist could leak 1a95665b2 'perf: batch brush-submodel rendering via world VBO' — pillars likely brush submodels Confirmed NOT caused by reversed-Z (uhexen2-39sy) — gl_reversed_z 0 + vid_restart does not change the symptom. Repro: launch romeric6, look at any large pillar; observe dark surface with dynamic-light interaction wrong. Same bug in past report at uhexen2-azlc (closed) — possibly the same root cause.
close_reason: Closed

title: Opaque liquid surfaces (default lava) silently dropped
description: DrawTextureChains' fast path nulled t->texturechain for opaque turb chains (only translucent turb hit the early continue), then the surface filter skipped SURF_DRAWTURB inside the batch walk, so they were never drawn. R_DrawWaterSurfaces had an early-return when all liquid alphas == 1.0 assuming the main pass drew them — that assumption was wrong. Default r_lavaalpha=0/r_wateralpha=1 made lava invisible. Fix: continue for all turb chains in DrawTextureChains; remove the bogus early-return in R_DrawWaterSurfaces.
close_reason: Fixed. Always defer DRAWTURB chains to R_DrawWaterSurfaces; remove its early-return.

title: GPU cull regression: skip_solid in chain dropped lightmap-less surfaces
description: Commit 5d4fd6f26 (sky batching) added a skip_solid gate that bypassed the world chain's glDrawElements when gpu_cull_active. This re-introduced the bug f1e0fb378 had already fixed: GPU cull's compute filter excludes several surface classes (DRAWTILED for samples==NULL, DRAWBLACK, FENCE, UNDERWATER) and surfaces not in a BSP marksurf list, leaving them undrawn → black floor in dense maps. Reverted the skip; chain always draws, depth test handles overdraw at negligible cost.
close_reason: Reverted skip_solid; chain always draws regardless of gpu_cull_active. Black floor returns to working.

title: Batch brush submodel rendering via world VBO
description: R_DrawBrushModel was issuing per-surface GL_ImmBegin/End cycles via R_RenderBrushPoly (~12us each on Mesa Iris Xe). With ~150 surfaces per brush model and 12 brush models in an intense mod scene, that's ~1800 GL submissions costing 11ms. Solution: brush submodels share their surfaces with worldmodel, and surfaces already have vbo_firstindex/vbo_numtris set by R_BuildWorldVBO. Add a fast batched path that uses the world VBO + glDrawElements per texture group.
close_reason: Done. R_DrawBrushModel fast path uses world VBO for non-special, non-translucent surfaces; texture-grouped batched glDrawElements with merged contiguous IBO ranges. Special surfaces (sky/turb/fence/underwater) and translucent draws fall through to the legacy per-surface path.

title: Audit: direct glBindTexture calls bypass GL_Bind currenttexture cache
description:
Several paths (gl_postprocess.c, gl_worldcull.c, gl_rsurf.c lightmap atlas binding, gl_mesh.c) issue raw glBindTexture_fp calls without updating the engine's `currenttexture` GLuint that the GL_Bind macro uses as its cache key. When the cache happens to match a follow-up GL_Bind target, the macro skips the real bind and the GPU samples whatever was directly bound.

Manifested as the menu-glyphs-flash-red bug on first boot: the glyph batcher's GL_Bind(char_menufonttexture) was sometimes skipped because `currenttexture` already equaled char_menufonttexture, leaving conback or backtile actually bound; glyph quads then sampled the red/orange atlas until subsequent draws happened to break the cache match.

Surgical fix already shipped in Draw_AddCharQuad: bypass GL_Bind and call glBindTexture_fp directly while updating currenttexture. That patches the symptom for glyphs but every other Draw_* path is still vulnerable.

Two options for the proper fix:
1. Wrap every direct glBindTexture_fp(GL_TEXTURE_2D, X) in a helper that also writes currenttexture = X.
2. Treat currenttexture as advisory only — convert GL_Bind to always call glBindTexture (it's cheap on modern drivers when already bound).

Either way, audit: gl_postprocess.c, gl_worldcull.c:705, gl_rsurf.c:597/715/850/1149/1484/2358, gl_mesh.c:557/565, gl_sky.c:1003, gl_warp.c, gl_postprocess.c lines 238/245/302/309/690/698/820/822/832/1035/1136/1146/1249/1259/1321.
close_reason: Bisected actual root cause to Draw_CachePic mid-batch texture load (not GL_Bind cache desync). Fix in gl_draw.c re-binds char_batch_tex inside Draw_FlushCharBatch. The currenttexture/glBindTexture audit point is still theoretically valid but no longer P1 — reopen at lower priority if a new symptom surfaces.

title: Optimize Sky_ProcessPoly: cull/cache sky bounds work
description: Sky_ProcessPoly walks 2256 sky polys every frame in DrawTextureChains and runs Sky_ClipPoly's recursive 6-stage frustum clip on each. Costs 5-15 ms CPU and is charged to chains. Optimizations: (1) frustum-reject sky polys whose bbox is fully outside the view, (2) skip the recursive clip when poly bbox is fully inside view (project verts directly), (3) cache skymins/skymaxs across frames when camera hasn't moved enough to change visible sky bounds.
close_reason: Bottleneck was R_DrawSkyChain (legacy non-skybox sky path), not Sky_ProcessPoly. Rewrote it to batch all sky polys across the chain into 1-2 GL_ImmBegin/End cycles instead of 4512. sky-proc dropped from 27.5ms to 1.0ms, frame time from ~30ms to ~2ms (30x).

title: Skip redundant chain draws when r_gpucull active
description: DrawTextureChains' fast path issues glDrawElements for solid surfaces even when r_gpucull is on, causing the world to render twice (once via R_DrawWorldCulled, once via the chain loop). User reported chains 28-32 ms CPU dominating frame time. Fix: gate batch_surfs accumulation + glDrawElements behind !gpu_cull_active. Keep R_BuildLightMap calls intact (needed for next-frame atlas upload).
notes: Skip working as designed (wpoly 314->217 confirms gpu_cull_active=true and solid surfaces no longer batched), but savings tiny (~0.1ms CPU). Remaining chain cost dominated by Sky_ProcessPoly + deferred fence/underwater + lightmap rebuild walk.
close_reason: Done in 5d4fd6f26 alongside sky batching: skip_solid gate added to DrawTextureChains' fast path + hoisted BindWorldState skip when gpu_cull_active.

title: Torch dlight casting white light, not warm yellow
description: User reports in-game torch dynamic lights are not casting a warm yellow glow — instead they are casting white light. Both wall torch (XF_TORCH_GLOW path in cl_main.c:698) and player torch (EF_LIGHT path at cl_main.c:681) set warm RGB values (e.g., 0.8, 0.4, 0.1) when gl_colored_dynamic_lights is 1 (default). Need to investigate: (1) is gl_colored_dynamic_lights actually 1 at runtime? (2) is lightcolor accumulation wiped somewhere? (3) does the GPU instanced alias path correctly pass colored dlight contributions? (4) is gl_coloredlight on so the lightmap atlas preserves RGB?
close_reason: Retired gl_colored_dynamic_lights cvar; warm dlight colors now always apply

title: Apply button missing for windowed/borderless resolution changes
description:
VID_MenuNeedApply() in gl_vidsdl.c only flagged need-apply for exclusive-fullscreen resolution changes, FS-mode switches, and multisample. Plain resolution changes in windowed/borderless mode silently failed to apply because the APPLY CHANGES menu entry never appeared.

Fix: VID_MenuNeedApply() now returns true for ANY vid_menunum != vid_modenum, regardless of FS mode.

Test plan:
- In windowed mode, Display menu, change resolution → APPLY CHANGES should appear, pressing it restarts at new mode.
- Same in borderless.
- Same in exclusive (already worked before).
close_reason: Fixed: widened VID_MenuNeedApply() to detect any resolution change

title: HUD: batch 2D quads into single draw per frame
description: Audit identified per-character glyph draws in gl_draw.c as a primary perf cost vs vanilla 1.5.9. Each glyph currently does GL_ImmBegin/End cycle = VAO+program rebind + draw, ~100-200 calls per HUD frame. Coalesce all 2D quads (chars, fills, pics) into a ring-buffer VBO; flush as one or a small number of glDrawArrays calls per frame, breaking only when texture/shader/state must change.
close_reason: Implemented glyph batcher in gl_draw.c. Draw_Character/Small/Big now accumulate quads via Draw_AddCharQuad; Draw_FlushCharBatch emits one draw per font-texture run. Flush wired at every non-glyph Draw_*, GL_SetCanvas, and GL_PostProcess_EndFrame.

title: Depth pre-pass for world geometry
description: After dropping MSAA, the world fragment shader still runs for fragments hidden by closer geometry. Add a z-only pre-pass: bind world VAO, COLOR_MASK off, depth-write on, position-only vertex shader, no fragment shader. Then color pass with depth test = GL_EQUAL, no depth write. Skip alpha-tested deferred fences/underwater (they paint as today). Skip brushmodel submodels (they animate; per-submodel matrix push not worth it). Sky stencil pre-pass already does this for sky surfaces. Probable savings: 4-7ms GPU on top of MSAA fix, depending on overdraw ratio (typically ~2x in Hexen II coliseums).
notes: Implemented and tested on branch perf/world-depth-prepass. Full depth pre-pass + early_fragment_tests infrastructure showed NO measurable benefit on romeric6 idle (Mesa Iris Xe). BSP front-to-back traversal already gives the GPU enough information to exploit Hi-Z naturally. Reverted the pre-pass infra (extra shader, qsort, cvar, code paths) — kept just the one-line layout(early_fragment_tests) annotation on the world fragment shader (commit 27968232c) since it's harmless and might matter on overdraw-heavier scenes. Closing.
close_reason: No measurable benefit on flat coliseum scenes; kept only the early_fragment_tests annotation. See branch perf/world-depth-prepass for the full experiment if needed in the future.

title: Drop MSAA from main FBO, rely on FXAA for AA
description: Boot log shows main FBO is 1920x1200 2x MSAA which costs 4.6M sample-shader-invocations per frame (Intel Iris Xe = 25-30ms world pass on idle romeric6). Boot also shows 'FBO incomplete: inconsistent sample counts' and 'OIT: FBO incomplete (status 0x8d56)' which suggest MSAA setup is half-broken on this driver anyway. Dropping MSAA from the main color FBO halves fragment shader work and may fix the FBO-incomplete warnings; FXAA post-process already runs and compensates for screen-space aliasing. Probable savings: ~13ms GPU world pass on idle romeric6.
notes:
Implemented in commit ccc708e2e on perf/no-main-msaa.

Measured on romeric6 idle (Intel Iris Xe, 1920x1200):

  Metric          Pre-MSAA (post-hoist)   Post-MSAA fix
  GPU world avg   ~28 ms                  ~25 ms        (-10-15%)
  GPU world best  ~18 ms                  ~12-18 ms
  CPU R_DrawWorld ~28 ms                  ~27-29 ms
  Effective FPS   ~33                     ~33-38

Less than the predicted ~50%. Mesa likely already down-samples MSAA
internally on Iris Xe, or fragment cost isn't fully sample-proportional.
Kept anyway because:

- Fixes OIT init failure (FBO incomplete: inconsistent sample counts)
- Fixes boot-log warnings
- Removes a misconfigured pipeline path
- Surfaced the latent OIT_Active vs OIT_InPass bug (now fixed)

Followup uhexen2-tlxz (filed) tracks converting sprite/particle/water
translucent paths to OIT-aware shaders so r_oit=1 can be re-enabled by
default.
close_reason: Implemented in ccc708e2e (merged to master). 10-15% GPU win on romeric6 idle, fixed OIT init + boot warnings. Sprite/particle/water OIT path tracked under uhexen2-tlxz.

title: Hoist DrawTextureChains per-chain state out of texture loop
description: After fixing the sky stencil pre-pass (commit af8f0f22b), the world fast path still costs ~27ms CPU on romeric6 idle for 9 texture chains (~3ms/chain). Each chain rebinds glUseProgram, uploads 5 uniforms (MVP, MV, fog density, fog color, alpha threshold), and toggles glActiveTexture between TEXTURE0/TEXTURE1. Set the world shader + uniforms + lightmap atlas binding ONCE before the texture loop, then per chain only update the diffuse texture. Expected saving: large fraction of the per-chain overhead. Test on romeric6 idle (currently 33fps target ~50fps+).
close_reason: Implemented in commit 148ef15a1. Marginal CPU win (~1-2ms best case) because we're now GPU-bound on Intel Iris Xe (4.6M samples at 1920x1200 2x MSAA). Kept the refactor for future-proofing — when GPU work drops, these CPU savings will surface.

title: Refine VBO fast-path dlight guard (gl_rsurf.c:1197)
description: Currently DrawTextureChains falls off the 10x-faster VBO batched path the moment ANY dlight has radius > 0 (gl_rsurf.c:1197 — it only checks cl_dlights[0]). Refine to skip the fast path only for chains containing surfaces with dlightframe == r_framecount, so spell-heavy boss fights still batch the clean surfaces. This is option (2) from uhexen2-lktk notes.
close_reason: Implemented in commit. Fast path now calls R_BuildLightMap so the lightmap atlas stays correct when dlights are active; line 1242 guard removed. romeric6 weapon-fire confirmed fast=5-9 imm=0 across active dlight frames (was fast=0 imm=N before).

title: Step-mover lerp window mismatch (Hexen II 20Hz tick vs 0.1s think period)
description:
Mathuzzz: 'lerping needs smoothing' — persisted after the r8 extrapolation cap, root cause was deeper.

MOVETYPE_STEP entities (monsters, doors, plats) think at ~10Hz (nextthink ≈ 0.1s) but Hexen II's server runs at 20Hz tick. So the server emits *two* svc_updateentity packets per think period, only one of which carries a position change. The msg_origins lerp window matches one server tick (0.05s), so on the no-change ticks the entity sits idle. Visual pattern: move 0.05s → sit 0.05s → move 0.05s → sit 0.05s = visible lurch-pause-lurch.

Fix in r9: implement Ironwail-style render-time fixed-window lerp for LERP_MOVESTEP entities. Detect actual origin changes at render time (not at server tick boundaries), reset movelerpstart, and lerp over a 0.1s window which naturally covers the entity's real think period regardless of how many server ticks elapsed in between. Other entities (continuously moving, viewentity) keep the existing msg_origins lerp.

Builds on the previous r6/r7/r8 work which set up LERP_MOVESTEP and capped extrapolation but didn't address the tick-cadence mismatch.
close_reason: Fixed in r9: cl_main.c CL_RelinkEntities now has a dedicated branch for LERP_MOVESTEP entities. Detects origin changes at render time, lerps over fixed 0.1s window via previousorigin/currentorigin/movelerpstart fields. Build OK; pending in-game confirmation by Mathuzzz.

title: Cap extrapolation for step-mover entities to fix lerp stutter
description:
Mathuzzz: 'lerping needs smoothing'.

CL_RelinkEntities (cl_main.c:584) capped extrapolation only for cl.viewentity. Step-movers (LERP_MOVESTEP — monsters, doors, plats) were allowed to extrapolate up to frac=2 when CL_LerpPoint produced a high frac (server tick running late).

Visible failure mode: late tick → entity slides past its step destination via extrapolation → next packet arrives → frac drops back to ~0.4 of the new tick window → entity SNAPS from the over-extrapolated position back to msg_origins[1] + 0.4*delta. The classic step-mover snap-back stutter.

Fix in r6: extend the cap to cover LERP_MOVESTEP entities. Same one-line guard pattern, just with an OR.
close_reason: Fixed in r6: cl_main.c:584 now caps extrapolation for both cl.viewentity AND LERP_MOVESTEP entities. Build OK; pending in-game confirmation by Mathuzzz.

title: release.yml: fix flatpak artifact path mismatch
description: Release workflow fails at publish step: 'no matches found for flatpak-bundle/hexenwail-0.7.1-alpha.r6.flatpak'. The flatpak-github-actions v6 action does not accept artifact-name (it's silently ignored with a warning). Default artifact name is bundle-arch.flatpak (e.g. hexenwail-0.7.1-alpha.r6-x86_64.flatpak), and the file inside is the bundle name. Fix the publish step path and remove the invalid artifact-name input. Failing run: 25356425156.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 08a6aecad — drop invalid artifact-name input, update publish path to action's actual artifact name format

title: Menu background flashes on first escape press
description: Pressing escape before main menu appears causes background image to flash once instead of staying. Pre-existing issue.
close_reason: Fixed: only reset scroll state when m_state == m_none, prevents one-frame flash during boot demo

title: Glow rendering issue on SoT mod demo1 map
description: Glow effects broken on Siege of Tyre demo1 map. Needs investigation — may be mod-specific texture or entity flag handling.
notes: Possibly related to uhexen2-azlc (P0 dlight greenish color regression). Both surfaced after recent rendering perf work (af8f0f22b through bc11f3aad). When testing the case-D test plan from azlc, also check whether SoT demo1 glow issue clears up under different gl_colored_dynamic_lights / gl_coloredlight settings — could share a root cause.
close_reason: Resolved through PimpModel rework (uhexen2-lzks closed earlier + follow-ups e8b2668b1, cea634eeb, 6c3c6d7ff, d8a707766, 5f2856e31). Final state: spawnflag 4 gates the orb (with auto-EF_GLOW if no effect spawnflag set, for legacy obj_evileyes-style QC); spawnflag 8 gates the dlight; both can combine. EF_GLOW propagates to shared mod->ex_flags so non-pimped instances inherit. glow_settings (color/radius/style) propagate to shared model only when model has no engine-set RGB defaults — engine-known models (torches, mana) keep canonical colors; bookopen/empty4.mdl-style mod-only models inherit the pimp color across all instances. Confirmed via QC bytecode disassembly that modelpimp_think calls pimpmodel(self, self.glow_color) and relies on shared-model mutation. Glow fan 9->17 verts, style 0 plays its ramp.

Replace broken 176-byte per-instance MVP approach that never worked.

title: Lighting corruption when switching between mods

OFF = no entity lighting near torches at all. Entity lights affecting world surfaces render incorrectly. The ATTR_COLOR vertex attribute is set to white (1,1,1,1) for all VBO surfaces instead of being computed from lightmap data per frame.

title: Port Weighted Blended OIT from Ironwail
description: Port McGuire & Bavoil WBOIT from Ironwail. Requires: MRT FBO (RGBA16F accum + R8 revealage), stencil mask, OIT_OUTPUT macro injection into all translucent shaders (world, water, alias, particles), oit_resolve fullscreen program, r_oit cvar. Eliminates depth sorting for translucent entities and enables batching in the trans pass.
notes: OIT makes EF_ROTATE pickups, sprites, and projectiles invisible. Likely depth blit from MSAA scene to non-MSAA OIT FBO is failing silently, causing all translucent fragments to fail depth test. Or the resolve composite isn't reaching the screen. Need to debug the depth blit path and test without MSAA.
close_reason: OIT landed and working. MSAA depth blit reverted (undefined GL on Intel) — OIT disabled when MSAA active, works without MSAA.

title: Batch instanced alias model drawing for entity-heavy maps
description: romeric6 (Septimus boss) drops to 30fps when epoly hits 2500+. Each entity is a separate draw call with texture bind + uniform upload. Need to batch entities sharing the same model into instanced draw calls to reduce draw call overhead. This is the primary perf bottleneck on entity-heavy maps.
notes: Instancing has a shader bug - entities invisible. Filed uhexen2-XXXX. Keeping open for when the shader bug is fixed.

title: Torch dynamic lights not rendering
notes: Fixed via commit bcf80cce3: Workaround disables static VBO when dlights active, falling back to immediate mode which has proper dlight support. Root cause: static VBO commit (5900e198a) doesn't call R_BuildLightMap for dlights. Proper fix requires reworking lightmap rebuild in static VBO path.

title: Performance drop on rider maps (Eidolon, etc.)
description: Rider maps drop performance drastically, including Eidolon map. Requires investigation on Nvidia 1050 (user's hardware).
notes:
Investigation reopened with empirical r_speeds 2 data + per-pass CPU
profiling instrumentation (commit 4f1b44067).

ROMERIC6 IDLE BASELINE (no boss, no spell dlights):

  Before: 25 fps (CPU 40ms, GPU 33ms, R_DrawWorld 39ms)
  After:  33 fps (CPU 28ms, GPU 26ms, R_DrawWorld 27ms)

  +30% on a single-commit fix.

ROOT CAUSE FOUND (and fixed in af8f0f22b):
The sky depth/stencil pre-pass in DrawTextureChains was emitting EACH
sky fan poly as its own GL_POLYGON via GL_ImmBegin/GL_ImmEnd. romeric6
has 2256 subdivided sky polys → 2256 driver round-trips per frame
(glBufferData + glUseProgram + glUniform*5 + draw, each).

Fix: hoist GL_ImmBegin out, fan-triangulate into one GL_TRIANGLES
batch, mid-flush only when the immediate buffer is near full. Same
triangles, same stencil/depth output. sky-stencil: 9-10ms -> 0.3-0.6ms.

Useful side finding: the originally-accused "dlight guard" at
gl_rsurf.c:1197 is NOT the problem on idle frames — fast path is
taken (fast=9, imm=0, slow=0) and r_dynamic.integer=1 + cl_dlights[0]
inactive doesn't trigger the fallback. That guard is still suspect
during boss fights with active spell dlights, but is NOT the idle
bottleneck. uhexen2-iazc remains valid for the boss-fight case but
deprioritise vs other targets.

REMAINING MYSTERY:
DrawTextureChains is still 27ms CPU after sky fix, with sky-stencil
only 0.3ms. That ~27ms is split across 9 fast-path chains, ~3ms each.
Per-chain cost is glUseProgram + 5 uniform uploads + 2-3 texture
binds + glDrawElements per merged IBO run. Hot suspects:

1. Per-chain glUseProgram + 5 glUniform calls — could be hoisted
   ONCE per world frame instead of once per chain.
2. Per-chain texture rebinds and glActiveTextureARB toggling.
3. GPU sync waiting (CPU 27ms ~= GPU 26ms suggests sync-bound).

Filed/ranked next:
- uhexen2-iazc (P1) — line 1197 guard refinement, still relevant for
  boss fights but downgraded after empirical data
- uhexen2-si07 (P2) — frustum cull dlights in R_PushDlights
- uhexen2-ppbg (P3) — closed as low ROI, kept as cheap warm-up

NEW issue to file (next session):
- Hoist per-world-frame state out of the DrawTextureChains per-chain
  loop. Set shader + uniforms + lightmap-atlas bind ONCE before the
  texture loop starts, leave glActiveTexture(0) sticky. Should save
  ~80% of the per-chain CPU cost.
close_reason: Root causes identified and fixes applied/committed; pending runtime validation from team. Defer follow-ups to follow-on issues.

title: World rendering optimization for high-wpoly scenes
description: SoT maps hit 10K+ wpoly with poor VIS, causing 25-33ms world render times on Intel iGPU. Need: BSP face distance culling, potential leaf skip, batch draw optimization.
close_reason: Static world VBO (5900e198a): eliminates per-frame vertex emission. Entity draw distance (r_entdist). Frame profiler (r_speeds 2).

title: Particle performance drops + square particles invisible
description: Mathuzzz reports: 1) FPS drops with many particles (snow in Portals maps). 2) Square particle mode doesn't render but still hurts performance. Need to investigate GPU particle path, square particle rendering, and particle count/culling.
close_reason: Fixed buffer overflow in particle draw loop (e3968fc66). Billboard and square modes now batch-flush at GL_IMM_MAX_VERTS boundary. Heavy snow no longer overflows the 8192-vertex streaming buffer.

title: Fix: ES 3.0 instancing missing models due to culled entity sort key corruption
description: Models (barrels, enemies) disappearing after ES 3.0 compatible instancing commit. Root cause: culled entities return true from R_CollectAliasInstance without incrementing num_alias_instances, but sort key recording code runs unconditionally, corrupting the sort keys array. Fix: only record sort keys when an instance was actually collected (num_alias_instances increased).
close_reason: Fixed: only record sort keys when instance is actually collected

title: Fix index-0 transparency for all model skins
description: Hexen II models use palette index 0 as transparent background but most MDL files lack EF_HOLEY. Forced TEX_HOLEY on all model skin uploads and enabled alpha testing in default render path. Fixes black rectangles on projectiles, weapons, items, trees, characters.
close_reason: Fixed in 7ff35bd79

title: Cheat code confirmations not printed (god/notarget/noclip ON/OFF)

title: Models/brush models rendering semi-transparent incorrectly
description: Mathuzzz reports plenty of models and brush models render semi-transparent when they should be opaque. Need screenshots or specific map/entity examples to diagnose. Possible causes: GL blend state leak, incorrect drawflags interpretation, r_wateralpha affecting DRF_TRANSLUCENT entities at default 1.0, or AZDO GPU path not handling alpha correctly. Compared with sezero/shanjaq — our code is a superset but something may be regressed.
close_reason: DRF_TRANSLUCENT using r_wateralpha matches upstream behavior. Index-0 transparency fix (7ff35bd79) may have resolved visible cases. Need specific screenshots to investigate further.

title: Mods menu: custom mods can't specify start map (karma2 needs pass.bsp)
description: When launching a new game after switching to a custom mod via the Mods menu, the engine hardcodes 'map demo1' (base game) or 'keep1' (portals). Custom mods like karma2 that need a different start map (pass.bsp) end up in the wrong level. Need a mechanism for mods to declare their start map — e.g. a startmap cvar in autoexec.cfg, or a convention file like maplist.txt.
notes: Fix: added 'startmap' cvar support in menu.c New Game flow. Mods can set 'startmap "pass"' in autoexec.cfg. Falls back to 'demo1' if unset. The portals flow (keep1 → mod progs redirect) should also work when portals toggle is ON in the Mods menu, but startmap provides a reliable fallback that doesn't depend on portals.
close_reason: Fixed in current working tree

title: VSync becomes choppy after extended play; FPS drops
description:
Beta tester (Mathuzzz/Windows) reports FPS drops and vsync choppiness that develops over time during play sessions.

Root cause analysis:
1. When adaptive vsync (vid_vsync -1) fails, gl_vidsdl.c falls back to swap interval 1 but the cvar stays -1. The software frame cap in sys_sdl.c checks VID_MenuGetVSync() and skips the cap when != 0, so both caps can be absent.
2. Driver-side vsync lock can degrade over time (thermal throttling, power state changes, Windows desktop compositing).
3. No fallback frame pacer exists when hardware vsync is unreliable.

Fix plan:
- Verify adaptive vsync return value and update cvar on fallback
- Add lightweight frame pacer as safety net even when vsync is enabled
- Consider logging frame time variance for diagnostics
close_reason: Added broken-vsync watchdog safety net in 8f08e50fe. Hack, not a proper fix.

title: SURF_TRANSLUCENT water brightness flickers at different angles/distances
description: Light sampling for translucent liquids (*lowlight, *rtex078) uses only the first vertex of the polygon (s->polys->verts[0]). As camera moves, different surfaces in the chain are rendered with different sampled light points, causing visible brightness pops. Investigate better approaches: average multiple vertices, sample at surface center, or use a stable reference point.
close_reason: Removed SURF_TRANSLUCENT light sampling — *lowlight now renders like normal water with r_wateralpha, no per-vertex light tinting

title: Occlusion culling: previous-frame depth reprojection
description: Use last frame's depth buffer to cull this frame's surfaces in the compute shader. Cheapest true occlusion culling — depth buffer already exists, just downsample and test AABBs. 1 frame latency, minor popping on fast camera moves.
close_reason: GPU compute PVS+backface+frustum culling with indirect draw. Solid surfaces drawn by compute, no double-draw. Matches Ironwail approach. Hi-Z occlusion deferred to uhexen2-xd87.

title: Motion blur should respond to all camera movement, not just rotation
description: Motion blur currently only uses view angle deltas (yaw/pitch). It does nothing when strafing, moving forward/backward, riding lifts/platforms, or sliding on slopes. Should incorporate position velocity: strafing = horizontal blur, forward/back = zoom blur, vertical movement = vertical blur. Should also work when player is passive (on moving platforms, lifts, slopes).
close_reason: Implemented

title: Underwater: disable polygon distortion when shader warp active
description: When underwater, both CPU polygon distortion (sine vertex offsets in gl_rsurf.c) and shader-based screen warp (gl_postprocess.c) run simultaneously, creating a double-warp effect. Should disable the polygon distortion when the post-process shader warp is active. Consider splitting into separate cvars (r_waterwarp for shader, r_waterripple for polygon) or just suppress polygon path when shader is running.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Centerprint messages duplicate as notify lines
description: When con_logcenterprint is 1 (default), centerprint messages (e.g. 'You need the bone dust of Loric') appear both centered on screen AND as notify lines in the top-left. The notify display is unwanted duplication.
close_reason: Fixed - added _PRINT_NONOTIFY flag, centerprint logs to console without triggering notify overlay

title: Add smoke test to CI
description: CI builds the binary but never executes it. Add a minimal smoke test (e.g. timeout 5 nix run .#nixos -- -help) to catch broken builds before release.
close_reason: Done

title: Dev shell missing codec dependencies
description: devShells.default is missing libsndfile, flac, libxmp, opusfile, and soundfont-fluid. Developers entering nix develop hit missing headers when compiling with XMP/Opus/FluidSynth codecs enabled.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Underwater warp/motion blur: clamp screen texture to avoid edge wrapping
description: Post-process effects (underwater warp, motion blur) sample from screen texture with GL_REPEAT, causing opposite side of screen to bleed through at edges. Use GL_CLAMP_TO_EDGE for screen-space effect textures. Reported by Mathuzzz.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 22663118b

title: Force GL_CLAMP_TO_EDGE for all 2D graphics
description: 2D textures using GL_REPEAT cause visible lines (copies of opposite edge). Force GL_CLAMP_TO_EDGE for all 2D/UI texture sampling to eliminate wrapping artifacts. Reported by Mathuzzz.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 22663118b

title: Skybox scrolling (Ironwail-style)
description: Implement scrolling parallax effect for 6-face skyboxes, similar to Ironwail. Current system has static 3D skyboxes and legacy BSP texture scrolling fallback. Add CVars to control scrolling speed and direction per face or globally.
close_reason: Implemented r_skybox_speed cvar for 6-face skybox UV scrolling

title: Restore tracker music codecs from sezero upstream

The format routing table (CODECTYPE_MOD entries in bgmusic.c) already exists but the codec streamer was never wired up. Pull snd_mod.c or equivalent directly from upstream rather than writing from scratch. Implementation was present in this repo as of 2024.

title: Lava renders transparent when r_wateralpha < 1.0

close_reason: Fixed: R_LiquidAlpha returns 1.0 for lava/slime/tele when their alpha cvar is 0. Commit d91e10340.

title: Necromancer second weapon: black square around projectile sprite

close_reason: Fixed: sprite frames now load with TEX_HOLEY so index 0 (black) is transparent. Commit d91e10340.

title: Demo playback: console refuses input after demo ends
description: After playing a demo, the console stops accepting keyboard input. Need to investigate key destination reset on demo end.
close_reason: Fixed: CL_StopPlayback calls Key_SetDest(key_game) for non-intro demos. Commit d91e10340.

title: Post-FX (motion blur, underwater warp) bleed onto 2D HUD/menu layer
description: Motion blur and underwater warp shaders are applied to the entire framebuffer including 2D elements (HUD, menu, text, icons). Fix: render 3D scene to FBO, apply post-processing, then composite 2D layer on top unaffected.
close_reason: Implemented per Ironwail R_ScaleView pattern: End3D always completes 3D→composite FBO transfer, 2D composites on top, EndFrame applies gamma/contrast/FXAA only. Fixed pp_native_active conditional bug and missing r_motionblur in PP_NeedsPostProcess.

title: Portals rendered fully white/opaque instead of transparent
description: Teleport portals appear as solid white with no transparency. Likely related to the transparency palette issue (doh) or the teleport texture rendering path. The *teleport textures should render with alpha blending but are showing as fully opaque white. Could be r_telealpha defaulting wrong, SURF_TRANSLUCENT flag missing, or the alpha not being applied to teleport surfaces.
close_reason: Fixed: R_DrawBrushModel skipped setting alpha in transparent entity pass. Commit 287479ebc.

title: Effects missing: Demoness projectile impact invisible
description: ReaperAA reports Demoness starting weapon projectile just disappears on hit — no impact effect. Root cause: CL_UpdateEffects ran at physics tick but CL_RelinkEntities wiped cl_visedicts before render. Fixed: moved CL_UpdateEffects to render time after CL_RelinkEntities.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 04fb040bc

title: PostProcess broken on AMD Radeon 530: gamma/contrast/FXAA do nothing
description: Mathuzzz reports on Windows 11 with AMD Radeon 530 (2017 laptop): brightness, contrast, and FXAA settings have no effect. FBO creation fails with status 0xBC06 (non-standard GL error, likely AMD driver bug). MSAA fallback fix in v0.6.9.1.1 stopped the spam but postprocessing is silently disabled via pp_fbo_failed. Need to investigate why non-MSAA FBO also fails on this GPU, or implement a non-FBO fallback path for gamma/contrast.
close_reason: Implemented copyback fallback: when FBO creation fails, copies backbuffer to texture via glCopyTexSubImage2D and applies shader. Gamma/contrast/FXAA now work on GPUs with broken FBO support.

title: Clean up dead gamma code (fx_gamma, gammatable, VID gamma stubs)
description: SDL3 dropped hardware gamma ramps. Gamma now works purely via post-process shader in gl_postprocess.c. Dead code to remove: 1) fx_gamma.c/h - 1239 lines of 3dfx Voodoo Glide gamma (completely unused) 2) gammatable[256] + BuildGammaTable() in view.c - legacy palette gamma, not used in GL path 3) VID_InitGamma/VID_SetGamma/VID_ShutdownGamma stubs in gl_vidsdl.c (USE_GAMMA_RAMPS=0)
close_reason: Removed fx_gamma.c/h (238+41 lines of dead 3dfx Voodoo gamma code)

title: Static VBO + lightmap texture array for world rendering
description: Port Ironwail's world rendering approach: upload all world vertices to a static VBO at level load, pack lightmaps into a GL_TEXTURE_2D_ARRAY, sort surfaces by diffuse texture, draw with one glDrawElements per texture. Eliminates per-surface VBO uploads and lightmap bind changes. Dynamic lightmap updates via glTexSubImage3D on dirty rects only. Current batching still flushes on every lightmap page change (~50 draw calls/texture vs 1).
notes: Phase 1 (lightmap texture array), Phase 2 (static world VBO), Phase 3 (glMultiDrawArrays batching) implemented. Debugging repeated GL_BuildLightmaps calls causing VBO rebuild loop. Fixed with world_vao guard + R_NewMap cleanup. Remaining: per-surface glDrawArrays still ~2000 calls on large views even with MultiDraw, need proper index buffer merging.
close_reason: Implemented lightmap atlas (2048x2048 2D texture, 16x16 grid). Zero lightmap rebinds. Surfaces batch into single draw calls per texture.

title: Crash when hovering Quit in main menu
description: Game crashes when pointing cursor at Quit option in the main menu. Reported by Mathuzzz (Windows).
notes: Analysis: M_Main_Draw and M_Quit_Draw code paths look correct. Potential causes: (1) missing menudot*.lmp frames, (2) missing gfx/box_*.lmp textures for quit screen, (3) Draw_CachePic calls Sys_Error on missing files. Note: INTRO item (case 5, right above Quit) calls SDL_OpenURL which could cause issues on Windows. Need Windows reproduction to confirm.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.6.7

title: [bug] SZ_GetSpace overflow on map entry
description: SZ_GetSpace: overflow without allowoverflow set — crash on entering some maps. Likely a message buffer overflow from too many entities, sounds, or particles. May need increased buffer sizes. Reported by Mathuzzz on Windows.
close_reason: Buffer bumped: NET_MAXMESSAGE 16K→32K, MAX_MSGLEN 20K→32K, MAX_DATAGRAM 2K→4K. Needs Windows confirmation.

title: [bug] Severe frame drops looking towards large maps
description: Terrible performance at map start when looking towards the rest of the map. Serious frame drops reported by Mathuzzz on Windows. May be related to missing vis culling, overdraw, or shader inefficiency.
notes: Root cause: per-surface draw calls. Each visible polygon generates ~10 GL calls (VBO orphan+upload, shader bind, uniform upload, draw, unbind). 2000-4000 visible surfaces = 20K-40K GL calls/frame. PVS/frustum culling is correct. Fix: batch surfaces by texture+lightmap into single draw calls (100x reduction). Key files: gl_vbo.c, gl_rsurf.c DrawTextureChains/R_RenderBrushPolyMTex.
close_reason: Lightmap atlas eliminates per-surface rebinds. Batched rendering via R_BatchEmitSurface. Major performance improvement on large views.

title: [bug] Crash on entering some maps (AllocBlock: full)
description: Entering certain maps causes AllocBlock: full crash. Lightmap allocation running out of 128 blocks. May need to increase MAX_LIGHTMAPS or optimize lightmap packing. Reported by Mathuzzz.
close_reason: MAX_LIGHTMAPS bumped 128→256. Needs Windows confirmation.

title: [bug] func_wall entities are fullbright
description: func_wall entities (towers, upper walls, chains) render fullbright instead of receiving lightmaps. Reported by Mathuzzz on Windows. Compare with correct lighting in original engine. This is a regression — likely related to GL 4.3 shader pipeline or lightmap handling for brush entities.
notes: Investigation: func_wall with MLS_ABSLIGHT (drawflags & 7 == 7) bypasses lightmaps entirely in R_RenderBrushPoly, using gl_shader_alias instead of gl_shader_world. Need to confirm if test maps set abslight on func_wall entities. Also: R_UpdateLightmaps only called from R_DrawWorld, not after R_DrawBrushModel — dynamic lights on brush entities delayed one frame.
close_reason: Fixed DrawGLPoly color override in v0.6.7 - MLS_ABSLIGHT intensity now applied. Remaining fullbright cases need map-specific testing.

title: [bug] Switching AA/anisotropy breaks textures until restart
description: Changing MSAA or anisotropic filtering in the Video/Display menu corrupts some textures. Likely the FBO or GL context needs to be recreated properly when these settings change. May need to flush and reload textures after AA/aniso changes.
close_reason: Fixed: Mod_ReloadTextures lost TEX_ALPHA/TEX_FENCE flags on fence textures during vid_restart. Also added GL_VBO/Shader shutdown before context destroy.

title: [epic] Unify sys_unix.c and sys_win.c — shared code into sys_sdl.c
description:
Move identical/near-identical functions from sys_unix.c and sys_win.c into shared sys_sdl.c. Strip platform files to only truly platform-specific code.

Subtasks:
1. Move Sys_SendKeyEvents to sys_sdl.c
2. Unify Sys_Sleep with SDL_Delay
3. Unify Sys_DoubleTime with SDL_GetTicksNS
4. Unify Sys_GetClipboardData with SDL_GetClipboardText
5. Unify Sys_DateTimeString with strftime
6. Remove all ifdef SDLQUAKE guards in sys_unix.c
7. Unify Sys_Error with platform-specific output branch
8. Unify Sys_PrintTerm with platform-specific output branch
9. Unify Sys_Quit with platform-specific cleanup
10. Unify main loop logic
close_reason: 6 functions unified into sys_sdl.c, ~220 lines of duplication removed. SDL_GetTicksNS, SDL_Delay, SDL_GetClipboardText, strftime, shared main loop. All SDLQUAKE ifdefs removed.

title: [epic] Clean up Windows legacy code after SDL3 migration
description:
sys_win.c and winquake.h still have dead Win32/DirectX/WGL code from before the SDL3 migration. Clean up all legacy Windows code that's no longer needed.

Subtasks:
1. Remove dead declarations from winquake.h (mainwindow, hwnd_dialog, dinput_init, DirectX version defines, CINTERFACE)
2. Remove deprecated GetVersionEx() OS version detection from sys_win.c
3. Delete dead wgl_func.h (WGL function pointers replaced by SDL3)
4. Remove or replace Win32 splash screen with SDL3
5. Replace Win32 clipboard API with SDL3 SDL_GetClipboardText/SDL_SetClipboardText
6. Replace timeGetTime()/timeBeginPeriod() with SDL3 timing
7. Review DPI awareness code vs SDL3 HiDPI handling
close_reason: Removed ~350 lines: wgl_func.h, GetVersionEx, splash screen, timeGetTime, Win32 clipboard, DirectX declares, DPI awareness. All replaced with SDL3 equivalents.

title: [epic] Remove all legacy GL capability detection (Ironwail approach)
description:
Strip all extension-string checking, capability detection, and fallback code paths. Assume GL 4.3 core as minimum — multitexture, anisotropy, NPOT, FBO, shaders are all guaranteed. No fallbacks.

Subtasks:
1. Remove CheckMultiTextureExtensions — always set gl_mtexable=true, load glActiveTexture
2. Remove CheckAnisotropyExtensions — just glGetFloatv(GL_MAX_TEXTURE_MAX_ANISOTROPY) 
3. Remove CheckNonPowerOfTwoTextures — always true
4. Remove CheckStencilBuffer — just check the SDL attribute
5. Remove CheckShaderSupport — always true (GL 4.3 guarantees it)
6. Remove GL_ParseExtensionList and gl_extensions string
7. Remove have_mtex, have_NPOT, have8bit, is8bit, gl_tex_NPOT variables
8. Remove all if(gl_mtexable) guards — always take multitexture path
9. Remove non-multitexture fallback code in gl_rsurf.c
10. Replace GL_MAX_TEXTURE_UNITS_ARB with GL_MAX_TEXTURE_IMAGE_UNITS
11. Remove old gl_alpha_test_program from gl_vidsdl.c (dead code)
12. Remove wgl_func.h (Windows GL 1.1 function pointers)
13. Clean up glheader.h — remove all manual GL constant defines
14. Switch to Core Profile context request
15. Add version check at startup: if GL < 4.3, print error and exit

Reference: Ironwail's GL_InitFunctions pattern — table-driven, core names only, hard fail.
close_reason: Stripped ~760 lines: all Check* functions, extension parsing, 3dfx detection, 8-bit palette, NPOT/multitexture/stencil capability variables and cvars, legacy GLSL 1.20 shader. Always-on multitexture, NPOT, stencil.

title: Decouple physics tick from render framerate (Ironwail approach)
description: Implement fixed-timestep accumulator pattern: server always ticks at sys_ticrate (20 Hz) regardless of render FPS. Renderer interpolates entity positions between physics ticks for smooth visuals at any framerate. Requires storing previous+current position per entity and lerping in the render loop. This is how Ironwail handles it — physics is deterministic, rendering is as smooth as the GPU allows.
close_reason: All components implemented: fixed-timestep physics accumulator (20Hz), Ironwail pendingcmd input accumulation, keyboard look at render rate, entity interpolation/extrapolation, mouse input at render rate, static entity jitter fix

title: Replace immediate mode GL with vertex buffers
description:
Major renderer rewrite: migrate from GL 1.x immediate mode to GL 4.3 core profile.

## Why
Required for: retro palettization/dithering, compute shader culling, bindless textures, proper MSAA via FBO, order-independent transparency, and all modern rendering features from Ironwail.

## Architecture (based on Ironwail analysis)

### Phase 1: VBO/VAO Foundation
- Replace all glBegin/glEnd with vertex buffer objects
- World geometry (BSP surfaces) → static VBOs built at map load
- Alias models → dynamic VBOs updated per-frame
- Particles → instanced point sprites or quad VBOs
- Sky → VBO quads
- 2D HUD/menu → orthographic VBO quad batch

### Phase 2: Shader Pipeline
- World shader: lightmap × diffuse, fog, dithering (GLSL 430)
- Alias model shader: per-vertex lighting, colormap
- Particle shader: point sprites or instanced quads
- Sky shader: scrolling layers or cubemap
- Postprocess shader: gamma, contrast, palettization, dithering
- Water shader: warp, alpha, lit water

### Phase 3: Modern GL Features
- GL 4.3 core context (remove compatibility profile)
- Uniform Buffer Objects for frame data (view/proj matrix, fog, time)
- Compute shader frustum culling (Ironwail approach)
- 3D palette LUT texture for retro palettization
- SSBO for light data
- Indirect multi-draw for world geometry
- Optional: bindless textures, persistent buffer mapping

### Phase 4: Advanced Effects
- Order-Independent Transparency (weighted blended OIT)
- MSAA via multisampled FBOs
- Retro softemu modes (fine dither, coarse dither, banded)
- Lightmapped liquid surfaces

## Reference
- Ironwail source at /home/josh/tmp/claudedir/ironwail/Quake/
- Key files: gl_shaders.h (all GLSL), gl_shaders.c (program management), gl_rmain.c (render pipeline), r_world.c (BSP rendering), r_alias.c (model rendering)
- Ironwail requires GL 4.3 minimum

## Effort Estimate
Very large — weeks of focused work. Should be done on a dedicated branch.
close_reason: GL 4.3 VBO/shader migration complete — zero immediate mode, zero hardware matrix stack

title: PimpModel dynamic light lingers after projectile despawns
description: When firing the wand, a blue orb of PimpModel light stays behind for a moment at the impact point after the projectile is destroyed. The per-entity pimp override persists in the table after the entity is freed/reused. Need to clear pimp overrides when entities are removed or recycled.

title: Default r_lerpmodels to off

title: SinglePlayer menu: relabel Portals items when a custom mod is running
description: When a mod (GAME_PORTALS set but gamedir != portals/data1) is running, the SinglePlayer menu shows Portals-specific labels (NEW MISSION/OLD MISSION/PORTALS INTRO) that confuse mod users. Both NEW and OLD launch the mod's startmap cvar; NEW also plays intermission #12 first. Relabel for mods: NEW MISSION->START W/ INTRO, OLD MISSION->START, PORTALS INTRO->MOD INTRO. Labels only, no behavior change.

title: Modder drop-in nolerp.txt: per-gamedir no-interp model list
description: User reported lerp making a SoT waterfall look weird. Rather than hardcode model names, add a modder-facing drop-in: an optional 'nolerp.txt' in the gamedir listing model paths (one per line, // and # comments ok) that bypass animation lerp, merged with the built-in r_nolerp_list. Implemented in gl_model.c (Mod_LoadUserNolerpList + Mod_SetExtraFlags merge; cache invalidated in Mod_ClearAll on gamedir change). Note: SoT waterfallh/t/_90/fountain are 1-frame, so vertex lerp doesn't affect them — the user should list the actual multi-frame model that morphs.
close_reason: Reverted per user. nolerp.txt drop-in feature backed out; gl_model.c restored to HEAD.

title: Instanced alias path reads stale lightcolor[] when gl_lightmap_format=GL_LUMINANCE
description:
Found while exploring uhexen2-ayrn during the khsa lighting hunt. Not a likely root cause for khsa itself (Mathuzzz's r_alias_gpu=0 toggle doesn't help, which rules this branch out for his setup), but a real latent bug for any user who runs with gl_lightmapfmt GL_LUMINANCE or -lm_1.

SHAPE:

R_LightPoint at gl_rlight.c:517 (the non-RGBA / GL_LUMINANCE sampler) returns a scalar `ambientlight` but does NOT populate the global `lightcolor[]`. Only R_LightPointColor (gl_rlight.c:639, GL_RGBA path) populates it.

AliasModelGetLightInfo at gl_rmain.c:1119-1129 dispatches on gl_lightmap_format and calls one or the other. So on the LUMINANCE branch, lightcolor[] holds either the initial zero or whatever the last RGBA-branch entity wrote — stale either way.

The legacy alias draw at gl_rmain.c:766-781 handles this correctly: its LUMINANCE branch ignores lightcolor[] and uses `colorshade_tint * shadedots * shadelight`.

The instanced-collector path at gl_rmain.c:2377-2403 does NOT — even in its non-RGBA branch it does:
    inst->light_color[0] = lightcolor[0] * shadelight;   (or * RTint[cs] * shadelight when colorshaded)
    ...same for 1 and 2

So under GL_LUMINANCE + r_alias_gpu=1 (default), every alias entity is uploaded to the SSBO with light_color = stale × shadelight ≈ 0 → models render fully black through the instanced path.

REPRO:
1. Boot with `gl_lightmapfmt GL_LUMINANCE` or `-lm_1`.
2. Load any map with alias entities.
3. r_alias_gpu=1 (default): all alias entities black or near-black.
4. r_alias_gpu=0: legacy path renders correctly because that branch doesn't read lightcolor[].

FIX OPTIONS:

A. Mirror the legacy branch shape in R_CollectAliasInstance — drop lightcolor[] from the LUMINANCE path:
       inst->light_color[i] = colorshade_tint_i * shadelight;
   (Verify per-channel tint behavior — legacy uses tmp_color[vi][i] = r*l where r,g,b are RTint/GTint/BTint and l = shadedots * shadelight.)

B. Always populate lightcolor[] in AliasModelGetLightInfo by routing R_LightPoint through a helper that also fills lightcolor[i] = ambient (mirror the RGBA path's semantics). Single-channel scalar broadcast to RGB.

(B) is cleaner — gives both paths a consistent input. (A) is faster to ship and matches legacy behavior exactly.

Not blocking khsa. File and move on; address after khsa lands.
