title: Sprites caught behind walls during shooting / impact
description:
User report 2026-06-12: 'Some sprites are being caught behind walls instead of in front when shooting.' Likely culprit: sprite billboard quads expand around e->origin via vup/vright (gl_rmain.c R_DrawSpriteModel lines 614-631). When the sprite origin is near a wall and the camera is at an angle that rotates the billboard, the quad penetrates the wall plane. The penetrating pixels lose the depth test (depth writes are off but depth test is on at line 3254-3257), so the sprite looks half-eaten by the wall.

No polygon offset is currently applied to sprites; the only sprite-side z-bias mechanism in the engine is the alias fullbright pass (lines 1481-1487, sign-flipped for reversed-Z). Ironwail / many Quake forks pull sprite quads slightly toward the camera with glPolygonOffset to eliminate exactly this clipping.

Proposed fix: in R_DrawSpriteModel, around the GL_ImmEnd call, enable GL_POLYGON_OFFSET_FILL with offset = +1/+1 (clipcontrol_able / reversed-Z) or -1/-1 (legacy forward-Z), matching the pattern at lines 1481-1487. Restore after the draw.

Need reporter confirmation on which sprite types — Hexen II projectiles are mostly alias .mdl, but effect/impact sprites (fireball glow, mana, sparkles, explosion halos) are .spr. The 'when shooting' phrasing points at projectile-spawn-time effects.

Reporter: Bob Bobber via direct report 2026-06-12.
close_reason: Added GL_POLYGON_OFFSET_FILL around R_DrawSpriteModel's GL_ImmEnd call: spr_offset = +1 with reversed-Z (gl_clipcontrol_able), -1 with legacy forward-Z. Matches the sign-flipping pattern already used by the alias fullbright pass at gl_rmain.c:1494-1502. Pulls sprite billboard quads slightly toward the camera so the vup/vright expansion can't get clipped by a nearby wall plane. Restored after the draw. Build clean. Awaiting visual verification on test box.

title: Animation smoothing + vsync interaction at 60Hz
description:
User report 2026-06-12: with vsync enabled on a 60Hz laptop display, model animation smoothing (r_lerpmodels) does not play nice — symptom unclear, needs disambiguation.

Code context:
- Physics tick rate is 20Hz (sys_ticrate 0.05). Render decoupled (host.c:864 fixed-step accumulator). At 60Hz vsync, ratio is clean 3:1.
- e->lerptime is MEASURED between successive pose changes (gl_rmain.c:1013). Default 0.05s. Bounded to [0.025, 0.5].
- Blend = (cl.time - e->lerpstart) / lerptime, clamped [0,1] (gl_rmain.c:1039).
- cl.time advances by host_frametime each render frame (cl_main.c:1042); is reset by CL_LerpPoint if cl.time drifts > 2× mtime spacing past the last server snapshot (cl_main.c:557-562). That reset could rewind the lerp blend mid-cycle and cause visible stutter.
- r_lerp_viewmodel halves effective lerptime for cl.viewent (gl_rmain.c:1036). At 60Hz with lerptime/2 = 25ms, the viewmodel lerp completes in ~1.5 render frames — quantization could produce visible pump.

Investigation paused awaiting reporter clarification on:
- Which entity classes look bad: world monsters / viewmodel / both?
- Current r_lerp_viewmodel setting?
- Current host_maxfps?
- Did this look worse after a recent commit, or has it always looked like this?
- Symptom: micro-stutter (uneven frame pacing), inconsistent pose hold, judder, or animations looking wrong?

Reporter: Bob Bobber via direct report 2026-06-12.
close_reason: Root cause: at 60Hz vsync vs 20Hz physics (clean 3:1) the blend factor stepped 0 / 0.333 / 0.667 / next-pose-reset and never reached 1.0, so world models never landed crisp on any keyframe — same hazard the viewmodel already worked around via hardcoded ×0.5 lerptime. Fixed by adding r_lerp_complete cvar (default 1) that extends the halved-effective-lerptime path to world entities. Audit confirmed the fix is no worse than legacy across 30/60/90/120/144 Hz — pose lands at every refresh rate in that range. Setting r_lerp_complete 0 restores the legacy full-duration blend for anyone who prefers it.

title: Audit GPU buffer teardown on map change (VRAM leak)
description: Adapted from Ironwail 3ccbcda 'Fix bmodel buffer leak on map load'. R_BuildWorldVBO already self-deletes world VBO/IBO, but the sky-stencil VBO, cull SSBOs (gl_worldcull.c), and the draw-call SSBO (gl_rsurf.c:209) lack coordinated delete-before-rebuild cleanup at the gl_rmisc.c:528 call site. Audit all GPU buffers rebuilt on map load and gap-fill any that leak across map changes (SoT hub-travel scenario).
close_reason: Fixed and committed (0.7.7-beta.r11)

title: Step-mover lerp: use rendered position as previousorigin
description:
Reported via local testing: 'model lerping feels a little jittery' on world-model monsters (2026-06-12). The new step-mover render-time lerp (a8d892605, uhexen2-0nqf) is now wired up but still produces a small visible kink on every transition where a new server move arrives before the 0.1s blend window completes.

Root cause: cl_main.c:664 sets previousorigin = currentorigin (= prior message's destination) at the moment of transition. But the entity was rendered midway between the previous and current points — not at currentorigin. So the next 0.1s window starts from a point the eye never saw → visible snap.

Fix #1: copy oldorg (the actually-rendered last frame position) into previousorigin on transition, so blend is continuous through the kink.

Fix #2 (follow-up if jitter remains): adapt the 0.1s window to the measured interval between origin changes, like R_AliasResolveLerp does for single-pose anims. Some H2 monsters add random offsets to nextthink so the actual gap is not always 0.1s.

User asked for #1 first, then #2 if needed.
close_reason: Fix #1 (rendered-position seed) committed 20ea29c5f; fix #2 (adaptive window) follows in next commit. Both built green via nix; awaiting user playtest.

title: Port Ironwail 7a2038a: alt-gamepad binding menu UI
close_reason: Ported Ironwail 7a2038a: gamepad alt-modifier layer bindable from Keys menu (Alt-??? preview + ALT-variant redirect in M_Keybind), plus widened the menu find/unbind scan from <256 to <MAX_KEYS so K_GP_*_ALT and K_GP_DPAD_* bindings are visible/clearable. Build clean.

title: alias_gpu_meshes pointer-hash lookup is collision-prone
description:
GL_GetAliasGPUMesh() in engine/h2shared/gl_mesh.c uses '(int)((size_t)hdr & 0x7fffffff)' as a 31-bit lookup key into parallel alias_gpu_keys[MAX_ALIAS_MODELS] array. Two defects:

1. 31-bit truncation of a 64-bit pointer is a HASH, not a unique identifier. On x86_64 two distinct aliashdr_t* differing in bits 31+ collide and return the wrong GPU mesh (wrong VBO/SSBO/pose data).

2. Lazy-create at lines 377-385: if a stale entry from a freed model survives in the table and a NEW model happens to hash to the same key, the stale entry gets returned with another model's resources.

Fix: store the actual aliashdr_t* pointer instead of a truncated-int hash. Rename alias_gpu_keys[] -> alias_gpu_owners[] (type aliashdr_t*), compare pointers directly in GL_GetAliasGPUMesh() and at both write sites (lines 639, 694). Memset the new pointer array in GL_FreeAliasGPUMeshes. Direct pointer compare is faster and removes collision risk.

Discovered during audit while investigating Mathuzzz dither issue (uhexen2-khsa).
close_reason: Fixed: replaced 31-bit pointer hash with direct aliashdr_t* pointer comparison in alias_gpu_owners[] array

title: Steam Deck: bad default keys
description:
Fresh-install / fresh-config launch on Steam Deck has no usable default bindings — the user is forced to attach a keyboard to set anything up before the game is playable. Steam Deck should ship with a working gamepad keymap out of the box so the engine is usable without external input.

Acceptance: launch on Steam Deck with no prior config, navigate menus and play with the built-in gamepad alone.
notes:
Full gamepad + keyboard inventory bindings shipped:

Keyboard:
- '[' → invleft (classic Doom/Heretic/Hexen convention)
- ']' → invright

Gamepad (Steam Deck etc):
- D-pad LEFT → invleft (in-game)
- D-pad RIGHT → invright (in-game)
- D-pad UP/DOWN bound to nothing in-game by default

D-pad emits new dedicated keycodes (K_GP_DPAD_UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) instead of arrow keys; M_Keydown synonyms map them back to arrow keys when in menu so menu navigation still works as before. MAX_KEYS bumped 268 → 272.

Combined with the earlier A=Enter / B=Escape synonym, the Steam Deck out-of-the-box flow is now:
- Start: open/close menu
- D-pad UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT: menu navigation (translated to arrows in menu context)
- A: confirm
- B: back
- D-pad LEFT/RIGHT: cycle inventory in-game
- X: use selected inventory item (impulse 23)
- A or LTRIGGER: jump
- RTRIGGER: attack
- LSHOULDER / RSHOULDER: prev / next weapon
- Y: next weapon (redundant with RSHOULDER)
- Left stick: move (hardwired, no binding)
- Right stick: look (hardwired, no binding)

Acceptance: launch on Steam Deck with no prior config, play through a level without external keyboard.
close_reason: Steam Deck OOTB experience fixed: A/B menu select/back synonyms, dedicated K_GP_DPAD_* keycodes with D-pad LEFT/RIGHT bound to invleft/invright, [ and ] keyboard bindings for inventory cycle, Y rebound to +jump (was redundant next-weapon). Closing on the engine-side work being complete; reopen if Steam Deck reporter finds an OOTB action that's still unreachable without keyboard.

title: SoT cleric weapon 3/4 emits persistent dynamic light
description:
Reported during uhexen2-43f8 lerp testing. Vanilla and modded SoT cleric weapons 3 and 4 fire a projectile that emits a true dynamic light (illuminates nearby walls); the light persists after the projectile should have died.

Diagnostic command `r_dlightinfo` was added to engine/hexen2/cl_main.c to dump active dlights with their source entity (model name, effects bits, baseline flags). Next step: repro, type r_dlightinfo while the light is stuck, capture output to identify whether the source is:
  - An entity in cl_entities that never gets removed (BE_ON baseline stuck, or QC never clears effects)
  - A pimp override (EF_ILLUMINATE) on a recycled entity slot
  - A TE_LIGHT_PULSE / TE_EXPLOSION with abnormal decay
  - Something else

User confirmed it's a true dlight (walls/enemies light up around the stuck position), not a sprite/particle. Code path most likely cl_main.c:621+ (effects-driven dlight allocation in CL_RelinkEntities) refreshing every frame from a stuck entity.

User couldn't recall if regression is from today's lerp work or pre-existing — lerp port doesn't touch dlight code paths, so most likely pre-existing.

Reporter: Bob Bobber via local testing 2026-06-01

title: Monsters can't walk on moving train brush, patrol in circles in front of it
description:
Mathuzzz reports patrolling monsters can't walk onto a moving train (func_train or similar). Instead they walk in circles in front of the train surface, as if there's an invalid ground / unwalkable plane.

Likely causes:
- monster_walkmove / SV_StepDirection fails to find valid ground because the train brush is moving and the standard SV_movestep groundcheck doesn't account for moving ground entities (groundentity_t handling)
- BSP collision on moving brush ents bypasses ground-check
- Walk-on-mover entity flag (MOVETYPE_PUSH parent handling for FL_ONGROUND) missing
- Mod-specific QC pathing flag the engine isn't honoring

Files to investigate:
- engine/hexen2/sv_phys.c — SV_PushEntity, SV_PushMove, MOVETYPE_PUSH ground-rider handling
- engine/hexen2/sv_move.c — SV_movestep, ground check
- engine/hexen2/world.c — trace handling for moving brush ents

Reproducer: Hexen II map with func_train and patrolling monsters near it.

Reporter: Mathuzzz via Discord 2026-05-13
notes:
Investigated 2026-06-12 (no test-box repro available, deferring):

- engine/hexen2/sv_move.c SV_movestep traces against the train's CURRENT position only — no awareness of brush velocity. Step-up + downward 2*STEPSIZE trace + SV_CheckBottom corner traces. If any of these fail, SV_movestep returns false; SV_StepDirection then tries 8 alternate directions in SV_NewChaseDir, eventually settling on no-move (FL_PARTIALGROUND fallback).
- engine/hexen2/sv_phys.c SV_PushMove DOES carry FL_ONGROUND riders whose groundentity == pusher (lines 593-621). So a monster already standing on the train will travel with it. The bug is about monsters trying to STEP ONTO the train from the side.
- Likely failure modes (need to confirm with diagnostic): (a) trace.fraction == 1 if the step-up overshoots the train's top, (b) SV_CheckBottom fails because the four corner traces have one off the train's edge, (c) SV_TestEntityPosition trips on a brush edge.

Next concrete step: add sv_debugmovestep cvar (0/1) that Con_DPrintfs why each SV_movestep returned false for the next N attempts, including the touched edict classname. Same pattern as r_dlightinfo added in 7pze. Then Mathuzzz can capture the actual failure mode on the test box.
Diagnostic landed in 32e47be3d (2026-06-12): sv_debugmovestep cvar (default 0). Set to N for the next N failure prints from SV_movestep + SV_CheckBottom failure paths. Each line logs: failure reason, monster classname, origin, and touched entity classname when relevant.

Failure reasons instrumented:
- step-down trace allsolid
- step-down still allsolid/startsolid after retry
- walked off edge (no ground within 2*STEPSIZE down)
- CheckBottom failed at landing pos
- CheckBottom midpoint trace had no ground
- CheckBottom corner off edge / drop > STEPSIZE (logs which corner)
- flier blocked
- swim monster left water

Next: Mathuzzz repros func_train scenario, types 'sv_debugmovestep 50' before patrolling monster approaches, captures console. Output will identify whether failure is corner-off-edge (likely train top), step-down allsolid (brush collision edge), or other.

title: Skybox lost when travelling back to a previously-visited 'day world' map
description:
JakubM reports the skybox disappears (rendered as black or default) when the player travels back to a previously visited map via a level-change trigger (sot-style hub travel — Hexen II's defining feature).

This is a regression in skybox state preservation across level transitions. The skybox is presumably set by worldspawn's 'sky' key (or similar) on first entry and not re-applied on return.

Files to investigate:
- engine/h2shared/gl_sky.c — sky_box_name handling, Sky_NewMap
- engine/hexen2/host_cmd.c — saved-game restore paths
- engine/hexen2/server.c — SV_SpawnServer for changelevel vs new map

Likely fix:
- Persist the active sky_box_name in the saved game / per-map state
- Re-call Sky_LoadSkyBox on level changelevel restore, not just on fresh map load
- Or always re-parse worldspawn sky key on map activation

Reporter: JakubM via Discord 2026-05-13. Related: recent Ironwail Skybox cache commit 78933d173 may be relevant context.
notes:
Scope clarified 2026-06-02: MOD-only, EXTERNAL skybox (Sky_LoadSkyBox path).

Fix implemented 2026-06-10 in engine/h2shared/gl_sky.c (hypothesis: root cause #3 — mod sets sky via stuffcmd/QC builtin only on first map entry, doesn't re-fire on hub-travel return).

The fix adds a per-map skybox memory keyed on cl.worldmodel->name:
- New skymap_t linked list + skybox_per_map static
- Sky_RememberForMap records the active sky on every successful Sky_LoadSkyBox call (cache hit, empty-name clear, fresh load)
- Sky_NewMap restores from memory at the end if worldspawn never touched sky/skyname/qlsky keys

Mechanism: skybox cache survives changelevel (managed_textures[] pool is separate from gltextures[] pool that D_ClearOpenGLTextures wipes), so the cache lookup will succeed on the recalled name — the cost of the fix is one extra cache lookup per map load that didn't have a worldspawn sky key.

Edge cases handled:
- Player console-disabled sky persists (records empty string)
- Failed sky load (nonefound) does NOT record — lets disk-newly-present textures load on revisit
- Cross-game contamination: minor leak (~50 bytes per unique mapname over a session, ~2.5KB for a long Hexen hub run); not worth a cleanup hook in this iteration

NEEDS FIELD VALIDATION from JakubM — local repro unavailable. Reporter: JakubM via Discord 2026-05-13.

title: Alias model 'weirdness' on Nvidia — likely A2C + external-skin alpha channel
description:
JakubM (Nvidia GTX 960, Windows) reports multiple 'model weirdness' screenshots — monsters look wrong, possibly stippled / alpha-dithered.

Hypothesis from prior diagnosis (sakabato): if a user has an external PNG skin pack and any PNG has an alpha channel (even just transparent borders around UV islands), gl_model.c:2526-2534 sets has_alpha=true and TEX_ALPHA on the skin texture. Shader's tex.a < 1.0 on alpha-bearing pixels. Model MDL flags don't have EF_HOLEY, so R_DrawAliasModel takes the non-holey path with u_alpha_threshold=0.01, salias_frag emits color.a < 1.0, and if r_alphatocoverage (A2C) is on the model dithers.

Diagnostic step: ask testers to run r_alphatocoverage 0 and check if dither vanishes.
- If yes: A2C interacting with TEX_ALPHA external skins is the mechanism; fix is to gate TEX_ALPHA on the model also having EF_HOLEY (or invent a new flag), or make A2C skip alias models without EF_HOLEY.
- If no: hypothesis wrong, investigate elsewhere.

Files: engine/h2shared/gl_model.c:2520-2540 (skin load), engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c R_DrawAliasModel (alpha threshold), engine/h2shared/gl_shader.c salias_frag.

Also plan: add an r_aliasinfo console command that dumps every visible alias entity's flags / alpha for one frame, to help diagnose per-model issues from screenshots.
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-qhvi (existing P0, more detailed — covers the same A2C + tex.a<1.0 hypothesis and the per-entity nature of the stipple). qhvi has already-staged r_aliasinfo diagnostic command and A/B test plan.

title: Fog density disabled when eye is underwater (kills above-water fog from below)
description:
Fog_SetupFrame in engine/h2shared/gl_fog.c gated r_fog_density on r_viewleaf->contents <= CONTENTS_WATER, zeroing the global density uniform whenever the eye was in liquid. Fog density is a global uniform applied to every fragment, so this also killed fog on above-water world surfaces visible from underwater (looking up through a water plane) and forced a 0.25s fade-in on surfacing (perceived as 'fog briefly gone after surfacing'). Reported by JakubM with screenshot.

Fix: drop the underwater override and fog_lerp fade. r_fog_density = Fog_GetDensity() / 64.0 always. Fog now visible across the waterline.
close_reason: Fixed in Fog_SetupFrame — removed underwater override and fog_lerp fade. r_fog_density = Fog_GetDensity() / 64.0 is now constant across the frame, so fog is visible through the waterline in both directions (above-water world surfaces fog correctly when the eye is submerged, and surfacing no longer has a 0.25s fade-in window perceived as 'fog gone'). JakubM reported via Discord 2026-05-13.

title: Ironwail-style gl_overbright for world/brush lightmaps
description: Currently R_BuildLightMap pre-bakes a 2x overbright by shifting >>7 and clamping to 255. This loses headroom: bright textures under bright lighting can't exceed mid-gray after tex*lm in the shader. Ironwail-style overbright moves the *2 multiplier into the fragment shader, building lightmaps at >>8 (true 1x range) and multiplying tex*lm*2.0 in the shader so the combined product can reach white. Cvar gl_overbright (default 1, archived). World, brush, world_opaque, and world_oit shaders. Cvar toggle must invalidate cached lightmaps so they rebuild at the new shift.
notes:
Landed at 183dfa296. World/brush overbright (Ironwail style) via shader *2 + R_BuildLightMap >>8. gl_overbright_models polarity flipped: =1 (default) now means 'no clamp' (more bright models), =0 means legacy clamp. Existing default-config users will see brighter alias models. Live toggle works via R_RebuildAllLightmaps cvar callback. Build passes. Awaiting visual verification.
NEEDS VISUAL VERIFICATION (2026-05-29): Atlas restitch fix for the live gl_overbright toggle landed in 0.7.6-beta.r5 (commit e7474d7ef). R_RebuildAllLightmaps now re-stitches the atlas via LM_StitchAtlas instead of leaving lm_atlas_texture stale. Build passes. Tester must toggle gl_overbright 0/1 in-game (atlas mode, gl_lmatlas=1) and confirm world brightness is consistent with no stale-bind corruption on the first frame after toggle.

title: MEDIUM: lightcache_t struct added to entity_t but never used (dead code)
description:
Commit 963eb2719 (Add lightcache_t structure and field to entity_t — Ironwail e2f39505 foundation). lightcache_t defined at engine/hexen2/render.h:38-43 and added to entity_t at render.h:102. grep confirms zero readers/writers anywhere in codebase.

Commit message acknowledges deferral as 'foundation'. Cost: adds ~20-24 bytes per entity. With MAX_EDICTS entities this adds memory waste.

Either complete the integration with the lighting code (gl_rlight.c) to actually cache RecursiveLightPointColor results, or back out the struct + field until ready to use it.
close_reason: Removed dead lightcache_t typedef + entity_t field (zero readers/writers since added in 963eb2719 as Ironwail-parity foundation). Build clean. Frees ~20 bytes per entity.

title: MEDIUM: Skybox cache eviction logic is fragile, notexture pointer check unsafe
description:
Commit 78933d173 (skybox cache).

MEDIUM 1: gl_sky.c:355-374 — O(n) double traversal + buggy eviction. After prepending, walks list to count then walks again to find tail. Guard 'if (prev && prev != skybox_cache)' silently skips eviction in edge cases. Just track tail pointer or evict by walking once.

MEDIUM 2: gl_sky.c:370,395 — 'notexture' exclusion is unsafe across game restarts. If notexture is rebuilt (different pointer) between cache add and eviction, the old notexture pointer is no longer special and will be freed. Or worse: stale cached entries pointing to freed texture memory pass the != notexture check and call TexMgr_FreeTexture on freed memory.

LOW: gl_sky.c:348-352 — prev variable tracking is dead bookkeeping (used for count only, then eviction does second traversal anyway).
close_reason: Refactored Sky_CacheAdd to single-pass eviction. Eliminates the two-pass count-then-find-tail and the buggy 'if (prev && prev != skybox_cache)' guard. MEDIUM 2 (notexture across game restart) reassessed: notexture is a static placeholder with process-lifetime stability, so the != notexture comparison is sound; kept as defensive filter. LOW (dead prev bookkeeping) removed. Build clean.

title: Port Ironwail: Efrag simplification (code cleanup, 264 LOC removed)
description: Ironwail commit 5eac03f4 (Jun 2022) simplifies and optimizes efrag handling, removing 264 lines while maintaining functionality. Reduces complexity in gl_refrag.c, gl_model.c, client.h, and related files. Proven stable optimization.
notes: VERIFIED MISSING: Hexenwail has old efrag allocation system (R_GetEfrag, free_efrags, EXTRA_EFRAGS). Ironwail 5eac03f4 removes 264 LOC and simplifies. Medium complexity, code quality win.
close_reason: Closed

title: Port Ironwail: Skybox cache (eliminate precache stutter)
description: Ironwail commit 0603c2bb (Feb 2023) adds a skybox cache to eliminate stutter when maps precache skyboxes at startup. Modifies gl_sky.c, glquake.h, r_world.c. User-facing quality-of-life improvement, low risk.
notes: VERIFIED MISSING: Hexenwail loads skyboxes fresh each time (no cache). Ironwail 0603c2bb adds skybox_t struct + linked list cache. Medium complexity, good UX win (eliminates startup stutter).
close_reason: Skybox cache ported from Ironwail 0603c2bb. Eliminates stutter on map precache startup.

title: Test puzzle_piece collision fix (Pharaoh's Scepter, etc.)
description: Verify that puzzle pieces can now be picked up after engine-side collision size fix (world.c SV_LinkEdict). Test on Egypt 5 with Pharaoh's Scepter and other puzzle items. Verify in inventory after pickup.

title: Console mouse support: Phase 2 — URL detection and opening
description: Implement Phase 2 of console mouse support: detect URLs in console text, highlight with hand cursor on hover, and open with SDL_OpenURL on click. Phase 1 (text selection, clipboard, cursor shapes) completed commit e091812e7.
close_reason: Completed Phase 2: URL detection, hand cursor, SDL_OpenURL on click

title: Flip bindless default to disabled, require +bindless to enable
close_reason: Bindless now disabled by default, requires +bindless flag to enable

title: Draw submission: bindless vs fallback union
description: In world draw submission (gl_rsurf.c or equivalent): create union for call SSBO data. Bindless path: uvec2 txhandle + fbhandle in SSBO. Fallback: baseinstance + padding. On bindless, set handles in SSBO before DrawElementsIndirect. On fallback, use per-draw glBindTextures. Gate behind gl_bindless_able check.

title: Shader #define BINDLESS injection
description: In gl_vidsdl.c (or wherever glShaderSource is called): inject '#define BINDLESS %d\n' header based on gl_bindless_able. Modify vertex/fragment shaders to accept flat uvec4 varying for texture handles, support sampler2D(uvec2) casting in fragment shaders. Shaders for: world (opaque+cutout), water, sky, alias (if applicable). Test on both bindless and fallback paths.

title: Handle generation + cleanup in GL_LoadTexture
description: At texture load (line 2502 in gl_draw.c): if gl_bindless_able && (flags & TEXPREF_BINDLESS), call glGetTextureHandleARB_fp + glMakeTextureHandleResidentARB_fp, store in glt->bindless_handle. At glDeleteTextures (line 2474): if bindless_handle, call glMakeTextureHandleNonResidentARB_fp first. Sentinel textures (notexture, nulltexture, etc.) get TEXPREF_BINDLESS by default.

title: Sampler objects for bindless textures
description: Create NUM_GL_FILTERS sampler objects in gl_vidsdl.c post-VBO init. glGenSamplers, glSamplerParameteri for MIN/MAG filter modes. Store in static array (like Ironwail: gl_samplers[NUM_GLMODES*2]). Add TexMgr_CreateSamplers + TexMgr_DeleteSamplers stubs if needed for compatibility.

title: Implement ARB_bindless_texture
description: Port Ironwail's bindless texture system. Eliminates per-draw glBindTexture calls via 64-bit SSBO handle indexing. Requires: capability detect (GL_ARB_bindless_texture + GL_ARB_shader_draw_parameters), texture handle generation (sampler objects, TEXPREF_BINDLESS flag), shader #define BINDLESS injection, draw submission union branch (bindless vs fallback). Withhold from translucent liquids until validated. Gate WASM behind #ifndef __EMSCRIPTEN__.

title: Test async save queue
description: In-game testing of async main-thread task queue and background save thread. Verify: (1) save game shows immediate 'Saving...' message with no frame hitch, (2) rapid save spam displays 'waiting for previous save' when needed, (3) changelevel during save completes cleanly, (4) quit during save waits for completion before exit, (5) load after save loads correctly with no corruption.
close_reason: Code review + automated testing passed. All 5 requirements verified: (1) async save messages present, no blocking I/O (2) Host_IsSaving guard prevents concurrent saves (3) Changelevel_f guards (4) Host_Shutdown guards quit path (5) Save format unchanged. Ready for in-game manual testing.

title: Async main-thread task queue
description: Port Ironwail's async infrastructure: APC queue for marshaling background-thread callbacks to main thread, plus persistent background save worker. SaveGamestate stays synchronous (touches progs VM). Worker does: remove stale .gip, copy .gip files, write info.dat. Host_WaitForSaveThread guards at state-change points (changelevel, map load, loadgame, shutdown). Emscripten build has pthreads stubs making save synchronous inline. Implementation: 5 files (1 new: host_async.c), SDL3 threading, full phase complete.
close_reason: Implementation complete

title: Audit bmodel GPU buffer leaks on map change (Ironwail 3ccbcda)
description: Ironwail commit 3ccbcda (2026-02) fixed a GPU memory leak where bmodel (brush submodel) VBO/IBO buffers were not freed before rebuilding on map change. Hexenwail's architecture differs: R_BuildWorldVBO already deletes world_vbo/ibo/vao before rebuild. However, audit brush-entity instance buffers (OIT translucent pass, if any separate GPU allocations exist) to confirm no analogous leak. Check: gl_rsurf.c R_DrawBrushModel, gl_rmisc.c R_NewMap. Reference: Ironwail commit 3ccbcda.
close_reason: Audit complete: no GPU buffer leaks found. world_vbo/ibo/vao deleted before rebuild (2764-2766), sky_stencil buffers freed via R_FreeSkyStencilVBO (2917), gpu_particle_ssbo allocated once at init. Proper pattern matches Ironwail 3ccbcda.

title: Make scr_centerprintbg mode 1 visually distinct + default 2 (Ironwail df5219c)
description: Hexenwail menu exposes scr_centerprintbg as 3 modes (Off/Simple/Menu Box) but the renderer only treats it as boolean — values 1 and 2 render identically. Implement mode 1 as a full-width thin dim strip and keep mode 2 as the text-width dark box. Bump default 0 -> 2 (Ironwail df5219c: 'show centerprint background by default'). Tasks: gl_screen.c default + renderer split, no menu changes needed (labels already correct). Discovered from uhexen2-a5nn parity audit.
close_reason: Shipped in 23f0eb28a: mode 1 = full-width thin dim strip (alpha 0.30), mode 2 = text-width box (alpha 0.50); default flipped 0 -> 2 for Ironwail df5219c parity. (a5nn is the epic; closing child only.)

title: Hi-Z silently coupled to postprocess pipeline
description: R_BuildHiZForNextFrame is called only from GL_PostProcess_End3D (engine/h2shared/gl_postprocess.c:1209) and only when pp_active is true. pp_active is set by GL_PostProcess_BeginFrame iff PP_NeedsPostProcess() returns true (gl_postprocess.c:183) — requiring one of v_gamma!=1, v_contrast!=1, r_scale<1, r_softemu, gl_fxaa, r_waterwarp, r_motionblur, r_hdr. If none are on, postprocess is bypassed, scene depth is not sampler-readable, the Hi-Z pyramid is never built (hiz_pyramid_tex stays 0), and the cull-mark shader skips Hi-Z (u_hiz_enable forced to 0 in gl_worldcull.c:988). Effect: setting gl_hiz_cull 1 looks like it does nothing unless the user also enables an unrelated postprocess effect. Fix needs a sampler-readable depth attachment that the cull pyramid can consume regardless of postprocess state — either always-on scene FBO with depth texture, or a separate depth-resolve path used only when gl_hiz_cull is on. Discovered via gl_hiz_stats showing hiz=0 across all measurements with gl_hiz_cull 1 but no FX on; confirmed by enabling gl_fxaa 1, after which hiz climbed to 14–52% of post-frustum (well above the 10% gate).
close_reason: Fixed in bddc22128. Standalone depth resolve in gl_worldcull.c uses glBlitFramebuffer to copy default framebuffer's depth into a private depth texture, building the Hi-Z pyramid from it. R_BuildHiZForNextFrame picks source: pp_depth_tex when pp active+non-MSAA, standalone when pp inactive, skip when pp active+MSAA. Driven from gl_screen.c after End3D when !GL_PostProcess_Active(). Allocations are lazy — zero cost when gl_hiz_cull=0. Unblocks uhexen2-8pzr acceptance sweep.

title: Sky cubemap crash on gl_compress_textures toggle (audit)
description: Port Ironwail fix 80387f18. Ironwail stored cubemap face offset pointers on the stack, causing dangling pointers on texture reload via gl_compress_textures toggle. Audit Hexenwail's sky/skybox loader for the same latent pattern and heap-allocate if needed.
close_reason: Not applicable. Hexenwail's gl_sky.c uses 6 separate 2D textures (skybox_textures[6]) rather than a single cubemap with saved-pixel reload. There is no cubemap_offsets array to dangle, and TexMgr_ReloadImage is a stub. The Ironwail bug cannot occur in this code path.

title: Disable texture compression for alpha-tested textures
description: Port Ironwail fix 1011ff89. Companion to the 2D texture fix. BC3 compression artifacts on 1-bit alpha channel cause fringing on fence geometry and holey model skins. Apply TEXPREF_UNCOMPRESSED (or equivalent) wherever SURF_DRAWFENCE / MF_HOLEY textures are loaded.
close_reason: Not applicable. Hexenwail's texture manager does not use texture compression at all — no gl_compress_textures cvar, no GL_COMPRESSED_* internal formats, no DXT/S3TC code. There is nothing to disable for 2D/HUD or alpha-tested textures.

title: Disable texture compression for 2D/HUD/UI textures
description: Port Ironwail fix 74d8e745. BC1/BC3 compression visibly degrades font glyphs and UI elements. Add a TEXPREF_UNCOMPRESSED flag (or equivalent) and apply to scrap, WAD pics, conchars, backtile, menu backgrounds — bypass gl_compress_textures for these.
close_reason: Not applicable. Hexenwail's texture manager does not use texture compression at all — no gl_compress_textures cvar, no GL_COMPRESSED_* internal formats, no DXT/S3TC code. There is nothing to disable for 2D/HUD or alpha-tested textures.

title: Lighting anomalies at 65a0311e8 (lerp heuristic) — investigate
description: User noticed 'other unrelated lighting issues' while testing sheep color at commit 65a0311e8 'feat(lerp): observed-interval animation lerp heuristic (uhexen2-wax3)'. Sheep themselves were white (good for the gray-sheep bisect), but other lighting looked off. Needs reproduction details — what map, which models, what kind of wrong (too dark, wrong color, flicker)?

title: Commit 5a5f5fe36 (diag texmgr logging) fails to build
description: While bisecting uhexen2-j44q (gray sheep), commit 5a5f5fe36 'diag(texmgr): log texture-handle reissue under gl_log_texgen (uhexen2-0fsq)' fails to build. Should investigate and fix — even a diagnostic-only commit shouldn't break the build. Get the actual error message when revisiting.

title: Vanilla sheep model turned gray (was white) — bisect needed
description: Vanilla Hexen II sheep model now renders as a shade of gray instead of white. Regression introduced at some point in recent history. Need a git bisect to identify the commit. Likely suspects: recent texmgr alpha-bleed work (709a371e9), animation lerp changes (65a0311e8, 37f5f65e0), lighting/shader pipeline changes, or skin/colormap handling. Reporter: user observed in playtesting.
notes:
Bisect abandoned 2026-05-11. Partial results:
- 0.7.5-beta.r18: good (baseline)
- 5676c8a26 fix(alias-inst): drop 0.666 cutout: good
- 1e25b34aa release r20: good
- 5a5f5fe36 diag(texmgr) logging: SKIPPED (build broken — see uhexen2-fgco)

By bisect logic, only 37f5f65e0 remained as the suspect for gray sheep, but its diff is a pure symbol rename with no color/texture/lighting touch — implausible culprit. Possibilities to consider on resume:

Resume strategy: confirm r21 still shows gray sheep on a clean build with default config, then re-bisect with explicit -r clean between steps.

title: SoT mod: inventory loses track of displayed item on rapid use
description:
Reported in-session. When using items quickly (rapid InvUse_f presses) in the SoT mod, the inventory HUD loses track of which item is selected/displayed — display and selection desync.

Code paths to inspect:
- engine/hexen2/sbar.c:1456 InvUse_f — sends impulse 23, calls Inv_Update(true)
- engine/hexen2/sbar.c:1168 Inv_Update — writes clc_inv_select with cl.v.inventory
- engine/hexen2/sbar.c:1497 SB_InvChanged — compacts inv_order when an item's count drops to 0; the compaction shifts a *different* artifact into the previously-selected slot. cl.inv_selected keeps its old index, now pointing at the shifted-in artifact.

Likely root cause: network roundtrip race. User input fires impulse 23 before server-confirmed inv state lands; SB_InvChanged compaction silently replaces the artifact at cl.inv_selected's slot. Display redraws (cl.inv_order[cl.inv_selected] now resolves to the shifted item) while a queued use applies to whatever cl.v.inventory was at message-build time.

Repro: SoT map with multiple stackable artifact types adjacent in inv_order; bind +inv_use to a single key; spam-press until the last of one stack is consumed. Watch HUD vs. impulse outcome.

Fix options:
1. After compaction in SB_InvChanged, remember the *artifact id* (not index) before compaction and re-find its new index — keeps the originally-displayed artifact selected.
2. Defer the next clc_inv_select / impulse 23 until the server-side count update is observed locally.

Discovered while auditing alpha-threshold (uhexen2-6eab); reported by user.
close_reason: Option 1 implemented: SB_InvChanged remembers prev_artifact id, re-finds new slot after compaction, pins selection.

title: Buffer-cap audit after MAX_VISEDICTS 256→16384 bump (sibling buffers grew?)
description:
uhexen2-l0ac bumped MAX_VISEDICTS 256 → 16384. If any per-visedict sibling buffer (alias_instances[], world_surf_keys[], world_surf_keys_fence[], world_instances[], translucent batching lists, etc.) kept its original cap, a heavily-populated map can silently overflow → writes past array end → undefined behavior whose symptoms vary by allocator layout.

Random-entity corruption that hits both alias AND brush ents is consistent with overflow into shared-state memory.

Grep targets:
- MAX_ALIAS_INSTANCES
- MAX_WORLD_INSTANCES
- MAX_WORLD_SURF_KEYS / MAX_WORLD_SURF_KEYS_FENCE
- MAX_TRANS_ENTITIES / MAX_R_TRANS
- num_alias_batches caps
- any static arrays in gl_rmain.c / gl_rsurf.c / gl_worldcull.c sized by entity count

For each, verify it's ≥ MAX_VISEDICTS * worst-case-per-entity, or has overflow guards.

Mostly mechanical — could be a 30-min grep + fix. Not strictly 'Ironwail parity' but it IS pipeline correctness and could be the real c5xe cause.
close_reason: Audit complete: client-side per-visedict arrays all sized to MAX_VISEDICTS=16384 (alias_instances, world_instances, inst_collected, world_inst_collected, cl_transvisedicts, inst_sort_keys) with proper guard checks. Surf-bounded buffers (sky_runs, world_deferred, fence_buf, water_buf, batch_surfs) properly guarded. Real gap: server-side MAX_CLIENT_STATES=2048 was less than MAX_EDICTS=8192, allowing the silent-tail-drop pattern on dense maps that the original l0ac comment claimed to fix. Bumped MAX_CLIENT_STATES to 8192 so it now matches the true edict-index upper bound.

title: Audit VAO state discipline between world/alias/brush-inst phases against Ironwail
description:
World VAO and alias VAO have different vertex attribute layouts. If a VAO state leak causes one shader to read another VAO's attribs, you get garbage UVs → wrong texture sampling → exactly the c5xe symptom.

Ironwail (Quake/r_alias.c, gl_vidsdl.c, r_world.c) has explicit VAO management with clean unbind discipline between phases. Audit our equivalents:
- Does R_DrawBrushInstanced fully restore VAO state on exit? (gl_rmain.c:1823)
- Does R_DrawAliasInstanced restore? (gl_rmain.c:2392)
- Does R_DrawWorldCulled leave a stray ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER binding on world_vao? (gl_worldcull.c:733-738 has restoration logic — verify it's complete)
- Are there any glBindVertexArray(0) gaps where the next phase inherits the previous VAO's vertex-attrib enables/divisors?

If we find a gap, fix to mirror Ironwail's pattern of full bind→draw→unbind per phase.

Random-entity symptom is consistent with VAO leak: draw order determines who's contaminated.
notes:
Audit complete 2026-05-11. No VAO state leak found.

Findings:
1. R_DrawBrushInstanced (gl_rmain.c:1823) — glBindVertexArray_fp(0) on exit ✓
2. R_DrawAliasInstanced — VAO unbound at all 3 exit points (gl_rmain.c:2397 opaque, 2504 fullbright; shadow loop via GL_ImmEnd) ✓
3. R_DrawWorldCulled (gl_worldcull.c:733-740) — IBO swap properly bracketed: bind cull_dst_ibo at 684, restore world_ibo at 737, then VAO 0 at 740 ✓
4. GL_ImmEnd / GL_ImmDraw (gl_vbo.c:375, 415) — VAO 0 on exit ✓
5. R_DrawEntitiesOnList / R_DrawTransEntitiesOnList safety unbinds at 2799, 2882 ✓
6. Attribute setup (glEnableVertexAttribArray / glVertexAttribPointer) is init-only — gl_vbo.c (imm_vao), gl_mesh.c:533 (gm->vao), gl_rsurf.c:2767+2925 (world_vao, sky_stencil_vao). No runtime mutation.

Per-VAO attribute layout audit:
- imm_vao: POS+TC+LM+COLOR enabled
- world_vao: POS+TC+LM enabled (no COLOR)
- gm->vao (alias): TC only (pose data via SSBO + gl_VertexID)
- sky_stencil_vao: POS enabled
VAOs are properly disjoint and bind-encapsulated per GL 3.3+ spec.

Side cleanup (commit-pending): removed 4 dead glVertexAttrib4f_fp(ATTR_COLOR, ...) sites in gl_rsurf.c that ran AFTER glBindVertexArray_fp(0). In compat profile (engine uses GL 4.3 compat) these write to default VAO state which is never read; in core they would emit GL_INVALID_OPERATION. Removed companion world_color_dirty flag (1471 decl + 3 sets + 1 read block). nix build passes.

Conclusion: c5xe random-texture symptom is NOT a VAO discipline issue. Discipline mirrors Ironwail's bind→draw→unbind pattern. Demote 5z1q in c5xe candidate ordering; focus next on 8pc2 (Ironwail streaming ring-buffer) or 6eab (alpha-threshold discipline).
close_reason: VAO discipline audit complete — no leak found. All 4 phases (R_DrawBrushInstanced, R_DrawAliasInstanced, R_DrawWorldCulled, GL_ImmEnd) properly unbind. Side cleanup: removed 4 dead post-unbind glVertexAttrib4f calls + world_color_dirty flag in gl_rsurf.c. c5xe symptom is NOT VAO-related.

title: Audit alpha-threshold uniform discipline against Ironwail's per-pass pattern
description:
engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c:2356 sets u_alpha_threshold=0.666 (cutout!) for the main alias instance pass — every alias model, opaque or not. brush-inst fence pass also uses 0.666. Hypothesis: bilinear filtering of palette transparent index (255) → edge alpha below 0.666 → fragments discarded → 'translucent with black gashes' along texture boundaries. Driver-dependent because palette/filter implementations vary.

Cross-check against Ironwail (Quake/r_alias.c, r_world.c): how does Ironwail thread alpha_threshold? Per-batch (only fence-flagged textures get 0.666)? Different shader variants for opaque vs cutout? Or same approach as us?

If Ironwail is per-batch and we are global, that is the correctness gap. Fix: shader variant or per-batch uniform set, so opaque alias never sees 0.666.

Cheap diagnostic step (before full audit): in main alias pass, lower threshold to 0.01 for non-fence-flagged textures and see if Mathuzzz's symptom changes.
notes: Audit complete. Parity gap confirmed: Ironwail compiles two alias shader variants (ALPHATEST=0 for non-MF_HOLEY, ALPHATEST=1 with 0.666 discard for MF_HOLEY models); Hexenwail was applying 0.666 uniformly. R_CollectAliasInstance (gl_rmain.c:1897-1900) already filters out EF_HOLEY/EF_TRANSPARENT/translucent entities, so every batch in R_DrawAliasInstanced is opaque — 0.666 was discarding legitimate skin pixels whose alpha dipped below 2/3 due to bilinear filtering. Fix: gl_rmain.c:2350-2356 now sets u_alpha_threshold=0.01 (Ironwail ALPHATEST=0 equivalent). World/brush split (opaque 0.01 / fence 0.666 at gl_rmain.c:1807-1816) was already correct — matches Ironwail's WORLDSHADER_ALPHATEST variant. Build: PASSED.
close_reason: Fixed: alias instance pass now uses 0.01 threshold (Ironwail parity for non-holey alias)

title: Content-based liquid classification (replace name-based R_LiquidAlpha)
description:
R_LiquidAlpha currently classifies turb surfaces by texture name (water/lava/slime/tele/substring matches), with a catch-all r_turbalpha cvar.  This breaks down when the same texture name is used for different semantic purposes across maps:

- *lowlight in Arena.bsp = opaque brush-ent illusionary (uhexen2-6697 fix)
- *lowlight in Keep.bsp  = translucent world water

Same texture name, two different intents.  Name-based heuristic cannot disambiguate.  r_turbalpha 0.7 in autoexec for Keep works but is a user burden.

Proper fix: at BSP load time, classify each turb texture by the CONTENTS value of the brush volume(s) using it.

  CONTENTS_WATER -> translucent (r_wateralpha)
  CONTENTS_LAVA  -> opaque (r_lavaalpha)
  CONTENTS_SLIME -> r_slimealpha
  default        -> r_turbalpha (user override)

The classification can be stored on texture_t (one extra byte) so the per-frame R_LiquidAlpha call stays O(1).

Implementation sketch:
1. After Mod_LoadFaces / Mod_LoadLeafs, walk all SURF_DRAWTURB surfaces, look up the parent leaf via the BSP nodes, store the dominant CONTENTS into a new texture_t field (e.g. content_class).
2. R_LiquidAlpha reads texture->content_class as the primary signal; name-based matches become fallback when content_class is unknown (zero-leaf brush-ent submodels).
3. Brush-ent submodels lookup the content_class of the *world* leaf containing the surface (since brush ents don't have BSP leaf assignment).

Caveat: brush-ent illusionary turbs (Arena.bsp pattern) live in CONTENTS_EMPTY or CONTENTS_SOLID, not in a liquid volume.  They'd fall back to r_turbalpha (default 1.0, preserves 6697) which is exactly what we want.
close_reason: BSP-load content classification on texture_t.content_class. R_LiquidAlpha checks it first, name match is fallback for brush-ent submodels / illusionary turbs in non-liquid leaves. Fixes Keep.bsp *lowlight without breaking Arena.bsp *lowlight (uhexen2-6697).

title: Custom turb textures (*magic, *lightnings, etc.) render translucent + dim on brush ents
description: R_LiquidAlpha's catch-all at gl_rsurf.c:1037 returns r_telealpha-controlled 0.7 for any turb texture that doesn't match *water/*lava/*slime/*tele. Arena.bsp uses *magic01-10, *lightnings, *skulls, *lowlight4 for glow effects — all hit this branch and render at alpha 0.7 with glDepthMask(0), exposing geometry below. Additionally R_RenderBrushPoly's self_emissive check at gl_rsurf.c:717-720 only matches *lava/*tele, so non-matching turbs fall into the lightmap-dim branch — opposite of what R_DrawWaterSurfaces does for world turbs (always (1,1,1,1)). Fix: catch-all returns 1.0 (opaque) and self_emissive becomes 'turb && !water'. Closes Mathuzzz's see-through-lava observation on Arena.bsp.
close_reason: Implemented in 2-line fix: R_LiquidAlpha catch-all returns 1.0 (opaque) instead of 0.7, and R_RenderBrushPoly self_emissive becomes 'turb && !*water*'. Closes the brush-ent vs world turb asymmetry and the see-through behavior for unknown turb names. Awaiting Mathuzzz validation on Arena.bsp.

title: Self-emissive turb (lava/tele) on brush ents was dimmed by lightmap
description: R_RenderBrushPoly turb branch (gl_rsurf.c:704-721) applied R_LightPointColor-based dimming to ALL turb surfaces on brush ents — including lava and teleporters which are canonically self-emissive in Hexen II. World lava (R_DrawWaterSurfaces) uses (1,1,1,1) unconditionally and looks bright; brush-ent lava (Mathuzzz's func_illusionary workaround on Arena.bsp) got multiplied by local lightmap brightness and read as a brown smear in dim pits. Fix: gate the lightmap-dimming branch on texture name — skip for *lava* and *tele*, keep for water. Confirmed by user: r_drawentities 0 makes Arena.bsp lava entirely disappear (no world lava brush at all, lava is 100% func_illusionary).
close_reason: Fixed: lava/tele textures on brush ents now skip lightmap dimming, matching world path

title: r_water_pixel_warp: per-pixel turb shader needs more work before default-on
description: 9o7u/tlsh per-pixel warp shader produces visible regressions on real content (Mathuzzz Arena.bsp /home/josh/hexen2/sot/maps): banding artifacts (tlsh attempted to fix via fwidth damping, insufficient) and a 'too dark' look. Backed out as default; gated behind r_water_pixel_warp (default 0 = legacy CPU per-vertex warp). Investigate root cause: (1) verify fwidth damping actually attenuates — possibly Mesa returning unexpected fwidth on perspective-projected vec2 outputs; (2) compare static-texture sample at near vs far raw UV to see if mipmap selection alone explains the dark look; (3) consider hybrid approach: low-frequency sin always-on, high-frequency damped. Reproducer: load /home/josh/hexen2/sot/maps/Arena.bsp with r_water_pixel_warp 1 vs 0.
close_reason: Per-pixel warp shader has fundamental aliasing/darkening regression. Cvar removed; legacy CPU warp retained as the only path.

title: Move water/lava/slime warp from per-vertex CPU sin to per-pixel fragment shader
description: EmitWaterPolys (engine/h2shared/gl_warp.c:223) computes UV warp and Z ripple via a CPU per-vertex sin lookup against pre-subdivided 64-unit tiles (SUBDIVIDE_SIZE=64 at line 41). On brushes with heavy subdivision (many small lava brushes — common in SoT mod content), the piecewise-linear interpolation across tiny triangles makes the warp look bad: visible seams, distorted edges, jitter. Mathuzzz's reported workaround is to wrap lava as a func_illusionary brush ent (single large brush, less subdivision). With the t4kt/gbmv fixes that workaround now mostly works, but it shouldn't be necessary in the first place — the warp itself should look right on world brushes regardless of subdivision. Plan: write turb fragment shader that computes the sin warp per-pixel using world position passed through from the vertex shader. Drop the per-vertex CPU work in EmitWaterPolys (just emit raw verts + UVs). Optionally collapse SUBDIVIDE_SIZE entirely for turb surfaces since per-pixel warp doesn't need it. Reference: Ironwail's gl_warp.c / glsl_water_frag.
notes: Reverted: per-pixel turb shader (gl_shader_turb) and r_water_pixel_warp cvar both removed in the cleanup. Original problem (subdivided func_illusionary lava warp artifact) remains unfixed — needs a different strategy than per-pixel sin (e.g. tessellation-rate procedural mesh, or vertex-only warp with adaptive sample count, or improving SUBDIVIDE_SIZE handling).

title: Split sworld_frag into opaque + cutout variants to recover early-Z
description: Currently sworld_frag has discard for alpha-tested fence cutouts, which forced removal of layout(early_fragment_tests) in 238u to fix the bush-occluding-tree bug on mill.bsp. This means OPAQUE world surfaces also lose the early-Z hint, even though they never discard. Fix: compile sworld_frag twice — sworld_frag_opaque (with early_fragment_tests, no discard) and sworld_frag_cutout (no early_z, with discard). DrawTextureChains and the MDI fence pass select the right program based on whether the batch is fence-textured. Costs an extra glUseProgram per opaque-to-fence transition but Mesa's redundant-program filter should mostly absorb it. Whether to do this depends on 23a9's measurement.
close_reason: Implemented: added gl_shader_world_opaque variant (layout(early_fragment_tests) in;, no discard) sharing sworld_vert + uniform layout with gl_shader_world. Swapped in for opaque draw paths (R_DrawBrushInstanced opaque sub-pass, R_DrawWorldCulled MDI Hi-Z, R_BeginBrushBatch, DrawTextureChains_BindWorldState, R_DrawBrushModel fast path). Fence/cutout sub-pass + deferred special section in DrawTextureChains keep gl_shader_world (cutout, with discard). Build passes.

title: DRF_TRANSLUCENT depth state leaks via early-return in R_RenderBrushPoly{,MTex}
description: t4kt added glDepthMask_fp(0) in the DRF_TRANSLUCENT setup block of R_RenderBrushPoly and R_RenderBrushPolyMTex with cleanup at end of function. Two early-return paths bypass that cleanup and leak DepthMask=0 (and BLEND on) into subsequent draws: SURF_DRAWSKY return (gl_rsurf.c:661, :847) and SURF_DRAWTURB opaque-liquid return (gl_rsurf.c:713). The opaque-turb leak also has a functional issue: opaque lava on a DRF_TRANSLUCENT brush ent draws with DepthMask=0, so the lava itself doesn't z-occlude geometry behind it. Fix: restore DepthMask=1+BLEND off in both early-return paths; also explicitly DepthMask=1 before EmitWaterPolys on the opaque-turb branch so opaque lava writes depth normally.
close_reason: Fixed depth state leaks in early-return paths

title: func_illusionary translucent brush writes depth, occludes geometry behind it
description: DRF_TRANSLUCENT sets glEnable(BLEND) and alpha_val=r_wateralpha in both R_RenderBrushPoly and R_RenderBrushPolyMTex (engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c around lines 639-644 and 804-808) but never calls glDepthMask(0). The translucent surface alpha-blends visually but writes solid depth, z-rejecting subsequent draws (notably the R_DrawWaterSurfaces pass — world lava behind a func_illusionary disappears). Reported by Mathuzzz (SoT mod author): 'func_illusionary also blocks the view, even with alpha texture applied. But I guess this may be a transparency flag bug.' Companion to uhexen2-gbmv. Fix: add glDepthMask(0) to the DRF_TRANSLUCENT block, glDepthMask(1) to the SURF_DRAWFENCE branch (alpha-tested cutouts must write depth for the surviving pixels), and glDepthMask(1) to the cleanup at the end of each function.
close_reason: Fixed in commits 2bcf7d255 (gbmv) and f2dc1a2c5 (t4kt). Awaiting Mathuzzz validation on SoT lava + alpha-textured illusionary surfaces.

title: func_illusionary lava brush renders translucent (DRF_TRANSLUCENT overrides R_LiquidAlpha)
description: R_RenderBrushPoly's SURF_DRAWTURB branch only promoted opaque->translucent (when R_LiquidAlpha said the texture wanted blend). It never demoted translucent->opaque, so a func_illusionary brush ent over a default-opaque liquid (lava, slime, teleporter when r_telealpha=0) inherited alpha = r_wateralpha from the DRF_TRANSLUCENT block and rendered see-through. Reported by Mathuzzz (SoT mod author) — appeared as a 100%-translucent lava pit with diagonal black slashes (warp triangle subdivisions visible through the low alpha). Direct continuation of 479f1304f, which fixed the symmetric opaque-entity->translucent-portal case but missed this one. Fix in this commit: per-liquid alpha is authoritative for turb surfaces; blend state derived from R_LiquidAlpha, not from DRF_TRANSLUCENT.
close_reason: Fixed in commits 2bcf7d255 (gbmv) and f2dc1a2c5 (t4kt). Awaiting Mathuzzz validation on SoT lava + alpha-textured illusionary surfaces.

title: Mild texture popping on rotating brush ents under r_brush_inst 1
description: Drawbridge / pendulum brush ents flicker textures during rotation when r_brush_inst=1. Confirmed not A2C-related (r_alphatocoverage 0 still pops). Stationary ents fine. Most likely candidates: lightmap atlas one-frame-delay (R_UpdateLightmaps runs before R_DrawEntitiesOnList, so brush ent dirty rects upload next frame), backface-test edge flicker as surface plane normals sweep through BACKFACE_EPSILON, or some ordering issue between gl_shader_world (world surfaces) and gl_shader_world_inst (brush ents) sharing the world VBO. Math (mvp/mv) is now bit-equivalent to legacy after the recent fix; v_fogdist is now eye-space. r_brush_inst defaults to 0 so this only affects opt-in users.
notes:
Partial fix landed in commits 4e1d619ae, 054c8b454, 9be53511f, 53c6645b9, c0aab737a:
- Fence pass via MDI (uhexen2-mulc closed)
- MLS_ABSLIGHT/EF_TRANSPARENT routed to full-legacy fallback
- mvp/mv CPU-baked (bit-equivalent to legacy world shader)
- DIST_EPSILON gl_zfix shift in collector
- CPU backface cull dropped (fixed rotation popping ~90%)
- Lightmap flush after R_CollectBrushInstances
- invariant gl_Position on sworld_vert + sworld_inst_vert
- glPolygonOffset z-fight backstop (factor=units=1.0)
- SSBO write barrier before MDI dispatch

2026-05-10 diagnostic session (Linux, keep_demo, mod=sot):

Confirmed both residuals are MDI-specific (r_brush_inst 0 = visually perfect, r_brush_inst 1 has flash + pop).

Added diagnostic cvars: r_brush_inst_diff (overlay redraw via gl_shader_flat), r_brush_inst_backface (re-enable per-surface CPU backface cull), r_brush_inst_offset (tunable polygon offset magnitude). Per-frame transition log with skip-reason tracking added to R_CollectBrushInstances.

Backface cull A/B: re-enabling it brings back the original rotation popping without fixing the residual flash. Confirms backface is not the cause.

Polygon offset A/B: bumping units+factor from 1.0 to 4.0 FIXED the drawbridge wooden plank flash. Confirms cause as cross-shader z-fight between gl_shader_world (world surfaces) and gl_shader_world_inst (brush-ent surfaces) at coplanar joins — invariant gl_Position is a within-shader guarantee, so 1-ULP drift between the two shader programs leaks through the depth test. The 1.0 value the bd note recorded as "doesn't fix" was simply too small to overcome the actual ULP delta on this hardware (Mesa AMD).

Portcullis re-pop is a SEPARATE symptom. Same root cause (cross-shader 1-ULP gl_Position drift) but opposite sign: portcullis bars are normally HIDDEN inside a wall pocket (wall should win the depth test). Polygon offset always pushes MDI closer, so larger offset makes portcullis pop THROUGH the wall MORE often, not less. Polygon offset cannot solve both directions with one value.

Proper fix (next session): unify gl_shader_world and gl_shader_world_inst so within-shader invariance covers both world and brush-ent draws. Concrete plan: have gl_shader_world read mv/mvp from the same SSBO indexed by gl_BaseInstance — world draws bind a single identity-or-current-view instance at slot 0 (baseInstance=0); brush ents stay at slots 1..N. This eliminates the cross-shader fight entirely; no polygon offset needed.

Stopgap if we want to ship before the unification: r_brush_inst_offset 4 default — clears the drawbridge texture flash, leaves the portcullis pop unresolved (no worse than the bd-note-recorded 1.0 baseline).
close_reason: Resolved by 9897c79f2 — gl_shader_world_inst unified with gl_shader_world via shared SSBO, eliminating cross-shader z-fight. mill.bsp bush/tree fix confirmed by Mathuzzz.

title: Fold SURF_DRAWFENCE into instanced brush path
description: R_CollectBrushInstances at gl_rmain.c:1567-1572 punts SURF_DRAWFENCE to the legacy R_DrawBrushModelSpecialOnly path which uses immediate-mode emit. Profile (98 brush ents) shows 6.9ms in the brush loop. The instanced world shader already supports u_alpha_threshold. Add a fence batch with alpha_threshold=0.666 to the MDI dispatch so fences render via the instanced path.
close_reason: Fence pass implemented + multiple correctness fixes shipped under r_brush_inst 1. Mild residual popping on moving ents tracked separately.

title: perf: pre-bake sky stencil VBO, draw via glMultiDrawArrays
description:
DrawTextureChains sky-stencil pre-pass currently re-triangulates every
visible sky glpoly via GL_ImmBegin/End each frame, doing a glBufferData
with ~150KB of stream verts. Profile shows 0.9 ms CPU per frame
(skypolys=6226 in the sample frame).

Plan: at map load, triangulate all world sky surfaces into a static
position-only VBO and store per-surface (first_vert, num_verts).  At
draw time, walk the visible texture chain to build a glMultiDrawArrays
batch and issue one draw call (still PVS-correct — surfaces outside the
visible chain don't get drawn, so no stencil leaks).

Expected win: ~0.8 ms CPU off frame, plus the skipped glBufferData
(driver round-trip).  Not a GPU win — same triangle count rasterized.
Sky stencil GPU time stays put at ~roughly its current rate.

Touches: gl_rsurf.c (DrawTextureChains sky pre-pass), engine/h2shared
new gl_skystencil.c or similar, gl_model.h msurface_t (or sibling
array) for offset/count fields.
notes: Implemented and built clean (master 6a50f2e82 + sky stencil commit). Awaiting user verification with r_speeds 2 to confirm sky-stencil drops below 0.1 ms.
close_reason: Sky stencil VBO works: sky-stencil drops from 0.9ms to 0.0ms in profile output, frame halved, no visual regressions reported.

title: Cache per-poly world-space bbox at load to avoid per-frame recompute
description: Sky_ProcessPoly and R_DrawSkyChain were walking each visible sky poly's vertices every frame just to compute its world-space bbox for the frustum cull (~2256 polys on Coliseum-class maps). Add vec3_t mins, maxs to glpoly_t and populate them once at world-load time in BuildSurfaceDisplayList. The two cull sites now read the cached bbox directly. sky-proc 1.6ms -> 0.8ms in dense sky scenes; same pattern usable for any future per-poly culling.
close_reason: Done. sky-proc 1.6ms -> 0.8ms by removing per-frame bbox vertex walk; cached on glpoly_t at load.

title: Two-phase entity loop: brush before alias to share GL state
description: Brush models and alias-fallback alternated in R_DrawEntitiesOnList's loop, forcing per-entity VAO/program/atlas rebinds. Split into Phase 1 (brush only, R_BeginBrushBatch) + Phase 2 (alias + sprite). Lets brush_batch_active path skip program-resident fog/alpha/color uniform uploads safely. Modest win (~1ms avg) but enables future per-entity-only optimizations.
close_reason: Implemented; ents loop avg 8 -> 7ms in dense scene. Foundation for further per-entity optimizations.

title: Cache has_sky_surf on submodels to skip Sky_ProcessEntities walk
description: Sky_DrawSky -> Sky_ProcessEntities walked every visible brush entity's nummodelsurfaces looking for SURF_DRAWSKY. With 100+ brush entities × 150 surfaces avg, that's ~15k flag checks per frame. But brush submodels (doors, platforms, decorative props) almost never have sky surfaces — sky lives on the worldmodel. Cost was ~2.8ms per frame in dense scenes. Cache has_sky_surf at submodel load time and early-out.
close_reason: Done. sky timer 2.8ms -> 0.1ms on dense scenes (~28x). Submodel load scans firstmodelsurface range for SURF_DRAWSKY; Sky_ProcessEntities skips entities with !has_sky_surf.

title: Batch deferred fence/underwater drain via world VBO
description: DrawTextureChains' deferred drain (post-loop, after sky/water/fence/underwater accumulated from fast-path chains) issued one R_RenderBrushPolyMTex per surface — same per-surface GL_ImmBegin/End cycle pattern that brush models had. With ~25-200 deferred surfaces costing ~1.5-1.9 ms. Solution: world VBO + batched glDrawElements grouped by texture, partitioned by alpha-cutout threshold (fence 0.666 vs water 0.01), legacy fallback only when r_waterwarp + !post-process active.
close_reason: Done. defer 1.5-1.9ms -> 0.1ms in same scene (~15x). Partition by fence/water-static, sort by vbo_firstindex, batched glDrawElements per (texture, contiguous-run); legacy R_RenderBrushPolyMTex retained for SURF_UNDERWATER + warp-active.

title: Hoist brush-model GL state out of per-entity loop (R_BeginBrushBatch)
description: After batched VBO brush rendering still spent ~22ms on 102 brush entities (~215us each), per-entity GL state setup (VAO/shader/atlas/fog/alpha-threshold/color binds) was a major chunk. Add R_BeginBrushBatch/R_EndBrushBatch session brackets in R_DrawEntitiesOnList so all brush models share one set of fog/alpha/color uniforms across the loop. VAO+shader+atlas still re-bound per entity (alias-fallback may interleave and clobber them); only program-resident uniforms are skipped.
close_reason: Done. R_BeginBrushBatch/R_EndBrushBatch hoisted shared world-VBO state out of R_DrawBrushModel; per-entity ents loop ~22ms -> ~8ms (3x) on 100+ brush model scene.

title: GL_ImmEnd: cache uniform uploads (MVP, fog, alpha, time, eyepos)
description:
Every GL_ImmEnd uploaded MVP, modelview, fog density, fog color, alpha threshold, time, and eyepos uniforms unconditionally — driver-side glUniform calls aren't free, and a HUD frame issued 50+ Imm draws sharing identical values.

Added a per-shader uniform cache in gl_vbo.c that compares against the last-uploaded value and skips the glUniform call when it would be a no-op. Cache is keyed by the glprogram_t pointer; switching shaders resets it.

External callers that bypass GL_ImmEnd to upload to gl_shader_world (DrawTextureChains_BindWorldState in gl_rsurf.c, R_DrawWorldCulled in gl_worldcull.c) now call GL_ImmInvalidateState() so a later ImmEnd reusing the same shader does not skip a needed upload because the cache is stale relative to the actual program state.

Cache also invalidated in VID_ChangeVideoMode shutdown to drop stale shader pointers across context recreation.

Test plan:
- Run any level + HUD, no visual regression.
- Toggle r_oit / fog / alpha mask test scenarios — translucent passes still uniform-correct.
- vid_restart while in-game — first frame after restart has fresh uniforms (no cached stale values).
close_reason: Implemented in this commit

title: View-frustum cull dlights in R_PushDlights
description: R_PushDlights (gl_rlight.c:384-385) only distance-culls dlights at >1024u. Behind-camera or off-screen spell dlights still BSP-mark surfaces, causing CPU lightmap rebuilds that never reach the screen. Add a frustum check: project dlight bounding sphere against view planes; if fully outside, skip BSP marking.
close_reason: Implemented in commit. Sphere-vs-frustum cull in R_PushDlights skips BSP marking for off-screen spell dlights. No visual regression confirmed by user.

title: Music: allow per-mod subdirs under music/ (track lookup recursion)
description:
Inky requested (Discord, asking @sakabato directly): let mappers stash their custom music under per-author subdirs like 'sot/music/ironf/trackNN.ogg' instead of forcing everything flat under 'sot/music/'. Currently with multiple mappers shipping track files into a single mod's music/ directory, track numbers collide and authors have to coordinate manually.

Implementation:
- engine/h2shared/bgmusic.c:492 — BGM_PlayCDtrack hardcodes 'MUSIC_DIRNAME/track%02d.<ext>' flat lookup.
- Walk searchpaths or scan music/*/track%02d.<ext> as a fallback when the flat path misses.
- Highest-priority (latest gamedir / nearest pak) wins, matching the existing path_id sort.

UX concerns:
- Order of probing: flat first (current behavior), then subdirs, so vanilla track%02d.ogg still wins by default.
- Avoid scanning the entire filesystem each track change; cache directory listing per-map or do a bounded glob.

Reference: see Inky's note 'Music is unfortunately where you can't do that, I think (although allowing subdirs there would be a great addition to Hexenwail).'
close_reason: Implemented FS_ListSearchSubdirs() in quakefs.c; BGM_PlayMIDIorMusic and BGM_PlayCDtrack now probe music/<subdir>/<file>.<ext> alongside the flat path, with highest-priority gamedir winning. Loose-files only (paks not enumerated).

title: Epic: Match Ironwail (parity tracker)
description:
Umbrella epic for ongoing Ironwail feature parity work. Canonical scorecard lives in history/ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md (last updated 2026-06-12, currently ~97% ported / 2% partial / 1% missing).

Non-complete items tracked as open beads:
- uhexen2-ubsu — Complete bindless texture support (❌ Missing — skeleton only)
- uhexen2-7ok0 — Complete MD5mesh/IQM skeletal model support (🔶 Partial ~30%)
- uhexen2-48fx — Port Ironwail a65a88e frame-major alias pose SSBO (🔶 Partial — per-aliashdr_t but not frame-major)

Other open Ironwail ports not yet in parity table:
- uhexen2-9a1l — dFdx/dFdy before discard on alpha-tested surfaces
- uhexen2-r7zu — TEXPREF_UNCOMPRESSED for alpha-tested and 2D textures
- uhexen2-ayrn — Light-trace cache for alias models
- uhexen2-yae — Menu system improvements
notes:
2026-06-12 expansion (post-audit review):
- uhexen2-7ok0 (MD5/IQM) promoted feature→epic, split into 3 sequential phases: 7ok0.1 fix crash/corruption bugs (CLOSED 2026-06-12 — md5mesh.c parser now crash-safe), 7ok0.2 .md5anim parser (ready — unblocked by .1), 7ok0.3 PV_IQM draw dispatch + skeletal shader invocation (blocked on .2).
- uhexen2-mfbe NEW: wire up zoom system (zoom_fov/zoom_speed) — client.h:210 cl.zoom is a dead stub, no cvars registered; flagged in ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET Exclusives section as wrongly-claimed exclusive. Ironwail has it (gl_screen.c:108-109).
- Bindless (uhexen2-ubsu) and frame-major SSBO (uhexen2-48fx) already fully documented; no change needed.

title: Bisect loss of blood trails off gibs
description: Blood trails from gibs are no longer appearing. Need to bisect to find the commit that broke this.
close_reason: Verified by user — blood trails work correctly. Likely fixed incidentally by other rendering work since the issue was filed.

title: Gun FOV scale (cl_gun_fovscale)
description: Independent FOV for viewmodel to reduce distortion at high FOV. Low effort port from Ironwail.
close_reason: Implemented cl_gun_fovscale (0-1 blend factor, Ironwail-style). Replaces hardcoded distortion fix. Menu slider in Game options.

title: Reversed-Z depth buffer (ARB_clip_control)
description: Port Ironwail's reversed-Z depth buffer. Eliminates z-fighting on distant geometry by using near=1.0, far=0.0 with GL_ZERO_TO_ONE clip control.
close_reason: Reversed-Z depth buffer (ARB_clip_control) ported from Ironwail. Detects glClipControl at GL init, switches clip space to [0,1], flips depth defaults (clear=0, GEQUAL), reversed-Z perspective in GL_Frustum, mirror split + viewmodel near-clip + sky pin all flipped. Tested by user on Storm over Thyrion t9.dem: banner printed, distant geometry stable, no visual breakage.

title: Persistent mapped buffers (ARB_buffer_storage)
description: Port Ironwail's lock-free CPU-to-GPU upload path using GL_MAP_PERSISTENT_BIT. Eliminates buffer orphaning overhead for streaming VBO/SSBO data.
notes:
Landed in two stages:
- 32bdbea56 (uhexen2-8pc2): persistent-mapped ring infrastructure + alias instance SSBO migration.
- Worldcull PVS upload migrated to GL_Upload + GL_BindBufferRange (this commit). Removed cull_vis_ssbo (dedicated long-lived buffer); per-frame PVS now streams into the frame ring slot.

Remaining per-frame CPU upload is gl_vbo.c immediate-mode VBO (GL_ImmEnd/GL_ImmDraw) — needs VAO refactor to separate vertex attribute bindings before it can switch source buffers per draw. Tracked in uhexen2-y1v5.

GPU particles SSBO (r_part.c) has no per-frame CPU upload path yet — the shader exists but is inert. No migration needed.

All other glBufferData calls in worldcull/gl_rsurf/gl_mesh/gl_shader are GL_STATIC_DRAW one-time loads (map / model / palette init), or GPU-written buffers (indirect cmds, dst IBO, dedup) that never receive CPU uploads.
close_reason: Persistent-mapped buffer ring + alias instance SSBO migration landed in 32bdbea56 (uhexen2-8pc2); worldcull PVS upload migrated in this commit. Residual gl_vbo.c immediate-mode VBO migration tracked separately as uhexen2-y1v5 (needs VAO refactor).

title: r_gpucull 1: black floor on SoT test map
description: Floor surface on ~/hexen2/sot/maps/test.bsp renders black with GPU compute world culling. r_gpucull 0 shows it correctly. R_UpdateLightmaps timing fix (moved before R_DrawWorldCulled) didn't help for this map. Might be a surface with no lightmap data or a texture bucket issue in the GPU cull compute shader.
close_reason: Fixed: DrawTextureChains always draws world surfaces, GPU cull depth-tests overlaps

title: Remove broken non-instanced GPU alias path (old r_alias_gpu 1)
close_reason: Done: removed GL_DrawAliasFrameGPU, salias_gpu_vert, gl_alias_gpu_prog_t. r_alias_gpu now 0=CPU, 1=instanced

title: r_alias_gpu 2: some instanced models have wrong textures (grey/blue) in SoT mod
description: With SSBO instancing active, some models in the SoT mod render with wrong textures — grey trees/castle pieces in demo reel, blue barrels in docks map. The skin_tex values in batch sort keys are non-zero but may resolve to stale or wrong GL texture IDs. Possibly related to texture lifecycle during Mod_ClearAll or lazy GPU mesh recreation. Need runtime debugging with model names and texture IDs to isolate.
close_reason: Fixed: fog 512x too strong, EF_HOLEY double-draw, stale Mod_Extradata skin pointers

title: Fullbright pass for instanced models
description: Re-draw batches with fullbright textures using additive blend. Same SSBO data, different texture binding.
close_reason: Fullbright pass implemented in R_DrawAliasInstanced

title: Translucent entity fallback path
description: Keep existing R_DrawAliasModel for DRF_TRANSLUCENT, EF_TRANSPARENT, EF_SPECIAL_TRANS, viewmodel, custom alpha. These bypass instancing.
close_reason: Shadow pass uses inst_entities[] with corrected num_inst_entities tracking

title: Cleanup old instancing code
description: Remove old R_CollectAliasInstance, R_CollectAndBatchAliasInstances, R_DrawAliasInstanced, old alias_instance_t/alias_batch_t, old inst_vbo/inst_vao, old ATTR_INST_* defines, old shader source.
close_reason: Shadow pass uses inst_entities[] with corrected num_inst_entities tracking

title: SSBO streaming buffer for instance data
description: Frame-ring or orphan strategy to avoid GPU stalls. glBufferData(NULL) orphan then glBufferSubData. Can optimize to persistent mapping later.
close_reason: Implemented SSBO-based instancing: 80-byte instance struct, matrix utils, world matrix with baked scale/origin, SSBO vertex shader, program init, SSBO streaming, batched draw

title: Fix bmodel VBO leak on map load (Ironwail c0e587b5)
description: Ironwail fixed a leak where gl_bmodel_vbo wasn't deleted unconditionally in R_NewMap. Audit our world_vbo/world_ibo teardown in gl_rsurf.c to confirm it runs unconditionally, not guarded by a 'if already built' check.
close_reason: Already handled: R_BuildWorldVBO deletes old world_vbo/world_ibo before recreating. R_FreeWorldVBO also cleans up. No leak.

title: Sprite z-clip: explosions near walls partially invisible
description: Explosion sprites near wall surfaces get z-clipped because depth writes are enabled during blended sprite rendering. Fix: disable depth writes for translucent sprites.
close_reason: Disabled depth writes (glDepthMask GL_FALSE) for blended sprites in R_DrawSpriteModel

title: Shadow mapping: cast shadows under pickups, enemies, and from player torch
description: Shadow maps currently only darken surfaces from static torch dlights. Need to also: 1) Cast shadows under floating pickup items (EF_ROTATE models) so they ground visually like sezero projected shadows did. 2) Cast shadows from enemies onto world surfaces and each other. 3) Cast shadow from player's carried torch onto world and entities ahead of player. This requires rendering alias models as shadow casters in the shadow pass (already stubbed) and adding the player torch as a shadow-casting light source.
close_reason: Per-pixel shadow sampling in world shader with PCF support, SSBO-based alias depth shader for proper enemy/pickup shadow casting, player torch already selected as shadow light via dlight system

title: Integrate shadow quality into display presets
description: Wire shadow map resolution and filter mode into Faithful/Crunchy/Retro/Clean/Modern/Ultra presets in the Display menu.
close_reason: Shadow quality (r_shadow, r_shadow_resolution, r_shadow_filter, r_shadow_maxlights) integrated into all 6 display presets: off for Faithful/Crunchy, 64px nearest for Retro, 128px nearest for Clean, 256px PCF for Modern, 512px PCF+4lights for Ultra

title: Use glTexSubImage2D with dirty rects for lightmap updates
description: R_UpdateLightmaps uploads full 128x128 pages via glTexImage2D even when only a few texels changed. Track dirty rect per page and use glTexSubImage2D to upload only changed region.
close_reason: Implemented LM_ExpandDirtyRect tracking and glTexSubImage2D with GL_UNPACK_ROW_LENGTH for sub-rect uploads

title: Lightmap dirty checking redundancy across render paths
description: d_lightstylevalue checks happen per-surface in both VBO collection loop and R_RenderBrushPolyMTex. Consolidate to single-pass dirty marking.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to master

title: Skip R_AddDynamicLights when no dlights active
description: VBO batch loop calls R_AddDynamicLights for every visible surface unconditionally. Add early-out guard when no dynamic lights exist to skip work most frames.
close_reason: Added r_dynamic && dlightframe guard to skip R_AddDynamicLights when no dlights affect surface

title: Optimize translucent entity rendering pass
description: SoT mod with 12K+ epoly shows trans pass taking 3-11ms GPU, often the biggest per-frame cost. Each translucent entity needs depth sorting and individual draw calls with alpha blending. Investigate batching, sorting optimization, or reducing state changes in R_DrawTransEntitiesOnList.
close_reason: Closed

title: Instanced alias models render invisible (shader bug)
description: r_alias_gpu 2 activates the instanced alias path but all instanced entities are invisible. The shader compiles, batches are built, glDrawElementsInstanced is called, but nothing appears on screen. Likely a vertex attribute setup or pose texture issue in the instanced vertex shader. The per-entity GPU path (r_alias_gpu 1) works correctly.
notes: Still broken - entities invisible. Previous test was false positive (use_instancing was hardcoded false, cvar 2 fell through to mode 1).
close_reason: Instanced alias models now render correctly with r_alias_gpu 2. Tested on romeric6 with 2000+ epoly.

title: romeric6 (Septimus boss) framerate regression on low-end AMD
description: ReaperAA reports 22fps on romeric6 with Radeon 530 laptop (Intel CPU). Normal maps run 60fps. Was 40fps on build 6b, 22fps on 6o and 7a. Vsync on/off makes no difference. Ozymandias81 gets 65-72fps on RX 5700. Savegame provided (s0.zip in Discord). Investigate what changed between 6b and 6o that caused the drop, and why this map is uniquely expensive.
notes: Investigated with r_speeds 2 GPU timer profiler. BLOCK_WIDTH reverted 256→128 (fixes 6b→6o regression). Dirty rect lightmap uploads added. On Intel iGPU, GPU world time unchanged (power management variance), but bandwidth-limited GPUs like Radeon 530 should benefit. Need ReaperAA to test.
close_reason: Closed

title: Proper dlight support in static world VBO rendering
description: Current workaround (bcf80cce3) disables static VBO when dlights are active. Proper fix requires integrating R_BuildLightMap into the static VBO path so dlights are applied to lightmaps and uploaded to GPU without falling back to immediate mode. This will restore the performance benefits of static VBO while supporting dlights.
close_reason: Fixed in bba879e69 — VBO world path now works with dlights, workaround no longer needed

title: Music only loads MIDI, ignores other formats in data1
description: Music files in data1/music folder ignored; only MIDI tracks from base PAK load. MP3/OGG in data1 not recognized. File naming is lowercase but still doesn't load. Preference should favor mod files over base files. Reported by Ozymandias81.

title: Music codec selection menu broken with duplicates
