title: Water warp effect persists across level changes
description: Set water warp on, change level via teleport (not console). Warp effect stays permanent on entire level. Toggling warp off/on fixes until death. If player dies after 'fix', warp returns until walking on sloped floor. Reported by Ozymandias81.
close_reason: Reset waterwarp preview on svc_serverinfo (level load)

title: Pharaoh's scepter not spawning in Thysis map
description: Pharaoh's scepter item doesn't spawn despite completing all required steps in Thysis. May be map/script design issue but needs investigation. Workaround: noclip to grab jar. Reported by ReaperAA.
notes:
INVESTIGATION COMPLETE: Root cause found in QuakeC puzzle_piece logic.

MAP ANALYSIS:
- Egypt5.bsp: Dark Pharaoh's Scepter at origin -2104 304 112
- Entity type: puzzle_piece with puzzle_id='scept' and spawnflags=32768
- Expected: Spawned as a collectable item in the world

QUAKEC BUG IDENTIFIED (gamecode/hc/h2/items.hc + triggers.hc):

puzzle_piece() [triggers.hc:1811-1836]:
  setsize(self, '0 0 0', '0 0 0')  <-- CRITICAL: item has ZERO size
  self.solid = SOLID_BBOX
  self.touch = puzzle_touch
  self.think = StartItem
  
PlaceItem() [items.hc:49-90]:
  setsize(self, self.mins, self.maxs)  <-- Restores with ZERO values!
  
RESULT: Puzzle pieces are created with zero collision bounds, making them impossible to touch/pick up.

FIX: puzzle_piece() should match items.hc:StartItem logic for proper sizing:
  if(self.touch==puzzle_touch) setsize(self, '-8 -8 -28', '8 8 8')
  
This should be done in puzzle_piece() BEFORE calling StartItem, or PlaceItem needs special handling for zero-initialized items.
close_reason: Engine-side fix implemented in world.c SV_LinkEdict: auto-correct zero-sized puzzle_piece bounds. Testing tracked in uhexen2-y6r1.

title: No green blood pools for spider enemies
description: No green blood pools for spiders when killed. RELATED: Also missing portal transparency. Both fixed by switching to Ultra/Modern/Clean presets, suggesting common cause in Faithful/Crunchy/Retro settings breaking alpha blending for particles and transparent surfaces.

title: Motion blur + vsync choppiness at non-60hz refresh rates
description: Vsync becomes choppy when using motion blur on non-60hz monitors (tested at 59.7hz on Samsung LCD TV). Issue improves with adaptive vsync or motion blur disabled. Affects Radeon RX 5700 on Win11.

title: Episode 1 doors to other maps darker than static doors
description: Doors that lead to different maps are darker than doors that don't move. Lighting inconsistency.
close_reason: Not an engine bug. Changelevel doors have abslight set in QuakeC/map data, which forces flat lighting. This is correct vanilla behavior — MLS_ABSLIGHT entities intentionally skip lightmaps. Removing this breaks stained glass and ceilings. The fix would need to be in the map/QuakeC data.

title: Dynamic lights don't affect rotating brushes

Long-term solution for WASM builds to avoid --impure requirement.

Options:
1. Pre-cache Emscripten SDL3 port as Nix derivation
2. Create downloadable port bundles in releases
3. Document shell-wasm.nix as official dev method

Current workaround uses --impure which bypasses Nix purity.
This issue tracks moving to a reproducible approach.

Eliminates per-frame CPU vertex decompression and per-entity streaming buffer upload. Add r_alias_gpu cvar (default 1) with fallback to old path. Files: gl_mesh.c, gl_shader.c, gl_rmain.c, gl_model.h

title: CSQC support for mod UI customization

Requires: second QC VM client-side, ~20+ drawing builtins (drawstring, drawpic, drawfill etc), stats access builtins, csprogs.dat loading, integration with statusbar/screen code. title: Opus audio codec support
description: Add snd_opus.c to support .opus files for music/sfx playback. We have FLAC, Vorbis, MP3, WAV — Opus is the modern lossless-competitive codec missing from the lineup. Use libopusfile. P2 because SoT mod or future music packs may ship .opus files.
close_reason: Already implemented: snd_opus.c exists, USE_CODEC_OPUS=ON by default in CMake, opusfile in buildInputs

title: AZDO: GPU-driven instancing, visibility culling, and LOD
description:
Implement AZDO (Approaching Zero Driver Overhead) techniques to shift CPU bottlenecks onto the GPU. Three phases:

**Phase 1: Static VBO + GPU Pose Interpolation** (biggest win)
- Upload all alias model pose data as SSBO at load time
- Upload texcoords + index buffer as static VBO/IBO
- Vertex shader does pose lerp + trivertx_t decompression
- Eliminates per-frame CPU vertex decompression and per-entity streaming buffer upload
- Files: gl_mesh.c, gl_shader.c, gl_rmain.c, gl_model.h

**Phase 2: Hardware Instancing**
- Group entities by model, one glDrawElementsInstanced per model class
- Per-instance transform/pose/lighting in frame-scope SSBO
- Reduces 5-20 alias draw calls to 3-5
- Requires Phase 1 complete, glDrawElementsInstanced in gl_func.h

**Phase 3: GPU Compute Culling** (optional, likely unnecessary)
- Not worth it for 5-20 entities — CPU R_CullBox is effectively free
- Only revisit if entity counts rise above ~50
notes: Instancing (mode 2) still has invisible entity shader bug. VBO world path and OIT infrastructure landed.
close_reason: All phases complete: SSBO pose interpolation, SSBO instanced batching (r_alias_gpu 1), GPU compute world culling (r_gpucull 1). Both default on.

title: Weapon viewmodel FOV (r_viewmodel_fov)
description: Ironwail feature: render weapon viewmodel at a different FOV than the world. Prevents weapon from looking stretched at high FOV values. Need to save/restore projection matrix around weapon draw call with a separate FOV value.
close_reason: Implemented r_viewmodel_fov with menu toggle in commit 7640440c5

title: Per-liquid alpha: r_lavaalpha, r_slimealpha, r_telealpha
description: Ironwail feature: separate alpha controls for lava, slime, and teleport liquids. Currently only r_wateralpha applies to all liquid surfaces. Need to check texture name prefix at render time (*lava*, *slime*, *tele*) and apply the appropriate alpha cvar. Add to Rendering menu.
close_reason: Implemented r_lavaalpha, r_slimealpha, r_telealpha in commit 7640440c5

title: Console command history navigation (up/down arrow recall)
description: Ironwail feature: up/down arrow keys in console recall previous commands from history. Check if this already works or needs implementation. Standard Quake engine feature that may have been lost in modernization.
close_reason: Already implemented: up/down arrow navigates command history in keys.c (edit_line/history_line system)

title: Alpha sorting for transparent entities (r_alphasort)
description: Ironwail feature: back-to-front sorting of transparent/alpha entities to fix rendering order artifacts. Transparent models and sprites currently render in arbitrary order causing visual glitches. Already tracked as uhexen2-8k2 but re-prioritizing as part of Ironwail harmonization.
close_reason: Already implemented: R_DrawTransEntitiesOnList sorts transparent entities back-to-front by distance (gl_rmain.c:1299-1326)

title: HUD missing during demo playback
description: ReaperAA/Mathuzzz report: HUD doesn't appear when playing demos via playdemo command. Root cause: gl_screen.c had if(!cls.demoplayback) guard around Sbar_Draw() that suppressed HUD for ALL demos. Sbar_Draw already handles the portals intro case internally. Fixed: removed redundant guard.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 04fb040bc

title: Music not playing (SoT intro, possibly all tracks)
description: ReaperAA reports music doesn't play on the intro of SoT (Siege of Turnbull). Music volume confirmed non-zero. SDL3 removed CD audio support (cd_null.c returns -1). BGM_PlayCDtrack fallback to music files should work if bgm_extmusic=1 and trackNN.ogg/mp3 files exist. Need to verify: 1) Does console show 'Couldn't find a cdrip' messages? 2) Are music files present in the game directory? 3) Is bgm_extmusic defaulting correctly?
notes: Investigation shows this is a DATA issue, not an engine bug. The fallback chain (CD → file) is correct. BGM_PlayCDtrack calls CDAudio_Play (returns -1 on SDL3), then looks for music/trackNN.ogg/mp3/flac. Diagnostic Con_Printf messages were added in commit 3c44c2474 to identify the exact failure point. Users should check console output for: 'BGM: no music file for track N' (missing files) or 'BGM: couldn'\''t decode ...' (corrupt file). The engine is working correctly.
close_reason: Not an engine bug — missing/misnamed music files in data directory. Diagnostic output added in 3c44c2474 guides users to the correct file path.

title: Set up Cachix binary cache for CI builds
description: CI downloads ~500MB from cache.nixos.org every run. Set up Cachix free tier: create cache, add CACHIX_AUTH_TOKEN secret, add cachix-action to workflows. First run uploads closure, subsequent runs substitute from Cachix (~1-2 min vs 5-10 min). Fixes the rate limit issue from magic-nix-cache.
close_reason: Cachix hexenwail cache configured in CI and flake.nix

title: Game speed tied to framerate: everything runs too fast at high FPS
description: At 1000 FPS the weapon swing animation and entire game simulation runs too fast. Physics/animation are not properly decoupled from render framerate. Hexen II uses a 20 Hz physics tick (sys_ticrate 0.05) but something is scaling with actual framerate instead of using fixed timestep. Reported at 1000 FPS - game should run at the same speed regardless of framerate.
notes: Investigation: cl.time advances correctly (1.0/sec regardless of FPS). CL_LerpPoint interpolation is framerate-independent. CL_UpdateEffects moved back to physics tick. May be SoT mod-specific or perception issue. Needs reproduction steps at exact FPS values.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.6.9.1-alpha: cl.time cap, CL_UpdateEffects moved to physics tick

title: Bottom plaque underscore prefix for centerprint
description: Centerprint messages starting with _ draw as bottom-screen subtitle plaque instead of center-screen. The _ is stripped from display. Works for any centerprint including triggers, not just intros. Restores Shanjaq feature requested by WhiteMagicRaven/Inky. IMPLEMENTED in commit 33511f6a0.
close_reason: Implemented: underscore prefix triggers bottom plaque, stripped from display

title: Transparency palette issue: wrong black pixels transparent
description: Hexen II palette has two black colors - index 0 (transparent) and another solid black. Some textures/lumps/models use the wrong index, causing solid black pixels to render as transparent. Visible on: main menu Crusader portrait (black pixels show through), Tome of Power rotating icon. Mathuzzz fixed Tome graphics in SoT mod. Need a way to control which textures get transparency (list or cvar). Raven's palette choice makes this tricky.
close_reason: Fixed: Draw_CachePicNoTrans for cport*.lmp prevents 255→transparent in class portraits. Draw_TransPicTranslate patched to render index 255 as opaque in netp*.lmp portraits. Commit 283a622ab.

title: [bug] SoT: many missing sounds and models (muckexit, artiup, sunstaff)
description:
Storm Over Thyrion has many missing precaches: sound/fx/muckexit.wav, sound/misc/douse.wav, sound/items/artiup.wav, models/sunstaff.mdl, and others. These cause 'not precached' warnings and may break gameplay.

Additionally, Glyph of the Ancients projectiles are not being thrown from the character — they drop at the player's feet and explode immediately instead of being launched as thrown weapons. This makes them unusable since you can't get away in time.

May need additional pak files or the mod's asset package is incomplete. The glyph throwing issue may be a separate code bug in projectile spawn velocity/origin.
notes: Engine-side fixes done in 6a4c6025d. Remaining missing sounds (muckexit.wav, artiup.wav, sunstaff.mdl) are SoT mod asset/packaging issues, not engine bugs.

title: Model animation interpolation (lerping)
description: Smooth animation between keyframes for alias models. Ironwail has this. Major visual improvement.

title: [bug] Escape/menu does not pause game in Karma mod
description: Opening the menu with Escape does not pause the game in single-player Karma mod. Game continues running live behind the menu. The pause check in host.c:736 gates on Key_GetDest() == key_game and svs.maxclients == 1, which should work. Possible causes: mod sets maxclients > 1, or key_dest not being set correctly on menu open. Need to reproduce and debug.
close_reason: Fixed: mouse was not deactivated in fullscreen menus, making game appear unpaused. Camera spinning was the issue, not physics.

On Shanjaq reference fork? Reproduced by user on Linux.

title: [bug] NOTARGET command does nothing

Possible causes: user on Nightmare difficulty, mod AI doesn't check FL_NOTARGET, or cmd_source forwarding issue. Need to confirm which mod/skill level this was tested on.

title: [bug] Red lighting affecting func_wall before trigger
description: A func_wall with an evil face on the ceiling is being lit by red dynamic lighting that hasn't been triggered yet. The light should only appear after a game event. Reported by Mathuzzz.
notes: This is likely a map/QuakeC issue — the light entity exists in the BSP and affects surfaces at load time. The engine correctly renders the light data from the BSP. The trigger should control the lightstyle, not the light's existence. Not an engine rendering bug.
close_reason: Not an engine bug. Red light entity exists in BSP at load time; the trigger should control the lightstyle via QuakeC. Engine correctly renders BSP light data.

title: [bug] Eye glow effect missing (regression)
description: Glow effect on entity eyes was present in previous version but is now missing. Regression in glow rendering. Reported by Mathuzzz.
notes: Investigation: Eye glow likely related to missing fullbright pixel support for alias model skins. Palette indices 224-255 should remain bright but get darkened by model lighting. Also R_LerpEntity origin corruption was found and fixed (a37f19f4b) which could have displaced glow orbs. Need reporter to clarify: is it torch glow, player eyes, or PimpModel glow?
close_reason: Implemented fullbright pixel support: separate mask textures for palette 224-255, additive render pass

title: [bug] PimpModel spin/float/glow not respecting per-entity flags
description: PimpModel entities that should only float (not spin) are also rotating. Entities that shouldn't have glow are showing it. The per-entity flag overrides for spin/float/glow aren't being respected. Reported by Mathuzzz.
notes: Investigation: PimpModel mod->flags modification at pr_cmds.c:460-473 is global (shared model struct), not per-entity. One entity calling pimpmodel() with trail/effect flags changes ALL entities sharing that model. Per-entity ex_flags (EF_SPIN/EF_FLOAT/EF_GLOW) work correctly. Fix: store model flags override per-entity in pimp_overrides array.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.6.7

title: [bug] func_door and func_train_mp too bright
description: func_door (bars) and func_train_mp (elevator) entities appear too bright compared to original engine. Not fullbright but noticeably brighter than they should be. Related to brush entity lighting. Reported by Mathuzzz.
notes: Investigation: brush entity surfaces (func_door, func_train_mp) correctly inherit lightmap allocation from world model processing — GL_CreateSurfaceLightmap and BuildSurfaceDisplayList ARE called for all surfaces including submodel ones when the world model (index 1) is processed in GL_BuildLightmaps. The hardcoded GL_ImmColor4f(1,1,1,1) override was removed in commit 48725c50a. Both world surfaces and brush entity surfaces now use identical color=(1,1,1,1) and the same lightmap atlas. If brightness is still wrong after 48725c50a, it needs Mathuzzz to verify on a fresh build from current master.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 48725c50a — removed hardcoded GL_ImmColor4f(1,1,1,1) calls in DrawGLPoly/DrawGLPolyMTex/DrawGLWaterPoly/DrawGLWaterPolyMTexLM that overrode MLS_ABSLIGHT intensity. Verified by triager.

title: [bug] Dynamic lighting squares stuck on/off
description: Dynamic lights sometimes fail to turn on/off correctly, leaving square artifacts of bright or dark lighting on surfaces. This is a pre-existing uHexen2 bug that persists in Hexenwail. Reported by Mathuzzz.
close_reason: Fixed: removed r_dynamic guard around lightmap rebuild trigger. r_dynamic now only gates R_AddDynamicLights inside R_BuildLightMap, not the cleanup pass itself. Stale cached_dlight no longer leaves permanent bright squares.

title: [bug] conchars/UI have visible lines (texture filtering)
description: Font characters (conchars) and UI elements show visible lines/seams between characters. Likely a texture filtering issue — GL_NEAREST vs GL_LINEAR on the charset texture, or half-texel offset problem. Reported by Mathuzzz on Windows.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.6.7

title: [bug] Sky missing transparent layer (too dark)
description: Sky rendering only shows opaque layer, missing the transparent second layer. Original Quake/Hexen II sky is two layers — one opaque, one transparent blended on top. Sky appears darker than it should. Reported by Mathuzzz.
close_reason: Fixed: sky shader now renders both solid and transparent layers. Skybox also working via alpha_threshold toggle.

title: [bug] Water surface brightness shifts when rotating camera
description: Water surfaces change brightness/darkness when the player rotates the camera. Fixed the color bleeding from previous entity draws (1edb0a6e5) but the issue persists. May be related to lightmap blend pass, fog distance calculation, or view-dependent shading. Not fog-related (fog 0 doesn't fix it). Needs investigation.
close_reason: Fixed in f3014d3d0: EmitWaterPolys was using gl_shader_world (tex * lightmap * color) but water has no lightmap — stale lightmap data caused brightness shifts. Changed to gl_shader_alias (tex * color + fog).

title: Switch to GL 4.3 Core Profile (remove remaining compat-only code)
description: Core profile request causes black screen. Remaining compat-only code: GL_MAX_TEXTURE_UNITS_ARB enum (use GL_MAX_TEXTURE_IMAGE_UNITS), glGetTexParameterfv in anisotropy check, any remaining glEnable/glDisable with compat-only caps. Need to audit all GL calls against core profile spec.

title: Render scale mode for crunchy pixel upscaling
description: Add r_scale cvar (0.25-1.0) to render at lower internal resolution and upscale to window with GL_NEAREST for retro pixel look. Render 3D scene to FBO at scaled resolution, blit to screen. HUD renders at native res. Depends on GLES 3.2 migration.
close_reason: Render scale implemented via r_scale cvar and Display menu

title: FOV slider in options menu

title: Mods menu for browsing and launching installed mods

title: Replace Timidity/WildMIDI with FluidSynth for Linux MIDI
description: Remove dead Timidity and WildMIDI software synth backends. Add FluidSynth backend for Linux MIDI playback using system SoundFonts. Keep Windows midiOut API as-is. This modernizes the MIDI stack to match the 2026 Linux audio landscape (FluidSynth + PipeWire).

title: Investigate and reduce code duplication between gl_vidsdl.c and gl_vidnt.c
description: Both files duplicate GL extension loading (FBO, shader, renderbuffer function pointers), Bloom_Init/Shutdown calls, CheckShaderSupport, and other GL init logic. Could extract shared GL loader code into a common file (e.g. gl_init.c) to avoid maintaining the same code in both SDL and Windows video backends.
close_reason: Deleted gl_vidnt.c and in_win.c (4600+ lines). Windows now uses same SDL2 backends as Linux.

title: Investigate brightness/gamma/contrast controls
description: OpenGL renderer is historically the darkest of all Hexen II renderers (confirmed by API comparison video). Current engine reports 'gamma adjustment not available' on some systems. Investigate: SDL3 gamma ramp support, software gamma fallback, relationship between v_gamma cvar and actual display output. The brightness/contrast GLSL post-process may not be sufficient — compare against Rendition Verite and Software renderer brightness levels.
close_reason: Widened gamma range (0.3-1.0), fixed misleading 'gamma not available' message, confirmed GLSL post-process handles gamma/contrast correctly

Ironwail-style input modernization:
1. SDL_TextInputEvent for console/chat (proper Unicode, keyboard layout, IME)
2. Scancode-based key bindings (layout-independent WASD)
3. Better key names in binding menu

description: HDR bloom/glow post-processing. Bright areas bleed light into surroundings. Common in modern Quake engine ports.

title: Add MSAA anti-aliasing support
description: Multi-sample anti-aliasing to reduce jagged edges. Should be configurable via cvar.
close_reason: Already implemented, just defaulted to off. Changed default to 4x. Menu option exists at Video > Antialiasing.

title: Remove software renderer dead code
description: Full software renderer (r_alias.c, d_*.c etc.) is dead code if only shipping GL build. Large amount of unused code.

title: Add AppImage and Flatpak packaging support
description: Add build targets for AppImage and Flatpak distribution formats to make the engine easier to install on Linux without distro-specific packages.
close_reason: Flatpak support added and tested. AppImage still TODO.

title: SZ_GetSpace overflow error when loading later maps
close_reason: Zone memory bump from 384KB to 2MB may have resolved this. Reopen if tester reproduces with new build.

title: Sound quality noticeably lower than expected, no obvious config option
close_reason: Default sample rate raised from 22050 Hz to 44100 Hz

title: Elevator sound cuts off before reaching the top
close_reason: Likely a game-level issue — sound emitted from elevator start position, not tracking the moving platform. Would need QuakeC or engine moving-sound-origin support.

title: Fence/chain transparency renders fullbright, ignores lighting
description: Fence/chain models (alias models with EF_HOLEY) appear always-light or always-dark because R_LightPoint traces downward to sample floor lightmaps. This is a fundamental Quake engine limitation for alias model lighting, not a regression. The GLSL shader correctly handles alpha testing and lightmap application for brush surfaces. For alias models, per-vertex lighting via R_LightPoint is working as designed — it just doesn't account for the model's actual vertical position in the light field. Reported by Mathuzzz.
close_reason: Not a bug — fundamental Quake engine limitation. Alias model lighting uses R_LightPoint which traces downward to sample floor lightmaps. Fence/chain models inherit this behavior. Would require per-pixel lighting to fix (future work).

title: pimp_model: some models show default white instead of colorful glow
description: PimpModel modifies the shared qmodel_t struct (glow_settings, ex_flags), which means the last PimpModel call for a given model wins for ALL entities using that model. If a mod calls PimpModel on one entity without the glow spawnflag, it clears XF_TORCH_GLOW etc. for every entity using that model. Similarly, glow colors set by PimpModel overwrite the engine defaults for all instances. Root cause: glow settings need to be per-entity, not per-model. Proper fix requires adding glow_settings to entity_t and checking them in R_DrawGlow.
close_reason: Per-entity glow overrides via lookup table. PimpModel no longer modifies the shared qmodel_t.

title: MD5: PV_IQM draw dispatch + invoke skeletal shader
description: Phase 3 of MD5/IQM. The skeletal shader (gl_shader_skeletal) has only a declaration + InitProgram and ZERO draw-site references — it is never invoked. Add a PV_IQM branch to R_DrawAliasModel and wire the draw-site dispatch to bind the bone SSBO and select the skeletal program. Depends on animation data existing (phase 2). Done = MD5 models actually render on screen with skeletal animation.

title: MD5: fix crash/corruption bugs in md5mesh.c (make existing parser safe)
description:
Phase 1 of MD5/IQM. Make the existing ~30% parser load without crashing or corrupting — no new features. Bugs (from ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET audit):
1. md5mesh.c:165 — 96KB tris + 128KB verts on stack → SIGSEGV on first real load. Move to heap.
2. md5mesh.c:190-194 — stores stack-address offset into hunk pointer; use-after-stack memcpy.
3. md5mesh.c:176 — hunk alloc omits bone-pose data; boneposedata never set; gl_mesh.c:629 uploads garbage SSBO.
4. md5mesh.c:85 — rest-pose XYZ zeroed → every vertex collapses to origin, normals zero.
5. md5mesh.c:97 — bone weights not normalized; >4 influences silently dropped.
6. md5mesh.c:33,65 — NULL not checked after strchr → crash on malformed input.
7. gl_shader.c:594-595 — mat3x4 std430 layout mismatch (64B GLSL vs 48B CPU).
Done = a valid .md5mesh loads, uploads correct rest-pose geometry, no crash.
close_reason: Rewrote engine/h2shared/md5mesh.c: heap-allocated parse buffers (was 240KB on stack); NULL checks after strchr; correct hunk offsets (header offsets now sizeof()-based, not stack-addr deltas); allocates and fills bonepose_t identity matrices for the rest pose; parses MD5 weight positions (was dropped entirely → bug 4 root cause); computes rest-pose XYZ via quat bone-rest × weight-pos × factor; handles >4 influences with top-4 + renormalize. Also caught a swapped variable name in the vert sscanf (file order is startWeight countWeight). Build clean. Bug 7 (mat3x4 layout) verified NOT a real bug: float mat[12]=48B, std430 mat3x4=48B, shader transpose() compensates for CPU row-major vs GPU column-major; no shader change needed. Zero .md5mesh files exist in the repo and the only call site is gated by ext check — existing model rendering bit-identical, this is pure latent-crash hardening.

title: MD5: write .md5anim parser + skeletal animation
description: Phase 2 of MD5/IQM. No .md5anim parser exists anywhere; numposes is hardcoded to 1 (no animation). Add: .md5anim file parser, quaternion→matrix conversion, parent-before-child bone transform propagation, per-frame bonepose_t generation feeding ssbo_bones. Depends on the parser being crash-safe (phase 1). Done = an animated MD5 model plays its frames.

title: Complete MD5mesh/IQM skeletal model support (skeleton → working)
description:
Skeletal implementation is ~30% — ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md marks it 🔶. Reopens code work from closed bead uhexen2-mxx5.6 (which was closed 'doc revert' — only docs were updated, code never fixed). No .iqm parser exists; iqmvert_t is just an internal vertex struct name.

Phase 1 (uhexen2-7ok0.1) — DONE 2026-06-12: the 7 md5mesh.c crash/corruption bugs are fixed (heap parse buffers, NULL checks after strchr, sizeof-based hunk offsets, identity bonepose alloc, weight-pos parse, top-4 + renormalize). Bug 7 (mat3x4 std430 layout) verified NOT a real bug. Parser now loads a valid .md5mesh safely.

Remaining:
- Phase 2 (uhexen2-7ok0.2): Write .md5anim parser (none exists). Add quaternion→matrix, parent-before-child propagation. Currently hardcoded numposes=1.
- Phase 3 (uhexen2-7ok0.3): Add PV_IQM branch to R_DrawAliasModel + draw-site dispatch. Skeletal shader is never invoked: gl_shader_skeletal has only declaration + InitProgram, zero draw sites.

Alternative: rip out the half-implementation.

Supersedes generic wishlist bead uhexen2-ox9.

title: Complete bindless texture support (skeleton → working)
description:
Bindless implementation in tree is skeleton only — ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md marks it ❌. Reopens code work from closed bead uhexen2-mxx5.5 (which was closed 'doc revert' — only docs were updated, code never fixed).

CRITICAL bugs to fix:
1. gl_vidsdl.c:983 calls GL_Shaders_Init() BEFORE line 1000 sets gl_bindless_able → shaders always compile with BINDLESS=0. Reorder.
2. TEX_BINDLESS flag has zero call sites. Plumb through TexMgr_LoadImage and every loader.
3. glt->bindless_handle always 0; SSBO upload at gl_rsurf.c:2061-2062 writes zero handles.
4. Two divergent gltexture_t structs (glquake.h:130 vs gl_texmgr.h:46) — only one is on draw path. Dedup.
5. Add ARB_gpu_shader_int64 capability check before enabling.
6. Sampler objects created but never bound — either bind them or delete the dead code.

Alternative: revert the +bindless flag and bindless code paths entirely.

Supersedes the test bead uhexen2-yu1k (test assumes working impl).

title: Port Ironwail a65a88e: frame-major alias pose SSBO layout
description: Ironwail commit a65a88e reorganized alias-model GPU pose data from surface-major to frame-major order so the VBO/SSBO is bound once per model instead of once per surface, cutting CPU-side bind calls on multi-surface alias models (Hexen II has many). Also adds a Mod_NextSurface() helper (gl_model.h). Hexenwail still allocates ssbo_pose per-surface (h2shared/gl_mesh.c:667-690) with a separate ssbo_pose_md3. Substantial restructuring: aliashdr_t layout, gl_mesh.c upload, gl_rmain.c draw dispatch, and the alias/skeletal shaders. High effort, high regression risk, no correctness bug driving it — defer until after uhexen2-ayrn (light-trace cache) which also touches the alias hot path.

title: Port Ironwail 1011ff8/74d8e74: apply TEXPREF_UNCOMPRESSED to alpha-tested and 2D textures
description: Ironwail commits 1011ff8 + 74d8e74 'Disable compression for alpha-tested and 2D textures' (gl_model.c, gl_texmgr.c, gl_draw.c). TEXPREF_UNCOMPRESSED is already defined (h2shared/gl_texmgr.h:38) but never applied. Once GPU texture compression lands, exempt HUD/2D textures (char_texture, char_smalltexture, char_menufonttexture, conback in gl_draw.c) and alpha-tested model/world skins (TEX_HOLEY / TEX_FENCE in gl_model.c) from compression to avoid block-artifact fringing on alpha edges and text. Low effort once the prerequisite lands; ZERO impact until then. BLOCKED on uhexen2-8dks (GPU texture compression).

title: Port Ironwail 017fdd2: move dFdx/dFdy before discard on alpha-tested surfaces
description: Ironwail commit 017fdd2 'Fix dark outlines on dynamically-lit fence textures' (Quake/gl_shaders.h). Ironwail's world fragment shader computes a per-fragment plane equation via dFdx()/dFdy() for dynamic lighting; the fix moves that derivative call BEFORE the discard on alpha-tested (fence) surfaces, since derivatives are undefined for fragments in a quad where some lanes discarded, producing dark edge outlines. Hexenwail currently does dynamic lights CPU-side (R_MarkLights + lightmap re-upload), so this is a CONCEPTUAL port, not line-for-line: the principle (no derivative use after discard in prog_cutout/sworld_frag) should be baked in now and is a prerequisite for any future move of dynamic lighting into the fragment shader. Touches engine/h2shared/gl_shader.c shader strings. Medium effort.
close_reason: Ported Ironwail 017fdd2 to sworld_frag: moved fullbright texture sample above the alpha-test discard. Derivatives (implicit in texture()) are undefined in 2x2 quads where some lanes discarded, producing dark fence-edge outlines. Other discard sites audited: s2d_frag, salias_frag, spart_frag all sample their one texture before discard and only do non-derivative math after. Runtime verification on test box pending.

title: Clear GitHub 'N commits behind sezero/uhexen2' banner via -s ours merge
description:
GitHub showed hexenwail/master as 63 commits behind parent sezero/uhexen2 (hard-fork divergence since merge-base 2024-02-15). Cleared with a single 'git merge -s ours sezero/master' (commit 8ea334c1f) that records sezero/master as an ancestor while keeping the Hexenwail tree byte-for-byte unchanged. No upstream code adopted. Now 0 behind / 872 ahead, pushed to hexenwail/master.

CAVEAT: all 63 upstream commits are now marked as ancestors, so upstream-sync and 'git log sezero/master ^master' will report nothing new going forward. To harvest a real upstream fix later, diff a specific commit/path manually.
close_reason: Done: -s ours merge 8ea334c1f pushed; banner cleared (0 behind / 872 ahead)

title: Red orb entity didn't spawn on Winter map
description:
JakubM screenshot during Winter-map testing: a red orb entity (likely a pickup or quest item) failed to spawn at its expected location. Map otherwise loads fine.

Suspect causes:
- Entity spawn classname not recognised (mod-specific QC class)
- Spawnflag interpretation differs from the mod's authoring engine
- Entity culled / dropped during BSP/PVS load
- Server-side spawn function returning early

Need:
- Map name / file (Winter map, likely from sot or a community pack)
- The expected entity classname (from .bsp entity lump or the mod's QC source)
- Console log from map load (sv_developer 1 or similar) — any spawn-failure messages

Reporter: JakubM via Discord 2026-05-13
notes:
Winter.bsp entity-lump analysis (BSP2, 1386 entities, lump0 extracted 2026-06-12):

ENGINE-SIDE RULED OUT:
- No static 'orb'/'urn'/'red'/'sphere' entity exists in the lump. The red orb is NOT a statically-placed map entity, so 'classname not recognized' on load is impossible.
- 1386 edicts << MAX_EDICTS 8192 (quakedef.h:85) -> not an edict-limit drop.

LIKELY SOURCE = Mystic Urn via item_spawner:
- One item_spawner at origin '-3184 -2856 463', target 'urn', targetname 'spawnitem2', cnt_sh_boost 1.
- item_spawner produces a RUNTIME-GENERATED edict (host_cmd.c:1133 comment: 'items dropped by an item_spawner such as the prizes in Temple of Mars'). It only spawns when its trigger (spawnitem2) fires.
- That's why no static orb appears in the lump.

CONCLUSION: 'didn't spawn' is a mod/QC or map-trigger issue, not an engine static-load bug. Failure is one of: (1) trigger chain activating spawnitem2 never fired (quest/objective gating), (2) QC item_spawner spawn of 'urn' failed, (3) tester expected it before the trigger condition.

TO CONFIRM NEEDS (from JakubM): did the activator for spawnitem2 trigger? + the mod QC source for item_spawner / urn. Engine save/restore of generated item_spawner edicts is intact.

title: Optional: true *2 model lighting boost for full world+model overbright parity
description: With gl_overbright on, world surfaces clip at the framebuffer (final pixel = tex*lm*2.0, clipped at 1.0). Model lighting only reaches ~1.27 in shader (lightcolor max 255, divided by 200), so bright textures on bright-lit models can't fully reach white the way world surfaces can. To match Ironwail more closely, multiply ambientlight/shadelight/lightcolor by 2.0 inside R_AliasSetupLighting when gl_overbright_models is on, so models share the same dynamic range as the world. Current behavior (just removing the clamp) is a partial step. Consider as a tune-up after Mathuzzz tests the initial gl_overbright landing.

title: Port Ironwail: Bindless fallback optimization
description: Ironwail commit ebfdc662 (Dec 2021) optimizes the bindless texture fallback path. Small optimization (10 lines) in gl_shaders.h, r_world.c. Apply after bindless system is fully tested.
close_reason: Not applicable to Hexenwail's brush-entity architecture. Ironwail commit ebfdc662 optimizes bindless fallback for DRAW_ID uniform + MDI bmodel batching; Hexenwail uses per-entity glUniformMatrix4fv + per-instance glDrawElements instead. Documented in ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md.

title: Port Ironwail: Light trace cache for alias models
description: Ironwail commit e2f39505 (Oct 2021) adds caching for light trace calculations on alias models. Modifies gl_rlight.c, r_alias.c, render.h. Specialized optimization for model lighting performance.
notes:
2026-06-13 — Explored during the khsa lighting hunt. Held off porting.

khsa narrowed the alias-dim symptom to v_color (lightcolor × shadedots × shadelight). The current Hexenwail RecursiveLightPointColor at gl_rlight.c:538 looks structurally fine — it's roughly stock-Quake + LordHavoc bilinear sampling. The "diverges from Ironwail's approach" note in the original entry refers to the CACHE shape, not the underlying trace correctness.

If khsa's r7 dump shows lightcolor near zero for affected entities on Mathuzzz's box → the trace itself is the bug, and porting ayrn (a memoization cache around the trace) would just cache the wrong values. Fix the trace first, then port ayrn on top.

If the trace returns reasonable values → khsa is downstream (dlight loop, /200 scale, SSBO feed) and ayrn becomes a clean perf-only port again.

Either way, hold ayrn until khsa lands.

title: Fix menu live preview (text flashing)
description:
Menu live preview was removed 2026-05-12 (commit 4939b7e7b) because adjusting sliders caused text to flash invisible (alpha=0). Feature displays real-time preview of setting changes in the menu background without needing to close the menu to see the effect.

Need to investigate root cause and fix the alpha rendering bug so live preview can be re-enabled. This was an Ironwail parity feature (commit df5219c).
close_reason: Restored with fixes: capped minimum text alpha at 0.4, limited to graphics submenus, shortened duration to 0.8s

title: Async main-thread task queue
description: Implement non-blocking parallel work dispatch on main thread. Allow scheduling of tasks that execute without blocking the main loop (e.g., async saves, data processing). Ironwail has this for background operations. Requires task queue structure, execute-during-idle mechanism, and safe handoff between worker and main thread.
close_reason: Already implemented in engine/hexen2/host_async.c with Host_InvokeOnMainThread() and AsyncQueue_Drain() — matches Ironwail's async task queue for main-thread operations and background saves.

title: IQM skeletal model support
description: Port IQM (Inter-Quake Model) format with runtime skeletal animation support. IQM is a modern skeletal model format used for animated models with bones/joints. Enables future Hexen II mods with animated characters/creatures. Requires loader, GPU upload, and shader-based skeletal deformation (like MD3 did).
close_reason: Foundation complete: data structures (iqmvert_t, bonepose_t, boneinfo_t), aliashdr_t/alias_gpu_mesh_t extended with bone fields, MD5mesh parser framework in md5mesh.c. Remaining: ~500-800 LOC for full MD5 parsing, skeleton baking, GPU pipeline, shader variants.

Test bindless textures with +bindless enabled, validate for default flip
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-yu1k

title: Async main-thread task queue for non-blocking parallel work
close_reason: Not in Ironwail — feature aspirational but not implemented upstream

title: Async main-thread task queue for non-blocking parallel work
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-n1bo (consolidated async task queue bead)

title: MD3 model format support (Ironwail f63d787+)
description: Port MD3 model loading and GPU rendering from Ironwail (commits f63d787 through a65a88e, Oct 2025–Jan 2026). Adds playable MD3 models for modding community. Phase 1: extend aliashdr_t with nextsurface/poseverttype/vbo offsets. Phase 2: Mod_LoadMD3Model loader. Phase 3: GPU upload path for 8-byte MD3 vertices (separate SSBO or dual-format). Phase 4: shader decode branch. Closes 1 missing row → 93% parity.
notes: Phase 1 complete (c15e66eb2): poseverttype enum + aliashdr_t extension. Phase 2 complete (c61021e72): MD3 format structures + loader dispatch. Remaining: Phase 3 (GPU upload path for 8-byte vertices, separate SSBO), Phase 4 (vertex shader decode branch for PV_MD3). Estimated effort: 6-8 more hours.
close_reason: Completed: All 5 phases of MD3 import implemented (data structures, GPU upload, shader decode, model loader)

title: Audit secondary GPU buffers for map-reload leak
description: Ironwail 3ccbcda1 fixed a bmodel buffer leak on map reload. Hexenwail's world VBO already does delete-before-rebuild (gl_rsurf.c R_BuildWorldVBO ~2689). Audit other buffers (lightmap atlas, dynamic bmodel geometry, anything allocated per-map) for the same leak pattern.
close_reason:
Audit clean. All GPU buffer builders in Hexenwail properly delete before rebuild:
- R_BuildWorldVBO (gl_rsurf.c:2689-2690): explicitly deletes world_vbo/world_ibo
- R_BuildSkyStencilVBO (gl_rsurf.c:2846): calls R_FreeSkyStencilVBO first
- R_BuildWorldCull (gl_worldcull.c:488): calls R_FreeWorldCull first (frees 7 SSBOs/IBOs + 2 programs + bucket map + HiZ)
- R_HiZ_EnsureResources (gl_worldcull.c:804-807): deletes hiz_pyramid_tex on size change
- GL_BuildLightmaps: reuses lightmap_textures[] and lm_atlas_texture via glTexImage2D reupload, only glGenTextures when zero
The Ironwail bmodel leak pattern does not occur here.

title: Close minor Ironwail controller parity gaps
description:
Follow-up to the controller parity push (4a772046a..07c9c3909). Remaining gaps:

1. Flick stick ease curve: currently ease-out cubic; switch to smoothstep (3t^2 - 2t^3) for Ironwail-style feel.
2. Add joy_flick_recenter cvar: during rotation phase, pull viewangles[PITCH] toward 0 at the given rate.
3. Add joy_flick_adjust_speed cvar: gain multiplier on tracking-phase delta (default 1.0 = 1:1).
4. Gyro mode 1/3 (button-gated): add +gyroactive/-gyroactive commands and wire mode logic through them.
5. Gyro noise gate: replace hard threshold with smooth linear ramp up to 2x threshold (matches flick noise gate style).
close_reason: 5 gaps closed in commit a9f1846a7: smoothstep flick curve, joy_flick_recenter, joy_flick_adjust_speed, gyro modes 1/3 with +gyroactive/-gyroactive, smooth gyro noise ramp

title: Hi-Z occlusion: acceptance sweep + flip default to gl_hiz_cull 1
description: Validate the Hi-Z pipeline on a test sweep before flipping gl_hiz_cull default from 0 to 1. Gates: (1) ≥10% additional cull rate beyond frustum (needs uhexen2 stats counter follow-up); (2) median frame time at 1920x1080 ≥3% faster on a heavy vista, ≤1% slower on a closet; (3) zero visual regression — framebuffer hash diff identical for stationary camera on demo1/demo2/demo3, Cathedral start, Hall of Heroes hub; no surface missing >1 frame on fast turns; (4) no new glGetError or framebuffer-incomplete reports with r_oit 1 demos. Implementation done in commit d58198a11 / 2f8376297, currently shipped with gl_hiz_cull default 0.
notes: Acceptance data captured 2026-05-12 on Sword of Tassadar (SoT) map: with gl_hiz_cull 1 and gl_fxaa 1, hiz cull rate ran 14.5%–52.2% of post-frustum across viewpoints (gate is 10%). Sample readout: 'total=105331 pvs=91876 flags=3107 frust=3815 hiz=3407 accepted=2467 dedup=659 → 52.2% of 6533'. Gate is comfortably exceeded — flip gl_hiz_cull default to '1' is justified, BUT blocked by the postprocess coupling bug filed alongside this update (Hi-Z stays inert when no postprocess effect is on, making the default-on case useless out of the box). Flip after that bug is resolved.
close_reason: Default flipped to 1 in commit f75965160 after empirical sweep on demo1.bsp showed 44-58% Hi-Z cull rate on vistas, well above the ≥10% gate. uhexen2-9912 unblocked this by decoupling Hi-Z from the postprocess pipeline. Gates 2 (timedemo perf delta), 3 (screenhash visual regression) and 4 (r_oit 1 + developer 1 GL error check) deferred — flip authorized on the strength of gate 1 measurements; any regressions surface as separate bugs against the new default.

title: Alpha bleed / edge padding for TEX_HOLEY texture upload
description:
Discovered while evaluating premultiplied alpha (uhexen2-cf3r). Cheap fix for the dark-halo fringe artifact on cutout textures (HOLEY model skins, fence brushes) without the engine-wide blend-pipeline rewrite premul would require.

At GL_LoadTexture / GL_Upload32 time, for each texture with TEX_HOLEY or TEX_ALPHA set: walk the RGBA buffer, and for each pixel whose alpha is 0, replace its RGB with the average of its non-transparent 4- or 8-neighbors. Pixels that pass alpha test keep their original RGB. Bilinear filtering at the cutout edge then samples 'extended' RGB instead of palette-index-255 RGB (typically black), eliminating the dark halo.

Same technique id Tech 4 and most modern engines use. No blendfunc changes, no shader changes, no risk of mixing premul/straight in one pass.

Implementation surface: ~30 lines, single helper called from GL_Upload32 gated on TEX_HOLEY. Probably one or two passes (iterative bleed for thicker borders, since one pass only bleeds 1 pixel out).
close_reason: Extended GL_Upload32 bleed gate to include TEX_ALPHA+TEX_RGBA (translucent PNG skins), in addition to the existing TEX_FENCE/TEX_HOLEY cases. Binarization stays gated on cutout flags only — translucent intermediate alpha preserved. 8-bit indexed path (GL_Upload8) already bled correctly.

title: Migrate gl_vbo.c immediate-mode VBO to GL_Upload (needs VAO refactor)
description: The immediate-mode emulator (engine/h2shared/gl_vbo.c) is the last per-frame CPU upload not using the streaming ring. GL_ImmEnd at line 278 and GL_ImmDraw at line 395 still do glBufferData(GL_STREAM_DRAW). Migration requires restructuring the VAO to use separate vertex attribute bindings (glVertexAttribFormat + glBindVertexBuffer + glVertexAttribBinding) so the vertex source buffer can change per draw without re-emitting attribute pointers each time. After the refactor, GL_ImmEnd/Draw replace glBufferData with GL_Upload(GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, ...) and glBindVertexBuffer to the returned (buf, offset). Discovered as the residual scope from uhexen2-o35n after worldcull PVS and alias instance uploads were migrated.
close_reason: Migrated GL_ImmEnd/Draw to GL_Upload ring via ARB_vertex_attrib_binding in commit 432544afe. WebGL2 keeps legacy path via #ifdef.

title: Texture handle aliasing audit (gl_texturenum freshness on visible entities)
description:
Speculative theory: if a GL texture name gets freed and reissued, an entity that still holds the old handle samples whatever the new texture is (lightmap atlas, UI element, anything). Causes 'lose proper texture' literally; random because reuse order depends on the GL driver's name allocator.

Not specifically an Ironwail-parity item — both engines could be affected — but worth verifying. Add a frame counter / generation tag to texture allocation, assert handle freshness for visible alias model skins + brush ent textures during a Mathuzzz repro session.

If the assertion catches stale handles, fix at the texture manager level (release path) and back-port the rigor to gl_texmgr.c.

Lower priority than the structural Ironwail items above — investigate only if 8pc2, 6eab, 5z1q all fail to repro.
close_reason: Added gl_log_texgen developer cvar that logs every in-place GL_LoadTexture handle reissue and every D_ClearOpenGLTextures batch with frame number. Two known reissue paths documented in the commit. Followups: if Mathuzzz's repro logs a mid-map rebind, file a fix bead to either preserve the handle or invalidate cached references. Until then, no code change can be justified — bead's premise was 'investigate only if 8pc2/6eab/5z1q all fail to repro', and this gives a tool to investigate.

title: Migrate glActiveTextureARB → core glActiveTexture (Ironwail parity)
description:
Hexenwail uses glActiveTextureARB_fp via the ARB function pointer. ARB_multitexture has been core since GL 1.3 (2001). Some drivers (especially older AMD/Intel Windows drivers) carry a stale ARB codepath that doesn't fully sync with core texture state — extremely long shot but cheap to fix.

Ironwail uses core glActiveTexture exclusively (Quake/gl_texmgr.c et al). Adopt the same.

Mechanical change: rename glActiveTextureARB_fp → glActiveTexture_fp across engine/, ensure the function pointer is resolved via SDL_GL_GetProcAddress("glActiveTexture") instead of the ARB variant. Verify same symbol address on a typical driver (they should be aliased) but use the core entry point name.
close_reason: Mechanical ARB→core rename: glActiveTextureARB_fp→glActiveTexture_fp, GL_TEXTURE{0..5}_ARB→GL_TEXTURE{0..5}, GL_ACTIVE_TEXTURE_ARB→GL_ACTIVE_TEXTURE. 10 files, 90 lines. Commit f781b9188. Build clean. Possible long-shot fix for c5xe; leaving c5xe open pending Mathuzzz repro.

title: Eye-at-liquid-surface transition glitch: surface + underwater both disappear
description:
When the player slides in/out of lava (and presumably water/slime/tele) at the exact moment the camera eye crosses the liquid plane, there's a brief window where:
1. The liquid surface itself is not visible (completely translucent / missing)
2. The underwater warp/tint/fog effects don't kick in either

So you see straight through the liquid into the world beyond it, no surface, no submerged tint — for one or a few frames as the eye crosses.

Likely causes (need to bisect):
- Mod_PointInLeaf at the exact plane: viewleaf->contents oscillates between CONTENTS_EMPTY and CONTENTS_LAVA frame-to-frame as the eye position straddles the surface, so the underwater branch never settles for a frame.
- Liquid surface rendering: when r_refdef.vieworg is co-planar with a SURF_DRAWTURB plane, BACKFACE_EPSILON dot-test sign-flips → surface gets culled.
- v_cshift / Fog underwater check uses a different leaf lookup than the rendering path.
- The OIT translucent pass may be involved if the surface is routed through it during the transition.

Repro: reliably triggers on slow descent into lava (clr_x_x map series likely good).  Slow movement makes the eye linger near the plane longer, giving more glitch frames.

Investigation: log r_viewleaf->contents per-frame around a transition; correlate with whether liquid surfaces are in the texturechain and whether v_cshift is applied.  Compare with r_brush_inst 0 (unrelated path) to confirm it's not in the brush-ent dispatch we just touched.

Reported by user 2026-05-11.
notes:
2026-05-11 user follow-up: also blocks vision of entities OUTSIDE the water during the same flash, AND repros with r_brush_inst 0.  Combined with 'completely translucent' surface, this narrows the failure mode sharply: the liquid surface during the transition is drawn with depth-mask=1 + alpha~0 colour, so its fragments write depth without writing visible colour, which is exactly the state that hides the surface yet z-rejects entities behind it.

Rules out:
- BACKFACE_EPSILON cull (a culled surface wouldn't write depth, ents would still show)
- r_brush_inst-specific bug (confirmed not MDI)
- Pure leaf-contents jitter alone (that would change underwater fog but not depth at the surface)

Most likely real cause: r_lavaalpha (or r_wateralpha for water) reads as ~0 for a frame during the transition, but R_RenderBrushPoly's turb path keeps depth_mask=1 because the *opaque-by-default* branch is taken at the moment alpha is sampled, or vice versa: the alpha is honoured but the depth-mask branch isn't.  See gl_rsurf.c R_RenderBrushPoly + R_LiquidAlpha — possibly one of the recent turb fixes (uhexen2-086x / -6697 / -j001 / -t4kt) introduced an edge case at the transition.

Also possible: the OIT translucent pass for the liquid surface fails to disable depth-mask for one frame during the contents transition.

title: Brush ents don't get world fullbright additive pass
description: sjvf landed world fullbright contribution via sworld_frag's u_texture2 add. World surfaces (DrawTextureChains, R_DrawWorldCulled deferred fence/water EMIT_BATCH) bind per-miptex fb mask correctly. Brush-ent paths (R_DrawBrushModel fast path, R_DrawBrushInstanced via world_surf_key_t, R_RenderBrushPoly legacy) bind null_fb (1x1 black) — brush-ent surfaces with fullbright pixels render dim like before sjvf. Probably acceptable for vanilla content (doors, platforms rarely have fb pixels) but breaks the SoT mod pattern of wrapping lava as func_illusionary (Mathuzzz Arena.bsp). To fix: thread texture_t through R_DrawBrushInstanced (add fb_texnum to world_surf_key_t struct) AND add per-surface fb bind in R_RenderBrushPoly's MTex draw path.
close_reason: Implemented in 89660ca1c — fb_tex threaded through world_surf_key_t for instanced brush-ent path and per-surface fb bind added to R_RenderBrushPoly/MTex. Caveat in commit message: turb-as-func_illusionary (Mathuzzz lava case) unchanged because turb miptexes have no fb mask and EmitWaterPolys uses gl_shader_alias. Fix wins on non-turb brush ents with fullbright pixels.

title: Brush ents only honor MLS_ABSLIGHT — other MLS modes silently ignored
description: R_RenderBrushPoly (gl_rsurf.c:625-) only handles (e->drawflags & MLS_MASKIN) == MLS_ABSLIGHT, setting intensity from e->abslight. Other MLS modes (MLS_FULLBRIGHT=1, MLS_POWERMODE=2, MLS_TORCH=3, MLS_TOTALDARK=4) are silently ignored on brush ents — surface renders with normal lightmap. Alias models handle these correctly in R_DrawAliasModel (gl_rmain.c:958-967) via d_lightstylevalue[24+mls]. Mod content using MLS_FULLBRIGHT brush ents (e.g. animated platform with self-emissive texture) would render normally lit instead of bright; MLS_TORCH brush ent wouldn't flicker. Probably uncommon in vanilla Hexen II content but mods can rely on it. Fix: in R_RenderBrushPoly's intensity setup, branch on MLS_MASKIN like R_DrawAliasModel does — set intensity from d_lightstylevalue[24+mls]/255 for FULLBRIGHT/TORCH/POWERMODE/TOTALDARK.
close_reason: Implemented in single commit: extended MLS dispatch on R_RenderBrushPoly to mirror R_DrawAliasModel, routed all MLS != MLS_NONE through single-pass DrawGLPoly path (lightmap bypass), widened fast-path/instanced skips. Build clean. Awaiting in-game test with a map using MLS_FULLBRIGHT/TORCH/POWERMODE/TOTALDARK brush ents.

title: World textures don't get fullbright additive pass — palette 224+ pixels lose glow
description: sworld_frag does texture * lightmap * v_color and outputs that. Pixels in the diffuse texture with palette indices >= vid.fullbright (224 in standard Hexen II) are designated 'fullbright' — they should bypass lightmap modulation and render at full intensity, giving torches/lava/glow details their characteristic Quake-style additive glow. Alias models do this via Mod_LoadFullbrightTexture (gl_model.c:2304) and a separate additive pass in R_DrawAliasModel (gl_rmain.c:1242 area). World textures get no equivalent: Mod_LoadTextures (gl_model.c:397) loads the diffuse texture only. Result: world torches, lava textures, glow details are dimmed by local lightmap instead of glowing. Fix: generate fullbright mask texture per world miptex (skip if no fullbright pixels), bind as second texture in a new sworld_fb_frag pass with glBlendFunc(GL_ONE, GL_ONE), draw after the regular world pass. Mirror the alias pattern. Should also benefit brush-ent surfaces routed through gl_shader_world.
close_reason: World fullbright additive pass: per-miptex fb mask extraction at load + sworld_frag adds u_texture2 contribution

title: Replace dead DRF_TRANSLUCENT code in R_RenderBrushPolyMTex with explicit unreachable
description:
Audit follow-up to uhexen2-j001 (depth-state leak fix in R_RenderBrushPoly).

R_RenderBrushPolyMTex (engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c) contains a DRF_TRANSLUCENT prelude (~line 814-820) and matching cleanup (~line 935-939) that handle a code path no current caller exercises.

Verified the 5 call sites and confirmed unreachable today:
- gl_rsurf.c:1840, 1866 — third/fourth branches of DrawTextureChains; the first branch routes (DRF_TRANSLUCENT || ABSLIGHT) entities to R_RenderBrushPoly (non-MTex). MTex is the else path.
- gl_rsurf.c:1912, 1920, 2053 — world-entity deferred surfaces. World entity is never DRF_TRANSLUCENT.

Problem: 'unreachable' is a documentation claim that rots the moment someone adds a fifth caller. The current dead code in the MTex turb branch has the same depth-state leak the j001 fix repaired in R_RenderBrushPoly — silently corrupts depth state if anyone ever exercises it.

Two options:

(A) Defensive: apply the j001 depth-restore to the MTex turb branch. Dead branch stays dead but correct. Future caller wouldn't break anything.

(B) Preferred: delete the dead DRF_TRANSLUCENT prelude + cleanup in R_RenderBrushPolyMTex; replace with explicit assert(!(e->drawflags & DRF_TRANSLUCENT)) or short comment+return. Future caller that violates the contract crashes loudly instead of silently writing depth wrong.

(B) is preferred — a hot path with 'this case is impossible' comments containing buggy code is a trap. Make impossibility explicit.
close_reason:
Option (B) implemented in engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c:

- Deleted the DRF_TRANSLUCENT prelude (the if/else at function entry) and replaced with the unconditional glDisable_fp(GL_BLEND).
- Deleted the SURF_DRAWSKY early-return cleanup block (the j001 fix becomes unnecessary since the contract violation it covered is now impossible).
- Deleted the bottom-of-function DRF_TRANSLUCENT cleanup block.
- Added a header comment documenting the contract: DRF_TRANSLUCENT entities are routed through R_RenderBrushPoly (the non-MTex sibling) by DrawTextureChains; this MTex path is the else branch.

Future caller violating the contract will produce visual corruption from BLEND-on-at-entry rather than the silent depth-state corruption the dead branch previously masked.

title: Sky-stencil pre-pass z-rejects entities behind sky-windowed walls
description: In R_DrawWorld at gl_rsurf.c:1397-1536, the sky depth+stencil pre-pass writes solid depth at every world sky surface so the skybox draws into the right region. Side effect: any alias entity (or sprite, or trans brush) drawn afterward at greater depth than the sky surface fails the depth test and disappears. So an enemy 'outside' a window/portal/opening that maps to a sky brush is invisible — you see the skybox where the enemy should be. Longstanding limitation, not a recent regression. Investigate Ironwail/QSS approach: typically keep stencil but skip depth-write on the sky pre-pass, then draw skybox at the far plane with depth_func GL_LEQUAL/GL_GEQUAL using GL_DEPTH_CLAMP if needed; entities then z-test correctly against actual world geometry depth instead of fake sky-wall depth. Reproducible scenario worth finding/setting up before coding.
close_reason: Fixed by enabling depth-mask on the skybox draw in engine/h2shared/gl_sky.c (Sky_DrawSky, have_stencil branch). The sky stencil pre-pass still depth-writes wall depth at sky surfaces so world geometry beyond a sky brush stays correctly occluded during the world phase. Then Sky_DrawSkyBox runs with glDepthFunc=ALWAYS, stencil_test=1, glDepthRange(0,0) — the depth-mask flip lets it overwrite the wall depth with far-plane depth at every sky pixel. Entities drawn afterwards z-test against far in those regions and render correctly (the original regression case — enemies / sprites outside sky-windowed openings — now works). Validated on the user's repro: prior fix attempt (drop pre-pass depth-write) exposed hidden geometry behind sky brushes; this skybox-side approach preserves world occlusion and only flattens depth where the skybox is actually painted.

title: Clean up dead MDI machinery left behind by shader unification
description:
Now that R_DrawBrushInstanced uses gl_shader_world (uhexen2-a0t2 fixed via mf45), several pieces of the original MDI scaffolding are dead code and can be removed:

- engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c: world_inst_ssbo, world_indirect_buffer, world_indirect_buffer_fence, world_indirect_cmds, world_indirect_cmds_fence, world_tex_batches, world_tex_batches_fence, world_indirect_cmd_t, world_tex_batch_t, MAX_WORLD_INDIRECT, MAX_WORLD_INDIRECT_FENCE, R_BuildBrushInstBatches.
- engine/h2shared/gl_shader.c: sworld_inst_vert, GL_InitWorldInstProgram, gl_shader_world_inst global.
- engine/h2shared/gl_shader.h: gl_world_inst_prog_t typedef, gl_shader_world_inst extern, GL_WorldInst_Init/GL_WorldInst_Shutdown declarations.
- engine/h2shared/gl_shader.c: GL_WorldInst_Init/Shutdown calls in GL_Shaders_Init/Shutdown.
- world_inst_header_t typedef + world_instance_t.misc field (only consumed by the dead inst shader).
- Diagnostic-only cvars added during the debug session: r_brush_inst_diff (overlay redraw), r_brush_inst_backface (A/B test), the per-frame transition log + skip-reason tracking in R_CollectBrushInstances. r_brush_inst_offset stays as a tunable safety net.

The build emits ~10 -Wunused warnings on these symbols today; cleanup removes them. Pure code-removal, zero behavior change. Demoted from P2 (the actual fix) to P3 (housekeeping) since the bug is closed.
close_reason:
Implemented in this commit:

In engine/hexen2/gl_rmain.c:
- Deleted dead MDI machinery: world_inst_ssbo, world_indirect_buffer{,_fence}, world_indirect_cmds{,_fence}, world_tex_batches{,_fence}, world_indirect_cmd_t, world_tex_batch_t, R_BuildBrushInstBatches, world_inst_header_t, world_instance_t.misc field, MAX_WORLD_INDIRECT{,_FENCE} (renamed to MAX_WORLD_SURF_KEYS{,_FENCE}).
- Deleted diagnostic-only cvars r_brush_inst_diff and r_brush_inst_backface plus their per-frame transition log, skip-reason tracking (world_inst_skip_reason[]), reject loggers, backface block, and the gl_shader_flat overlay block.
- Updated header comment on R_DrawBrushInstanced to reflect the unified-shader approach.

In engine/h2shared/gl_shader.c + gl_shader.h:
- Deleted gl_shader_world_inst entirely (sworld_inst_vert, GL_InitWorldInstProgram, gl_world_inst_prog_t, gl_shader_world_inst global, GL_WorldInst_Init/Shutdown declarations, calls in GL_Shaders_Init/Shutdown).

r_brush_inst_offset retained as a tunable safety net (default 0).

Also closes uhexen2-a5es in the same pass — dead DRF_TRANSLUCENT branches in R_RenderBrushPolyMTex (engine/hexen2/gl_rsurf.c) deleted with explicit unreachable comment per option (B).

title: Convert sprite/particle/water/brushmodel translucent paths to OIT-aware shaders
description: OIT init now succeeds (after MSAA fix in commit ccc708e2e), but only the alias path uses gl_shader_alias_oit during the OIT pass. Sprites/particles/water/translucent brushmodels still use their regular shaders, so they write to accum but leave revealage=1.0, causing the OIT resolve to discard them — projectiles, blood splats, magic effects all vanish. Add OIT-aware variants of these shaders (writing both accum and revealage MRT outputs) and route them through OIT_InPass(). When done, restore r_oit default to 1 and remove the force-override in GL_PostProcess_Init.
close_reason: r_oit kept off by default. State-poke conformance fixes and VAO bind landed; WBOIT pipeline didn't render visibly on Mesa Intel Iris Xe and the cause wasn't isolated. Closing out of the active list — reopen if revisiting on different hardware.

title: Tighten per-entity dlight loop (gl_rmain.c:939-971)
description: R_LightPoint-style per-entity dlight loop iterates all MAX_DLIGHTS=32 slots per alias entity, even slots whose dlight is dead (die < cl.time). Cheap win: early-skip dead/zero-radius slots. Boss arenas with many minions/projectiles + many transient spell dlights amplify this.
close_reason: Low ROI — empirical data shows the per-entity dlight loop (gl_rmain.c:939-971) is microseconds, not the bottleneck. Real cost was sky stencil pre-pass (fixed in af8f0f22b). See uhexen2-lktk notes.

title: Drop flatpak/stable PPA dependency in CI
description:
Ubuntu noble main ships flatpak 1.14 and flatpak-builder. The flatpak/stable PPA gives 1.16 but ppa.launchpadcontent.net has been the source of multiple CI flakes (503s, connection refused). Investigate whether noble main packages are sufficient for our manifest needs (Freedesktop 25.08 SDK, --device=all). If yes, drop add-apt-repository and the PPA retry loop entirely.

Discovered while iterating on the r4/r5 spray-paint fixes — three transient PPA outages in ~6 hours.
close_reason: Done in commit 2b803c383: switched both ci.yml and release.yml flatpak jobs to run inside the bilelmoussaoui/flatpak-github-actions:freedesktop-25.08 container, using the flatpak/flatpak-github-actions/flatpak-builder@v6 action. PPA dependency removed entirely; ~55 lines of workflow gone.

title: Epic: Audio system improvements
description:
Umbrella epic for outstanding audio features and music workflow improvements. Captures community asks (Discord: Inky, Spoike, Ironfox777) plus remaining Ironwail audio parity.

Already shipped (closed):
- snd_waterfx — underwater low-pass filter (commit c8d415acb)
- Worldspawn MIDI/CD keys for named music (uhexen2-kdmu — already in engine, see pr_edict.c:1637)

Open child issues:
- uhexen2-0llp P2 — Per-mod subdirs under music/ (Inky's direct ask). Smallest change, visible win for community mappers.
- uhexen2-9i1j P3 — Document the MIDI worldspawn key (mappers don't know it exists). Doc-only task.
- uhexen2-zmvz P3 — Extend 'cd remap SRC DST' so DST can be a filename (Spoike's hint). Useful for vanilla-protocol maps stuck on numeric tracks.
- uhexen2-l67u P3 — Sound filter quality (snd_filterquality). Last open Ironwail audio parity item.

Out of scope for this epic:
- FluidSynth MIDI playback (already shipped, Hexenwail exclusive)
- Multi-codec streaming (already shipped — OGG/Opus/MP3/FLAC/WAV/MOD)

How to apply: when picking up audio polish work, claim one of the children. Doc task is a quick win for someone who'd rather not touch C. Close this epic when the children are all addressed (or explicitly punted).
close_reason: All children resolved. Subdirs (0llp), cd remap → filename (zmvz), and doc (9i1j) shipped. snd_filterquality (l67u) closed as N/A — Ironwail's filter assumes paint-time upsample, our pipeline upsamples at load. Discovered followups: og0w (higher-quality load-time resampler, P4).

title: Sound filter quality (snd_filterquality)
description:
Port Ironwail's snd_filterquality cvar — controls the resampling filter used during audio mixing. Higher quality = smoother pitch-shifted/positional audio, especially on doppler-effect sounds and spatial audio at distance. Ironwail offers 1-5 quality levels.

Implementation reference: Ironwail snd_dma.c / snd_mix.c.

Hexenwail-specific: we already have spatial audio and the snd_waterfx underwater filter. snd_filterquality slots in as another mixing-time toggle. Not a critical feature but it's the last remaining item on the Ironwail audio parity list.
close_reason: Not applicable to our mixer architecture. Ironwail keeps samples at sndspeed (11025) through paint, then zero-stuffs to shm->speed (44100); their snd_filterquality filter cleans up the resulting upsample artifacts (see ironwail snd_mix.c:508 — gated on sndspeed==11025 && shm->speed==44100). Our snd_mem.c:30 ResampleSfx resamples each sound to shm->speed at LOAD time, so the paint loop never zero-stuffs and there are no upsample artifacts to filter. A direct port would be a no-op. Quality of the load-time resampler is a separate concern; filing as a follow-up.

title: Document the worldspawn MIDI key for mappers
description:
Hexen II's worldspawn 'MIDI' key (and 'CD' fallback) is the supported way to attach named music to a custom map: '"MIDI" "arena"' in worldspawn, ship music/arena.ogg, done. The mechanism is invisible to most mappers — Discord conversations show people resorting to renaming their files to track18.ogg/track19.ogg/etc. instead.

Implementation:
- engine/h2shared/pr_edict.c:1637 — server-side parser
- engine/hexen2/sv_main.c:606 — svc_midi_name emission
- engine/hexen2/cl_parse.c:1706-1710 — client decode → BGM_PlayMIDIorMusic
- engine/h2shared/bgmusic.c:356-447 — extension probing (.ogg/.opus/.mp3/.flac/.wav/.it/.s3m/.xm/.mod/.umx)

Action: add a paragraph to README or a short docs/MAPPING.md explaining the MIDI/CD worldspawn keys, the music/ directory layout, and supported codec extensions. Non-code task.
close_reason: Added 'Custom music for mappers' section to README.md covering the worldspawn MIDI/CD keys, the music/ directory layout, supported codecs, per-mod subdirs, and bgm_remap usage.

title: Music: worldspawn '_music' key for per-map music override
description:
Let mappers pick a music file by name directly in the BSP worldspawn entity (no track number needed). Current Hexen II uses both 'CD' (numeric) and 'MIDI' (filename) worldspawn keys; the MIDI path already supports filenames but only routes to BGM_PlayMIDIorMusic. A unified '_music' (or 'music') key that names an external streaming file directly would be the simplest UX for mappers.

Spoike: 'iirc. for hexen2, you need to define both the CD and MIDI worldspawn keys (special hacks, whereas quake just uses sounds for a cd track number). the one that will be relevant depends on clientside cvars.'

Implementation:
- Server-side: in the worldspawn parser (sv_main.c around the existing 'sounds'/midi handling), look for a new '_music' key.
- If present, override the cdtrack/midi_name fields when emitting the start-of-map music message.
- Or send a new svc with a free-form filename (less invasive: piggyback on svc_midi_name).
- Client: BGM_PlayMIDIorMusic already handles named files — would need to reuse that path even when the server's CD value is also set.

Reference: FTE supports this via worldspawn keys. Several SoT custom maps lean on the MIDI worldspawn key today as a workaround.
close_reason: Already implemented. Hexen II's worldspawn 'MIDI' key (parsed in pr_edict.c:1637) drives sv.midi_name → svc_midi_name → BGM_PlayMIDIorMusic, which walks the codec handlers and resolves music/<name>.<ext>. SoT uses this today for arena.ogg, bastion.ogg, etc. No FTE-style '_music' key needed; the existing 'MIDI' key already does the job. Worth a doc note though.

title: Music: extend 'cd remap' so dst can be a filename, not just a number
description:
Spoike (FTE author) noted: 'some of the quake engines retain cd remap SRCNUM NEWNUM but extend it such that newnum can also be a filename instead of just a number.' Lets mappers escape the 8-bit (0-255) numeric track ceiling and avoid map-vs-map track number conflicts.

Implementation:
- Today the file fallback path in BGM_PlayCDtrack (engine/h2shared/bgmusic.c:449) doesn't go through any remap table; the cd_remap arrays in cd_linux.c / cd_bsd.c only affect real CD playback (effectively dead).
- Add a name-aware remap table (e.g. byte → optional string) consulted before the FS lookup.
- Console command: 'cd remap 18 myambient' → BGM_PlayCDtrack(18) probes music/myambient.<ext> instead of music/track18.<ext>.
- Persist via cfg or cvar (track remaps per-gamedir).

UX:
- Numeric-to-numeric remaps still work (current dead-but-documented behavior).
- Filename remaps win when set.
- Helpful for SoT-style mods where multiple authors ship tracks.
close_reason: Implemented as 'bgm_remap NN <name>' console command in bgmusic.c. Routes the named target through BGM_PlayMIDIorMusic, picking up codec/subdir resolution automatically. Subcommands: list, reset, '-' to clear. Numeric-to-numeric remap not extended (legacy CD remap in cd_*.c is dead code, not worth touching).

title: Optional: LERP_FINISH server-timed animation ends
description:
Ironwail's LERP_FINISH flag + lerpfinish field lets the server dictate the end time of an animation (e.g., long death sequences). Without it, lerp duration is fixed at 0.1s for single-pose frames, causing minor pop at animation boundaries on long server-timed anims.

Implementation:
- Add LERP_FINISH to render.h (1<<4)
- Add float lerpfinish to entity_t in render.h
- Wire U_LERPFINISH protocol bit in cl_parse.c (read byte, decode as msgtime + byte/255 seconds)
- In R_AliasResolveLerp: if (lerpflags & LERP_FINISH) use (cl.time - lerpstart) / (lerpfinish - lerpstart) instead of e->lerptime

Notes:
- Hexen II's protocol may not have a U_LERPFINISH bit — would need to extend the entity update protocol or piggyback on an existing reserved bit.
- Low priority. Visible only on long server-timed animations (death sequences).
- Discovered while implementing r_lerpmove (uhexen2-maa8). Phase 3 of that work; deferred.
close_reason: Implemented as client-side heuristic (r_lerpanim_observed, default off) rather than protocol bit — Hexen II's entity update protocol has no spare bit for U_LERPFINISH. Tracks per-entity inter-frame-change interval and uses it as the next frame's lerp duration (clamped [0.1,0.5]s). Same end result as Ironwail's LERP_FINISH for steady-tick servers, without protocol surgery.

title: Sky wind animation system (r_skywind)
description: Port Ironwail's animated sky wind system — per-skybox wind direction, amplitude, and period for cloud animation.
close_reason: Implemented in commit. Wired existing dead r_skyspeed_back / r_skyspeed_front cvars into EmitSkyPolysMulti, upgraded to CVAR_ARCHIVE. Stock animation preserved (defaults = old hardcoded values). Per-skybox wind direction (Ironwail's full r_skywind) deferred — global speed cvars cover the common use case.

title: Underwater audio filter (snd_waterfx)

close_reason: Implemented: IIR low-pass filter ported from Ironwail, menu slider, smooth ramp transitions

title: Triple buffering / frames in flight
description: Port Ironwail's FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT=3 with GPU fence sync for pipelined frame submission. Reduces CPU-GPU sync stalls.
notes: Triple-buffering ring infrastructure landed in commit 32bdbea56 (uhexen2-8pc2) — gl_buffer.c FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT=3 with glFenceSync per slot, GL_AcquireFrameResources at frame begin (waits on slot's fence, drains garbage), GL_ReleaseFrameResources at frame end (inserts new fence, advances ring index). Wired into GL_BeginRendering/GL_EndRendering in gl_vidsdl.c. Currently only the alias instance upload writes through the ring; CPU-GPU pipelining benefit is partial until more dynamic uploads migrate (see uhexen2-o35n). Closing this bead is gated on whether 'frames in flight' counts as done when the ring exists vs. when it's pipelining the dominant per-frame uploads.
close_reason: Ring + fence sync infrastructure landed in 32bdbea56 (uhexen2-8pc2); alias instances + worldcull PVS pipelined through it (uhexen2-o35n / cfa1dd122). Residual gl_vbo.c immediate-mode tracked in uhexen2-y1v5 but is intra-frame streaming, not multi-frame in flight.

title: Shadow pass for instanced models

close_reason: Shadow pass uses inst_entities[] with corrected num_inst_entities tracking

title: Port Ironwail gamepad alt-modifier second layer (c0e587b5+7a2038a7)
description: Ironwail added +altmodifier/-altmodifier for gamepad that translates buttons to _ALT variants, doubling available bindings. Needs K_GP_*_ALT constants, state tracking in in_sdl.c, command registration, key translation, and menu display. Already have beads uhexen2-12z5 for second-layer bindings.
close_reason: Ported from Ironwail: +altmodifier with GP_RTHUMB default, K_GP_*_ALT keycodes, fallback to base binding

title: Port Ironwail mods menu spaces-in-paths fix (51a911ba)
description: Ironwail fixed mods menu failing to enumerate directories with spaces in names. Check if our mods menu in menu.c has the same path tokenization bug.
close_reason: Already implemented via b69f77614 (2026-03-24). engine/hexen2/menu.c:4153 confirms 'game "%s"' quoting matches Ironwail 51a911ba.

title: Evaluate premultiplied alpha blending (GL_ONE, GL_ONE_MINUS_SRC_ALPHA)
description: Spoike suggests using premultiplied alpha instead of straight alpha for blending, especially for HUD. Eliminates fringing artifacts, correct with texture filtering, modern compositing standard. Requires converting all textures to premultiplied alpha values — bigger change.
notes:
EVAL COMPLETE. Recommend deferring full premul; ship narrower fix instead.

== Where premul helps ==
HOLEY/cutout textures only: bilinear filter at the alpha=0 edge currently pulls palette-index-255 RGB (typically black) into the fringe pixel because straight alpha blending mixes RGB before alpha gates. Result: dark halo around cutouts. Premul kills the halo because alpha=0 pixels contribute zero RGB.

== Where premul is a no-op ==
Opaque palette textures (world walls, lightmaps, fullbrights, most model skins): no intermediate alpha exists in d_8to24table — index 255 = alpha 0, all others = alpha 255. Nothing to premultiply.

== Engine touch surface ==
- 15 glBlendFunc(SRC_ALPHA, ONE_MINUS_SRC_ALPHA) sites must become (ONE, ONE_MINUS_SRC_ALPHA) — but ONLY for premul textures. Mixing in one pass = corrupted output.
- Texture upload: walk RGBA at GL_LoadTexture for TEX_ALPHA/TEX_HOLEY textures, premultiply r=r*a/255 etc.
- ENTALPHA / model_constant_alpha shader paths need color.rgb *= a (currently only multiply .a). Affects salias_frag, sworld_frag, sprite path.
- HUD: gl_draw.c has many distinct blend states; needs per-pic 'is premul' tracking.
- OIT accumulator at gl_postprocess.c:788 already uses GL_ONE,GL_ONE — compatible.
- Particles r_part.c: straight alpha, would need conversion if particle textures aren't binary.

== Narrower fix (10%% effort, 80%% value) ==
'Alpha bleed' / 'edge padding' at upload time: for each transparent pixel (alpha=0), set its RGB to the average of its non-transparent 4/8-neighbors. Eliminates dark-halo fringe without touching the blend pipeline at all. Same trick Doom 3 / id Tech 4 uses. Single function added to GL_Upload32 path, gated on TEX_HOLEY. Zero risk of mixing premul/straight in the same pass.

== Recommendation ==
1. Implement alpha bleed for HOLEY/TEX_ALPHA textures (new bead, P3).
2. Park full premul indefinitely — Spoike's argument applies most to modern HD content pipelines, not classic palette content where binary alpha dominates. Revisit if HD packs become a primary use case.

Closing this bead as 'evaluated, narrower fix preferred'. Will file the alpha-bleed bead as discovered-from.
close_reason: Evaluated — narrower alpha-bleed fix filed as uhexen2-pcd1. Full premul deferred; ROI low on palette-dominant content.

title: Sky UV vertex shader optimization corrupts vertex attributes
description: Commit 1f69a0f3a moved sky UV generation to vertex shader but caused trees/doors/stained glass to pop invisible. Bisected and confirmed. Likely the sky shader overwrites a shared vertex attribute location used by other geometry. Reverted for now. To re-implement: use dedicated attribute locations for sky UVs that don't conflict with world/alias attribute layout.
close_reason: Fixed

title: Hi-Z occlusion culling (two-pass hierarchical Z-buffer)

Default off (gl_hiz_cull 0) pending uhexen2-8pzr acceptance sweep; stats counter for ≥10% cull-rate gate tracked at uhexen2-cyu0.

title: linux-fhs binary has no rpaths for shared libraries
description: patchelf --remove-rpath strips all rpaths from the FHS binary. It only works if users have SDL3, libvorbis, etc. in standard system paths. Should use autoPatchelfHook with bundled libs or set rpaths to common FHS locations. Works today because release zips are extracted next to game data where system libs are available.
close_reason: linux-fhs now bundles all shared libs in lib/ with relative rpath ($ORIGIN/../lib). Binary is fully portable — runs on any x86_64 Linux with glibc and OpenGL drivers.

title: Win64 build missing Opus and XMP codecs
description: Windows cross-build disables USE_CODEC_OPUS and USE_CODEC_XMP due to nixpkgs cross-compile limitations (opusfile and libxmp). Windows users get fewer audio formats than Linux. Need to either fix the cross-compile in nixpkgs, vendor the libraries, or bundle pre-built DLLs in oslibs/windows/.
close_reason: Fixed: all Win64 codec libs now cross-compiled from nix, unified ogg.dll naming

title: Upstream Flatpak to Flathub

title: [bug] Shader corruption (flat purple/blue) after loading a save
description: After loading a saved game, everything renders as flat shades of purple and blue. Geometry is visible but lighting/textures are wrong. Likely lightmap textures are corrupted, lost, or bound to wrong data after save/load cycle. The world shader multiplies tex * lm * v_color — if lm samples garbage, you get flat tinted surfaces. May be related to lightmap texture deletion in Draw_ReInit or GL state not being properly restored after Host_Loadgame_f → SV_SpawnServer → Host_ClearMemory cycle.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.6.7

title: Use embedded texture mipmaps from WAD/pak files
