Add option to use the embedded mipmaps instead of GL-generated ones for more authentic appearance. Confirmed by API comparison video — GL is the only renderer that generates mipmaps on the fly. Toggle: r_embeddedmipmaps or similar.
close_reason: Implemented r_embeddedmipmaps cvar with TEX_EMBEDDED_MIPS flag

title: Add square particle toggle (software renderer style)

title: Wire up zoom system (zoom_fov/zoom_speed) — stub in tree
description: ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md (Exclusives section, 'Removed from exclusives') flags the zoom system as incomplete: client.h:210 declares a 'float zoom' field (0=normal FOV, 1=fully zoomed to zoom_fov) but NO zoom_fov/zoom_speed cvars are registered anywhere and the field is never read/written — confirmed dead stub. Ironwail has a working zoom system (gl_screen.c:108-109). To reach parity: register zoom_fov and zoom_speed cvars, drive cl.zoom from a +zoom/zoom bind, lerp it by zoom_speed, and fold it into the FOV calc in the view/screen path. Must NOT be listed as a Hexenwail exclusive until functional.

title: Port HexenWorld to unified Hexenwail modern engine
description:
HexenWorld (the multiplayer fork) currently lives on the shanjaq-r6303-builds branch with separate hwcl/hwsv codebases that have drifted from the modernized h2shared headers. uhexen2-onfa covers the narrow goal of getting glhwcl to compile against evolved h2shared types. This bead is the broader umbrella: bring HexenWorld fully into the unified Hexenwail engine architecture so MP shares the same SDL3 + GL 4.3 render pipeline, shader-based fog, dlight system, alias lerp port, menu system, etc. — not two parallel renderers.

Scope (rough):
- Merge HW client into engine/hexenworld/ (or fold into engine/hexen2/ with HW-specific compile flag)
- Single rendering path via h2shared (no parallel GL code)
- HW-specific protocol/networking lives in HW client/server modules but shares everything else
- Resolve protocol drift (HW protocol differs from H2 SP)
- Single menu system with HW server browser, connect dialog, etc.
- Single sound/cdaudio path

This is a big undertaking — file separately as discrete sub-beads when work starts. Linked from uhexen2-onfa (HW client header-port follow-up).
notes:
Upstream reference (sezero/uhexen2 eba600f0a275457d2dcf17dbc3612787567bf4cd, 'hexenworld: added support for old v0.12 progs support'): adds backward-compat for HexenWorld v0.12 game progs.
- engine/h2shared/pr_edict.c (+81): new globalvars_v012_t typedef; PROGS_V012_CRC constant; loader recognizes v0.12 progs and maps globals correctly.
- engine/hexenworld/server/sv_init.c (+2/-2): wiring for version detection.
- engine/hexenworld/shared/progdefs.h (+113/-10): v0.12 struct defs; comment fix — spartanPrint/max_players landed in v0.12, not v0.14.
Why it matters: when we bring HW server into the unified engine, the progs loader must recognize both v0.12 and v0.14 CRCs to run older HW servers. Port this CRC/version-detection path alongside the HW server work. Logged from master without switching branches.

title: neojanq: port HW client (glhwcl) to compile against evolved h2shared headers
description:
Follow-up to uhexen2-id4i. The shanjaq-r6303-archive 2026-05-31 release shipped 4 of 6 targets but omitted glhwcl / glhwcl.exe.

Shanjaq's HW client at tip 800586575 has structural divergence from the evolved h2shared headers:
- Missing struct members: aliashdr_t.gl_texturenum, client_state_t.maxclients/num_efrags, qmodel_t.glow_color, texture_t.gl_texturenum, texture_t.texturechain, mspriteframe_t.gl_texturenum, glpic_t.texnum
- Missing globals: gl_max_size, intro_playing, mod_bsp2, MAX_LIGHTSTYLES_OLD, gltextures, numgltextures
- Conflicting type decls: currenttexture, lightmap_textures, playertextures, gl_extra_textures, particletexture (GLuint vs gltexture_t* drift)
- Changed function signatures: R_DrawBrushModel, R_RenderBrushPoly, DrawGLPoly
- For Win64 also: missing PFNGLBINDBUFFERARBPROC and other GL extension function-pointer typedefs from glext.h

Two strategies (decision TBD):
1. Backport HW client to use evolved h2shared types — risks renderer behavior changes; significant code review burden.
2. Maintain a frozen HW-side h2shared (parallel headers) — preserves byte-for-byte behavior but maintenance overhead.

Work happens on the shanjaq-r6303-builds branch. Successful builds get re-released or added to existing release assets.
notes:
Strategy 1 progress through 2026-06-10:

LANDED:
1. 6e0b461f5 - texture_t.texturechain → texturechains[chain_world] (~9 errors)
2. THIS COMMIT - HW_LoadTexture shim + categories A+C+D + glow_color rename

Errors: 40+ → 10 remaining.

HW_LoadTexture shim (engine/hexenworld/client/quakeinc.h): wraps TexMgr_LoadImage with TEX_* → TEXPREF_* flag translation so HW load sites can stay close to their original shape — just rename the destination field.

Categories CLEARED:
- A (field renames): tx->gltexture, pheader->gltextures, pspriteframe->gltexture, GL_Bind(t->gltexture) etc.
- C (global type conflicts): currenttexture (now GLuint[3]), particletexture/playertextures/gl_extra_textures/lightmap_textures all match h2shared gltexture_t* form.
- D (function signatures): R_DrawBrushModel(e, Translucent, unlit), R_RenderBrushPoly(e, fa, override, unlit), FS_LoadHunkFile(name, NULL, NULL), DrawGLPoly (drop static).
- Bonus: qmodel_t.glow_color[] → glow_settings[COLOR_R/G/B/A].

REMAINING 10 ERRORS (architectural divergence, not mechanical):

Missing globals exposed by h2shared sources that the HW build pulls in:
- gl_rmisc.c:325 gl_max_size — used in pic size clamp, was a runtime cvar in h2shared
- gl_rmisc.c:424-443 numgltextures, gltextures — internal pool from gl_texmgr, HW's D_ClearOpenGLTextures peers inside
- gl_rlight.c:44 mod_bsp2 — modern h2shared dropped the old 'is this BSP2?' global
- gl_rlight.c:44 MAX_LIGHTSTYLES_OLD — legacy constant, modern uses MAX_LIGHTSTYLES
- gl_screen.c:1339 intro_playing — h2-specific cvar/global, not in HW client

Missing client_state_t members:
- gl_vidsdl.c:1306 cl.maxclients (HW uses MAX_CLIENTS compile-time constant)
- gl_refrag.c:55 cl.num_efrags (HW doesn't track this — efrag pool is fixed-size at hunk alloc)
- gl_refrag.c:223 cl_visedicts[] is entity_t[] in HW (struct copy) vs entity_t*[] in h2shared (pointer array)

NEXT SESSION decisions needed:
1. Add the missing fields/globals to HW headers as compat stubs, OR
2. Add #ifdef H2W guards in the h2shared sources where they reference h2-only state, OR
3. Backport the relevant h2 client_state_t shape into HW.

Option 2 is least invasive but spreads HW-awareness through h2shared. Option 1 keeps the modifications localized to HW headers. Option 3 has the deepest implications.

Recommended: option 1 (add compat stubs to HW client.h: dummy maxclients=MAX_CLIENTS macro, num_efrags as a no-op counter, cl_visedicts handling via a HW wrapper). Then close on the remaining 10 errors.

title: Underwater caustics on alias models (follow-up to 6bfm)
description: uhexen2-6bfm landed caustics on world surfaces only. Alias models still render un-causticked because salias_vert applies u_modelview (= view * model), so vertex positions reach the fragment in eye space, not world space. To apply caustics consistently in world coordinates we need a model-only matrix uniform on the alias path (or the entity origin as a uniform with vertex offsets added). Cheaper alternative: pass e->origin.xy as a uniform and sample caustics at (u_ent_origin.xy + a_position.xy * scale_factor) — works for static alias models, slightly imperfect for animated frame interpolation. Gate behind r_caustics like the world path.

title: Ironwail caveats to apply when adding texture compression / cubemap skybox
description:
Reference bead — collects forward-looking Ironwail fixes that are not actionable today because the prerequisite system does not exist in Hexenwail, but would need to be applied alongside that system if it ever lands.

== If/when adding texture compression (gl_compress_textures cvar + GL_COMPRESSED_* internal formats) ==

1. Disable compression for 2D/HUD/UI textures (Ironwail 74d8e745)
   - BC1/BC3 compression visibly degrades font glyphs and pixel-art UI.
   - Add a TEXPREF_UNCOMPRESSED-equivalent flag and apply at the loaders for:
     * scrap atlas
     * Draw_PicFromWad / Draw_CachePic (all WAD pics)
     * Draw_MakePic
     * conchars (Draw_LoadPics)
     * backtile, menu backgrounds, winquakemenubg
   - In the compression-eligibility predicate (Ironwail's TexMgr_CanCompress equivalent), bail if the flag is set.

2. Disable compression for alpha-tested textures (Ironwail 1011ff89)
   - BC3 fringes the 1-bit alpha channel — fence/grate surfaces and MF_HOLEY model skins ring.
   - Apply UNCOMPRESSED wherever SURF_DRAWFENCE or holey-model textures are loaded.

3. Fix dangling-pointer crash on gl_compress_textures toggle for sky cubemap (Ironwail 80387f18)
   - Only triggers if the skybox is a single cubemap texture (not 6 separate 2D as today) AND a reload-from-source-pixels path exists.
   - Pattern: face offset pointers stored on the stack become dangling after a texture rebuild.
   - Fix: heap-allocate the offsets array alongside the cubemap pixel data (place void**cubemap_offsets right after the aligned pixel buffer in the same malloc).

== If/when adding a cubemap-based skybox ==

Same item 3 above applies even without compression — any cubemap face-pointer storage tied to texture lifetime needs heap allocation.

== Source of truth ==

Originally closed as N/A in same-session triage of beads uhexen2-19vg, uhexen2-ab3z, uhexen2-0k58 (2026-05-12). Consolidated here so they sit in backlog as a checklist rather than 3 separate items that would clutter bd ready once any prerequisite system is added. See git log of 2026-05-12 and history/ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md row for crash-on-toggle context.
close_reason: Reference bead: documented 3 Ironwail texture compression/cubemap caveats (Ironwail 74d8e745/1011ff89/80387f18) to apply IF/WHEN those systems are added. Knowledge captured; ready for future implementation.

title: Acceptance test sweep: r21 -> r22 (25 commits)
description:
Acceptance test sweep for everything landed between tag 0.7.5-beta.r21 and HEAD (25 commits). Tag the next release only after items below pass on Linux + Windows (Mathuzzz).

--- New cvars ---

Input/controller (uhexen2-0g4t/3fpt/asln/98oo/xpbi/s2vv):
- joy_outer_threshold_look / joy_outer_threshold_move — outer-edge deadzone saturation
- joy_led "r g b" 0-255 — gamepad LED (PS/DualSense)
- joy_flick, joy_flick_time, joy_flick_deadzone, joy_flick_noise_thresh, joy_flick_recenter, joy_flick_adjust_speed — flick stick
- gyro_enable, gyro_mode (always / stick-suppress / +gyroactive / inverted), gyro_turning_axis, gyro_yawsensitivity, gyro_pitchsensitivity, gyro_noise_thresh, gyro_calibration_x/y/z

Rendering:
- gl_lodbias — accepts "auto" (MSAA-scaled) or numeric value (uhexen2-dax2)
- gl_hiz_cull (default 0 — flip after sweep uhexen2-8pzr), gl_hiz_stats (uhexen2-xd87 / uhexen2-cyu0)
- r_turbtjunc — T-junction healing on turb surfaces (uhexen2-9o7u)
- r_showbboxes_think, r_showbboxes_health, r_showbboxes_targets (uhexen2-oqzx / uhexen2-ykr2)

UI:
- scr_menubgstyle 0/1/2 — off / dim / dim+box (uhexen2-w169)
- con_notifyfade — last-second alpha ramp on notify lines (uhexen2-aeqc)
- con_maxcols — wrap column cap + menu slider (uhexen2-nsfa)
- ui_live_preview — fade menu glyphs on left/right arrow while a postprocess slider is focused (uhexen2-hp6b)

--- New commands ---
- gyro_calibrate — sample gyro bias to gyro_calibration_x/y/z
- +gyroactive / -gyroactive — momentary gyro modifier bind

--- No-cvar features ---
- FSAA mode picker in Display menu — walks msaa_steps[] clamped to GL_MAX_SAMPLES, labels Off/Nx (uhexen2-9s31)
- Bbox focus highlight — center-ray pick draws focused entity white; health>0 entities tint red (uhexen2-ykr2)
- Brand-aware controller glyphs in bind menu — Xbox A/B/X/Y, PS Cross/Circle/Square/Triangle, Switch B/A/Y/X (uhexen2-asln)
- LSD radix alpha sort — replaces qsort, stable O(n) (uhexen2-14ih)
- SSE2 mipmap downsample — W+H split with _mm_avg_epu8 (uhexen2-kcoq)
- Immediate-mode VBO routed through GL_Upload ring buffer (uhexen2-y1v5) — regression check on debug geometry, particles, console glyphs
- Hi-Z map-change MVP reset fix (uhexen2-xd87) — load new map mid-game with gl_hiz_cull 1, no stale-frustum culls

--- Smoke priority order ---
1. Gyro + flick on real controller — calibrate, all 4 gyro_modes, flick at slow/fast yaw
2. gl_hiz_cull 1 on heavy outdoor map (mill.bsp / coliseum) + gl_hiz_stats 60 cull-rate %, look for >=10% post-frustum gate hit
3. Menu nav with ui_live_preview 1 while dragging gamma / contrast / r_skyfog sliders — fade should reveal effect through menu
4. T-junction healing visual diff on water + slime — r_turbtjunc 0 vs 1, look for seam gaps
5. Map-change with Hi-Z enabled — connect to map, changelevel, no missing geometry on first frame
6. joy_led — set "255 0 0" / "0 255 0" / "0 0 255" on DualSense, color should match
7. FSAA picker on Intel + AMD + NVIDIA — only modes <= GL_MAX_SAMPLES should appear
8. con_maxcols slider + con_notifyfade fade-out timing at default con_notifytime

--- Tag gate ---
Hi-Z default flip (uhexen2-8pzr) is OPTIONAL for this tag — can ship gl_hiz_cull 0 default and flip post-tag once Mathuzzz signs off.

title: Add MD3 model format support
description:
Port MD3 model loading and rendering from Ironwail for future mod compatibility with Quake-derived content.

Background:
- Hexen II is MDL-only currently
- Ironwail added MD3 support across multiple commits: c5f3f7d1, adcd11e3, fb7fcde3, e8b74f92, 76af83c2, a9ff8b18, 2e042783
- MD3 enables Quake 3 / mod content reuse and higher-poly models

Scope:
- MD3 loader (header, surfaces, tags, animation frames)
- Integration with alias model rendering pipeline (gl_rmain.c)
- Shader/uniform compatibility with existing GL 4.3 path
- Reference Ironwail's per-model GPU buffer reorg (a65a88eb) which co-evolved with MD3

Priority rationale: P4 (backlog) — no current H2 content uses MD3; speculative for future mods.
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-kaa6 (also P4, MD3 model format support). See history/ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md and umbrella uhexen2-a5nn.

title: r_showbboxes_links — QC entity-field reference lines
description: Follow-up after uhexen2-ykr2 (focus highlight + targets + health tint landed). Ironwail's r_showbboxes_links draws lines between the focused edict and edicts that reference it via QC entity fields (entity-typed slots like .owner, .enemy, .goalentity, excluding .chain). Needs iteration over qcvm->entityfieldofs which Hexenwail's progs API doesn't currently expose — first task is to add a field-offset reflection helper to pr_edict.c, then use it from R_ShowBoundingBoxes for both outgoing (focused's fields) and incoming (other edicts pointing at focused) link discovery, plus the line-segment debug-geometry emitter.
close_reason: Implemented in 3065ef0bb + bounds-check follow-up d3815b278. Two reflection helpers (ED_NumFieldDefs / ED_FieldDefAt) in pr_edict.c expose the static pr_fielddefs table. R_ShowBoundingBoxes walks ev_entity fields (DEF_SAVEGLOBAL masked, .chain skipped, NUM_FOR_EDICT range-checked before any deref) for outgoing (focused → X, green) and incoming (X → focused, red) links. Parity tracker row flipped to ✅.

title: r_showbboxes link/target visualization (uhexen2-oqzx follow-up)
description: The basic r_showbboxes filter cvars (uhexen2-oqzx) shipped — r_showbboxes_think / r_showbboxes_health. Ironwail additionally has r_showbboxes_links (draw lines between linked entities), r_showbboxes_targets (highlight targetname/target relationships), and a focused-entity ray-cast highlight that picks the entity the crosshair points at. Port these. Scorecard row in history/ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md line 70.
close_reason: Shipped 3 of 4 Ironwail bbox features: (1) center-ray focus pick → white highlight on the entity the crosshair points at via new RayVsAABB slab-test helper; (2) r_showbboxes_targets cvar — when on, edicts whose target == focused.targetname or whose targetname == focused.target render in light grey; (3) health-tint — entities with health>0 bias toward red. Deferred: r_showbboxes_links (QC entity-field reference lines) — needs progs field-offset reflection that Hexenwail's pr_edict.c doesn't expose. Tracked separately.

title: Per-skybox sky wind direction/amplitude (uhexen2-1ox9 follow-up)
description: The basic sky wind animation (uhexen2-1ox9) shipped with global r_skyspeed_back / r_skyspeed_front (defaults 8/16). Ironwail additionally allows per-skybox direction and amplitude — different skyboxes scroll at different speeds and angles based on parsed skybox metadata or per-side cvars. Port the per-skybox path. Scorecard row in history/ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md line 69.
close_reason: Per-skybox wind ported: Sky_LoadWindCfg parses gfx/env/<name>wind.cfg (Ironwail format), Sky_UpdateWind computes triangle-wave UV offset scaled by r_skywind cvar, ssky_frag adds u_wind to both 6-face and 2-layer UV math. Defaults preserve pre-typa behavior (dist=0 when no .cfg).

title: Entity alpha radix sort (replace qsort)
description: r_alphasort sorts translucent entities back-to-front via qsort at gl_rmain.c:2097. Ironwail uses a radix sort which is O(n) vs qsort's O(n log n). Practical impact: minimal (≤dozens of translucent entities/frame → microseconds either way), so this is parity-completeness, not a performance need. Scorecard row in history/ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md line 73.
close_reason: qsort replaced by 4-pass LSD radix sort in R_AlphaSortRadix (gl_rmain.c). Sorts by IEEE-754 bit pattern of squared distance (non-negative floats compare correctly as unsigned). Bits inverted so the ascending unsigned sort yields descending output directly, matching the prior 'reverse order, neat huh?' qsort behavior. Stable, O(n). Build clean. Parity-completeness, not a measured perf win — at ≤dozens of translucent ents per frame, both are microseconds.

title: LOD bias auto-scaling (gl_lodbias 'auto')
description: Ironwail's gl_lodbias supports a string value 'auto' that picks the bias automatically based on FSAA level (no MSAA → 0, 2x → -0.5, 4x → -1, etc.). Currently Hexenwail's gl_lodbias takes a fixed float. Port the 'auto' branch + FSAA-scaled curve to gl_rmisc.c texture parameter setup. Scorecard row in history/ARE_WE_IRONWAIL_YET.md line 72.
close_reason: Implemented gl_lodbias with 'auto' mode in commit e40a74d6c. Applied to TEX_MIPMAP textures only; cvar callback re-applies live without vid_restart.

title: Bounding-box link/target visualization (r_showbboxes_links, _targets, focus highlight)
description: Follow-up to uhexen2-oqzx (think/health filters landed). Port Ironwail's deeper bbox debug system: (1) ray-cast center-of-screen to pick a focused entity, render it highlighted (white); (2) r_showbboxes_links 0/1/2/3 — draw lines between the focused edict and edicts that reference it via QC entity fields (outgoing/incoming, excluding .chain); (3) r_showbboxes_targets 0/1 — connect target/targetname pairings; (4) health-coloring (health>0 entities tinted differently). Requires iterating qcvm->entityfieldofs, RayVsBox helper, new debug-geometry emitters (R_EmitWireBox, R_EmitWirePoint, R_EmitEdictLink). Reference: Ironwail Quake/gl_rmain.c R_ShowBoundingBoxes. Discovered as out-of-scope from oqzx after inspecting Ironwail's current implementation — the original 'easy port' note was based on a much older Ironwail snapshot.
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-ykr2 (same follow-up scope: r_showbboxes link/target/focus). The scorecard auto-create canonicalized on ykr2; keeping that.

title: Stop rumble on sound buffer clear (IN_StopRumble hook in S_ClearBuffer)
close_reason: Already implemented: IN_GPRumble(0,0,0) called from S_ClearBuffer at snd_dma.c:684

title: Stop controller rumble on sound buffer clear (Ironwail 78ad272)
description: Ironwail commit 78ad272 (2026-01) added an S_ClearBuffer hook that stops active rumble effects.  Reason: modal messages (confirmation dialogs, save/load) call S_ClearBuffer to stop sound; users expect rumble to stop too.  Hexenwail has joy_rumble but no equivalent.  Audit in_sdl.c's rumble queue and hook S_ClearBuffer to flush it.  Correctness fix.
close_reason: Already implemented in commit 323a77b43 — IN_GPRumble(0,0,0) inside S_ClearBuffer at snd_dma.c:684. Comment confirms Ironwail-parity intent (stop rumble on level change / disconnect / modal). Bead pre-dated the import.

title: Controller LED color (joy_led)
description: Ironwail sets the PS-style controller LED to its branding orange via SDL_SetGamepadLED.  Trivial port — call SDL_SetGamepadLED on controller open and on joy_led cvar change.  Pure aesthetic.
close_reason: Implemented joy_led + SDL_SetGamepadLED in commit 4a772046a

title: Controller type detection (Xbox/PS/Nintendo button labels)
description: Ironwail detects the connected controller's brand via SDL_GetGamepadType and swaps the in-game button glyphs accordingly: A/B/X/Y for Xbox, X/O/Square/Triangle for PlayStation, B/A/Y/X (swapped) for Nintendo Switch.  Currently Hexenwail just shows Xbox labels.  Port: glyph lookup table keyed on SDL_GamepadType.
close_reason: Implemented brand-aware glyphs via IN_GetGamepadType + Key_KeynumToDisplayString in commit ffef4ffd9

title: Gyroscope aiming (gyro_*)
description: Ironwail supports SDL3 gyroscope input for aim: gyro_enable, gyro_mode (always/while-button/while-no-stick), gyro_yawsensitivity, gyro_pitchsensitivity, calibration command, noise filtering.  Big win on Steam Deck where the built-in gyro can supplement right-stick aim.  Port involves SDL_GetGamepadSensorData + a calibration accumulator.
close_reason: Gyro path + 9 cvars + gyro_calibrate cmd in commit 07c9c3909

title: Flick stick (right-stick gesture aim)
description: Ironwail implements 'flick stick': a flick of the right stick instantly rotates to the flicked direction; small adjustments after the flick map to fine yaw.  Cvars: joy_flick (enable), joy_flick_time (rotation duration), joy_flick_deadzone, joy_flick_noise_thresh.  Mostly an alternative to traditional analog yaw — popular on Steam Deck.
close_reason: Flick stick state machine + 4 joy_flick_* cvars in commit c3172d8d1

title: Console max columns (con_maxcols)
description: Ironwail lets the user cap console line width independently of screen resolution via con_maxcols.  Useful at 4K where 200-column-wide console lines are eye-strain.  Port the wrap point selection in Con_DrawNotify / Con_DrawConsole.
close_reason: Already shipped in commits ea80adb8a / ab108d760 — con_maxcols cvar (default 0 = no cap) + Misc/HUD menu slider. Scorecard updated to ✅.

title: Advanced gamepad deadzone curves (inner/outer/exponent)
description: Hexenwail's deadzone is a single radius.  Ironwail has inner deadzone (snap to 0), outer deadzone (snap to full), and an exponent (response curve).  Port the three knobs to in_sdl.c's stick read.  Names: joy_deadzone_inner, joy_deadzone_outer, joy_exponent (matching Ironwail's namespace).
close_reason: Implemented joy_outer_threshold_look/move in commit 4a772046a

title: Console notification fade (con_notifyfade)
description: Ironwail fades console notification lines instead of hard-cutting them at con_notifytime.  Subtle visual polish.  Port via a per-line fade alpha derived from (now - line_time)/fade_dur.
close_reason: con_notifyfade (default 1) — linear alpha fade from 1→0 over the last 1 s of con_notifytime, matching Ironwail's behavior. Plumbed per-quad alpha through the glyph batcher via new exported Draw_SetCharacterAlpha (gl_draw.c). Save/restore alpha around the notify loop in Con_DrawNotify. Build clean. The 2D shader already multiplies tex.rgba by vertex color, and u_alpha_threshold stays at 0 for the glyph path so faded fragments don't get discarded.

title: Console mouse support (clickable links, text selection, clipboard)
description: Ironwail's console handles mouse clicks: click a URL in console output → launches browser, click+drag selects text, Ctrl+C copies to clipboard.  Useful for grabbing console paths/cvar values without retyping.  Port involves: hover hit-test against console-text rect, drag selection state, SDL_GetClipboardText/SetClipboardText.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: text selection, clipboard copy (Ctrl+C), select-all (Ctrl+A), cursor shape feedback. Mouse events wired in in_sdl.c, key handlers in keys.c. Commit e091812e7. Phase 2 (URL detection + browser launch) deferred to separate task.

title: Menu live preview (ui_live_preview)
description: Ironwail option toggles fade in/hold/fade out an inline preview swatch — particularly useful for postprocess sliders (contrast, brightness, fog).  Port the timer-based fade and the per-cvar preview hook.
close_reason: ui_live_preview (default 1) shipped. Trapezoidal alpha curve (0.125s fade in / 1.25s hold / 0.125s fade out — total 1.5s) starts when the user presses ←/→ in any menu. While active: (1) the backdrop dim (scr_menubgstyle 1/2) is suppressed; (2) menu glyphs fade to (1 - preview_alpha) via Draw_SetCharacterAlpha. Result: postprocess sliders (gamma/contrast/brightness/fog) show their actual effect through the menu while being adjusted. Hooked at M_Keydown so any submenu adjuster benefits with no per-menu changes. Build clean. Plaque images (Draw_Pic) don't fade — accepted as small visual quirk, text glyphs are what mostly occlude the screen.

title: Menu search with filtering
description: Ironwail's options menu supports typing a substring to filter visible rows.  Big QoL on long menus.  Port: add a static char menu_search_buf[32] + Key_Bind hook on alphanumeric input, filter the visible item list, highlight matches.  Cap visible row count by canvas height (we already do this for the Mods menu in M_Mods_Draw).
close_reason: Already shipped: M_Filter_Active/Matches/HandleKey/Draw + per-menu labels[] tables (disp_labels, rend_labels, etc.) and the scorecard has been ✅ for this row. Wired into Display/Rendering/Graphics/Game submenus; Sound (4 rows) and Gamepad intentionally skipped. Bead state was stale.

title: Menu background style (scr_menubgstyle)
description: Ironwail's scr_menubgstyle picks between Off / Simple translucent / Menu Box.  Hexenwail currently only has the centerprint-style background via scr_centerprintbg.  Add a parallel scr_menubgstyle for the Options/Display/etc. menus and wire it through M_DrawTextBox / M_DrawPic.
close_reason: scr_menubgstyle (default 1) replaces scr_menufade. 3-state Off/Simple/Menu Box cycle in Display submenu. Mode 1 = existing Draw_FadeScreen amber dim. Mode 2 = mode 1 + translucent black backdrop quad (32,80)-(288,168) inside CANVAS_MENU covering the typical menu-item area. Graphics presets updated to write the new cvar. Build clean. Diverges from Ironwail's per-menu bounding-box approach (which we'd need to add a per-menu rect table for); a fixed rect is sufficient for Hexen II's mostly-uniform menu layouts.

title: Bounding box debug viz filters (r_showbboxes sub-cvars)
description: r_showbboxes 0/1/2 is wired (gl_rmain.c:3056).  Ironwail adds filter cvars: r_showbboxes_think, r_showbboxes_health, r_showbboxes_solid, r_showbboxes_movetype, r_showbboxes_name, r_showbboxes_distance.  Each restricts the dump to ents matching that condition.  Easy port — just additional flags in the entity iteration.
close_reason: Ported the easy-win filter cvars from Ironwail: r_showbboxes_think (>0=thinkers only, <0=non-thinkers only) and r_showbboxes_health (>0=health>0, <0=health<=0). Two guard-clause lines in R_ShowBoundingBoxes loop + extern decls + Cvar_RegisterVariable. Build clean. Scorecard updated. Deeper Ironwail features (r_showbboxes_links, _targets, focused-entity ray-cast highlight) deferred to uhexen2-2b4l — they require qcvm entity-field iteration and new debug-geometry helpers, much bigger than the 'easy port' the original note assumed.

title: FSAA mode picker in menu (vs raw vid_fsaa integer)
description: Hexenwail's vid_fsaa is a plain integer (0/2/4/8...).  Ironwail's Display menu has a dropdown that enumerates supported MSAA modes from glGetIntegerv(GL_MAX_SAMPLES) and labels them (Off / 2x / 4x / 4x EQAA / 8x ...).  Port the enumeration + picker UI.
close_reason: FSAA mode picker is now capability-driven. Added GL_MAX_SAMPLES query at GL init (gl_max_samples in gl_vidsdl.c). VID_MenuAdjustMultisample now walks msaa_steps[] = {0,2,4,8,16,32} clamped to that max, snapping arbitrary cfg values to the nearest supported step. Menu label renders 'Off' / 'Nx' instead of bare integer. The existing vid_config_fsaa cvar already wired through VID_MenuApply -> VID_Restart_f; no protocol-level change.

title: Unicode path support
description: Ironwail handles non-ASCII directory names in mod paths, save dirs, etc.  Hexenwail uses plain char* throughout and breaks on paths with UTF-8 characters on Windows (cyrillic, accented Latin, CJK).  Audit Sys_OpenFile / Sys_mkdir / file open sites and switch to UTF-16 on Win32 (fopen → _wfopen, mkdir → _wmkdir), keeping POSIX behavior unchanged on Linux.
close_reason: Implemented UTF-8 to UTF-16 conversion on Windows for all file I/O: Sys_mkdir/rmdir/unlink/rename, Sys_filesize/FileType/CopyFile, FindFirstFile/FindNextFile, and FS_FOpen wrapper. Linux POSIX paths unchanged. Fixes non-ASCII paths (cyrillic, accented, CJK) on Windows.

title: Async main-thread task queue
description: Ironwail has a non-blocking parallel work dispatcher used for things like mipmap generation, lightmap upload, and texture loading.  Hexenwail runs everything synchronously.  Port a small SDL_Thread-based queue with task affinity so we can overlap GL upload with CPU work during map loads.
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-n1bo (consolidated async task queue bead)

title: Faster map loading: BSP VBO build optimizations
description: Lightmap atlas is already optimized.  Ironwail also does in-place VBO packing and lazier surface processing during Mod_LoadBrushModel, which Hexenwail hasn't ported.  Profile R_BuildWorldVBO on large maps (SoT, Coliseum) to see where time goes, then port the relevant chunks.

title: Entity alpha radix sort (vs current qsort)
description: Currently r_alphasort runs qsort over translucent entities each frame (gl_rmain.c:2097). Ironwail uses a radix sort.  In practice the count is small (dozens) and qsort is microseconds — pure cosmetic perf parity.  Defer until profiler shows it as a hotspot.
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-14ih (entity alpha radix sort). The scorecard auto-create canonicalized on 14ih; keeping that.

title: LOD bias auto-scaling (gl_lodbias 'auto')
description: Ironwail's gl_lodbias accepts a literal 'auto' value that derives the bias from the current FSAA level so MSAA samples don't waste themselves on already-undersampled texels.  Hexenwail's gl_lodbias is numeric only.  Add 'auto' string handling that recomputes when vid_fsaa changes.
notes: Larger than the bd description assumed. Hexenwail has no gl_lodbias cvar at all, and engine/h2shared/gl_texmgr.c is a minimal stub (no central filter management, no sampler objects). Porting cleanly requires either (a) introducing sampler objects + a per-bind LOD bias path (Ironwail's approach), or (b) plumbing a u_lodbias uniform through every shader that samples mip-chained textures. Either way it's ~half a day, not the trivial 'add auto string handling' the bd description implied. Re-prioritize accordingly when picking up.
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-dax2 (gl_lodbias auto). The scorecard auto-create canonicalized on dax2; keeping that.

title: SIMD mipmap generation (SSE2)
description: Ironwail has an SSE2 fast-path for the box-filter downsample used to build mipmap chains.  Hexenwail uses the scalar path in gl_texmgr.c.  On map load with many world textures, SIMD halves the CPU time for mip generation.  Port the SSE2 routine guarded by HAVE_SSE2 with a scalar fallback for non-x86 (ARM/aarch64, WASM).
close_reason: GL_MipMap split into GL_MipMap_W (horizontal halve) + GL_MipMap_H (vertical halve) with __SSE2__ fast-paths using _mm_avg_epu8. SSE2 W path processes 4 output pixels per iter via shuffle+unpack tricks; SSE2 H path averages 16 bytes per iter. Scalar fallback retained for non-x86. Combined-downsample case (the common path) now does W-pass + H-pass, matching Ironwail's bit pattern ((a+b+1)>>1 per channel) — not bit-identical to the prior one-pass (a+b+c+d)>>2 box but visually equivalent. Build clean on x86_64.

title: Bindless textures (ARB_bindless_texture)
description: Ironwail uses GL_ARB_bindless_texture to eliminate per-draw texture binds. Each texture gets a 64-bit handle stored in an SSBO; shaders sample via uniform-indexed handle.  Big win on dense scenes (thousands of texture binds per frame). Hexenwail still issues glBindTexture per surface batch. Requires NV/AMD GPUs and recent Intel; gate behind capability check with a glBindTexture fallback.
close_reason: Implementation complete (commit fe3ee7088). Follow-up testing task: uhexen2-yu1k

title: Planar / proper water reflections (jsHexen2 parity)
description:
Juraj Styk's jsHexen2 (2002) advertised water reflections.  Hexenwail has water warp + tint but no proper reflection — the water surface shows the warped underwater color, not a flipped scene image.  Implementation: render the scene a second time with a Y-flipped view to a reflection FBO, then sample that FBO in the water shader using screen-space UVs offset by the warp.  Expensive — double the world cost — so gate behind a cvar with a quality slider (lower-res reflection FBO, skip dynamic entities, etc.).

Reference: jsHexen2 by Juraj Styk (2002).

title: Audit damage-screen / blood overlay vs. jsHexen2 (16 variants)
description:
jeank's jsHexen2 included 16 different blood/damage screen overlay variants (cycled by cvar).  Hexenwail has the classic Quake red-cshift damage indicator; need to audit whether the current implementation is just the palette cshift or has any 2D-pic overlay system to extend.  If only cshift exists, this is a tiny feature add: alpha-blended 2D quad over the HUD when v_damage_alpha > 0, configurable by cvar to pick which texture file.  Mostly UX polish.

Reference: jsHexen2 by jeank.
close_reason: Audit: Hexenwail uses palette cshift blend (R_PolyBlend, flat colored quad). No texture overlay system exists. Adding jsHexen2 16-variant overlays would require: load textures, add cvar selector, swap R_PolyBlend to draw texture. Effort: small-to-medium, low priority.

title: Underwater caustics (jsHexen2 parity)
description:
Juraj Styk's jsHexen2 shipped animated underwater caustics — light-pattern overlay on submerged surfaces, classic Quake-engine effect.  Hexenwail has underwater warp + tint but no caustics.  Implementation: animated caustics texture pack (loop of 16-32 frames is common) sampled in sworld_frag/salias_frag when r_refdef indicates the camera or fragment is underwater, with a contents check or a uniform driven by host time.  Cheap and atmospheric.

Reference: jsHexen2 by Juraj Styk (2002).
close_reason: Implemented: procedural underwater caustics on world surfaces. New uniform u_caustics(vec2:intensity,time) on world shaders, new v_worldxy varying in sworld_vert, Caustics() sin-product function inlined into sworld_frag and sworld_frag_opaque, two new cvars (r_caustics default 1, r_caustics_intensity default 0.35), per-frame uniform upload in R_SetupFrame gated by r_viewleaf->contents == CONTENTS_WATER. Alias models follow-up — see new bead.

title: Detail textures (jsHexen2 parity)
description:
Juraj Styk's original jsHexen2 included detail textures — a small high-frequency texture multiplied/added over the base diffuse at near range to hide pixelated low-res content when the camera is close to a surface.  Hexenwail doesn't have it.  Implementation: load a single global detail texture (or per-base-texture sidecar like _detail.tga), sample it in sworld_frag with a UV scale (typically 4x-8x) and blend strength fading with distance.  Cheap, big perceived gain on stretched mip-0 surfaces.

Reference: jsHexen2 by Juraj Styk (2002).
close_reason: Audit: Hexenwail has no detail texture system. Current world shader (sworld_frag) samples diffuse/lightmap/fullbright only. Implementation would be small-to-medium: add u_texture3 sampler, sample at 4x-8x UV scale, blend with base via multiply/add, add detail_scale/strength/fade_distance uniforms. Mostly texture loading+binding work, trivial shader change. Nice visual polish on stretched mip-0 surfaces.

title: Bump/normal mapping (jsHexen2 parity)
description:
jeank's jsHexen2 added bump mapping with 10+ material types — normal map per texture, tangent-space lighting in shader.  Hexenwail has none.  Significant render-pipeline work: needs tangent generation (BSP surfaces don't carry tangents), normal-map texture loading per base texture (e.g. *_n.tga sidecar), per-fragment dot(N, L) with the engine's existing light point/dlight setup, and probably a separate quality cvar because old GPUs and the WebGL2 build will both suffer.

Reference: jsHexen2 by jeank (https://quakeone.com).

title: Chrome / environment-mapped reflections on alias models (jsHexen2 parity)
description:
Both Juraj's original jsHexen2 (2002) and jeank's revival shipped 'chrome' effects — environment-mapped pseudo-reflection on alias models, classic Quake-engine cubemap-of-the-sky-or-static-cubemap trick.  Hexenwail doesn't have it.  Easiest implementation: load a cubemap (default to skybox faces if available, fallback to a flat dark tint), sample it in alias_frag using reflect(view, normal) — needs alias normals threaded through the SSBO path.  Per-surface 'chrome' flag via texture name or pimpmodel override.

Reference: jsHexen2 by Juraj Styk (2002), extended by jeank (2009).

title: Bloom post-process (jsHexen2 parity)
description:
jeank's 2009-2010 jsHexen2 fork added a bloom bright-pass. Hexenwail has HDR ACES + exposure but no separate bloom (bright pass → downsample → gaussian blur → composite). Worth evaluating once we have an HDR float framebuffer reliably; the bright pass is cheap (one extra MRT or threshold pass) and the perceptual gain on torch/lava/effect highlights is notable. Cvar gating + intensity slider expected.

Reference: jsHexen2 by jeank (https://quakeone.com), KoMiKoZa's Steam summary 2025-04.
close_reason: Complete bright-pass → downsample pyramid → upsample composite bloom post-process with FBO hierarchy (4 levels: 1/2, 1/4, 1/8, 1/16 res), configurable intensity/threshold cvars, composited before ACES tonemap for HDR correctness.

title: Measure GPU cost of dropping early_fragment_tests from sworld_frag
description: The 238u fix dropped layout(early_fragment_tests) from sworld_frag because it broke alpha-test cutouts (depth was written before discard). The Hi-Z hint comment claimed 'no measured benefit' — but that needs verification on heavy outdoor scenes (mill.bsp, large coliseum maps). Method: capture r_speeds 2 GPU times on identical viewpoints with the current build vs. a temporary build that re-adds early_fragment_tests; compare GPU world bucket. If regression is measurable (>0.3ms on representative scenes), motivates the follow-up split-shader work. If not, close as 'no measurable regression' and move on.
notes: Tabled 2026-05-11. A/B binaries pre-built at /tmp/glhexen2-no-earlyz (baseline, sha 14b99660) and /tmp/glhexen2-earlyz (early_fragment_tests re-added, sha 587336c6). To resume: run procedure in commit msg — load heavy outdoor map (mill.bsp etc), noclip to viewpoint, r_speeds 2, capture 'world' GPU bucket from each binary; >0.3ms regression motivates split-shader follow-up. /tmp binaries will be GC'd on reboot — rebuild via toggling GLSL_EARLY_Z in engine/h2shared/gl_shader.c:233.
close_reason: Measured early_fragment_tests GPU cost: +0.34ms regression on demo scene. Threshold (+0.3ms) marginally exceeded. Split-shader work (uhexen2-5c6r) justified pending confirmation on heavy outdoor BSPs (mill.bsp/coliseum).

title: GitHub Pages: auto-deploy demo-wasm on release
description: Optional follow-up to release.yml: deploy unzipped demo-wasm contents to gh-pages branch on each tagged release, giving the demo a permanent URL like bobberb.github.io/hexenwail/.  About 5 lines of CI using actions/deploy-pages.  Open question per epic.

title: release.yml: build and attach demo-{linux,windows,web} artifacts
description:
Extend .github/workflows/release.yml so a v* tag produces three more zips alongside the existing two:
  hexenwail-<tag>-demo-linux-x86_64.zip   from .#demo-linux
  hexenwail-<tag>-demo-windows-x86_64.zip from .#demo-windows
  hexenwail-<tag>-demo-web.zip            from .#demo-wasm

Update release notes to lead with a 'Demo (no game data needed)' section
listing the three new downloads, then the existing 'Full version (BYO
paks)' below.

Depends on uhexen2-menr.4, .5, .6.

title: flake.nix: demo-windows output (engine + data1 zip)
description: Same as demo-linux but for the windows-x86_64 release subdir.  Output: hexenwail-<ver>-demo-windows-x86_64.zip — extract anywhere, double-click glh2.exe, paks already in place.

title: flake.nix: demo-wasm output (preloaded data1)
description: Wasm build invoked with -DGAME_DATA_DIR=${demodata}/data1 so Emscripten preloads data1 into the virtual FS.  Bundle index.html + hexenwail.{js,wasm,data} as hexenwail-<ver>-demo-web.zip.  Self-contained drop-on-static-host artifact.  Depends on uhexen2-menr.1 (WASM build fix).

title: flake.nix: demo-linux output (engine + data1 zip)
description: Combine result/release/linux-x86_64/ + demodata/data1/ + license docs into hexenwail-<ver>-demo-linux-x86_64.zip.  Engine binary, codecs, and existing license docs come from the release derivation; data1/ from demodata; SUBLICENSE.txt added from assets/demo/.  Output is a single zip ready for upload to the GitHub release.

title: flake.nix: demodata derivation extracts data1 from tarball
description:
Pure (no network) Nix derivation that takes
assets/demo/hexen2demo_nov1997-linux-i586.tgz and exposes its data1/
subdirectory as a flake output.

Approximate shape:
  demodata = pkgs.runCommand "hexen2demo-data" {} ''
    mkdir -p $out
    tar -xzf ${./assets/demo/hexen2demo_nov1997-linux-i586.tgz} \
      -C $out --strip-components=1 \
      hexen2demo_nov1997/data1
  '';

Then nix build .#demodata produces $out/data1/ ready to copy into
release zips or pass as -DGAME_DATA_DIR= to the wasm build.

title: assets/demo: add license docs and provenance README
description:
Tarball is at assets/demo/hexen2demo_nov1997-linux-i586.tgz already.
Add alongside it:
  assets/demo/README.md — provenance, sha256, source URL, intended use
  assets/demo/HEXEN2_DEMO_LICENSE.txt — extracted from SUBLICENSE.doc
                                       (binary Word file inside the tar)
  assets/demo/DEMO.TXT — plain-text demo readme from the tarball
  assets/demo/ABOUT — release notes from the tarball (optional)

The .doc → .txt extraction can be done with a one-liner using libreoffice
or antiword, or by hand since it's small.

title: Fix WASM build: guard remaining _fp value-tests
description:
WASM build broken on master.  After OIT and GPU profiling landed on
desktop, two function-like macros (glBlendFunci_fp, glGenQueries_fp) are
tested as values in C, which doesn't compile under Emscripten where
those *_fp tokens are #defined to ((void)0).

Partial fix exists on branch wasm-demo (commit e542a799):
- gl_postprocess.c: introduce HW_OIT_HAS_BLEND_FUNCI helper (0 on
  Emscripten, runtime-tested on desktop).  Replace 4 value-test sites.
- gl_rmain.c R_ProfileInit: #ifdef __EMSCRIPTEN__ early-return.

The gl_worldcull.c part already shipped on master via the file-level
#ifndef __EMSCRIPTEN__ guard — no work needed there.

Reproducer:
  nix develop -f shell-wasm.nix --command bash -c 'cd engine && \
    mkdir -p build-wasm && cd build-wasm && \
    emcmake cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DUSE_CODEC_VORBIS=OFF \
      -DUSE_ALSA=OFF .. && emmake make -j$(nproc)'

Definition of done: clean WASM build, ~1.4 MB hexenwail.wasm produced.

title: Demo distributable: bundle engine + Hexen II demo data1 (Linux/Windows/Web)
description:
Build a redistributable demo package that bundles the modernized Hexenwail
engine with the Nov 1997 Hexen II demo data — same idea Hammer of Thyrion
has used for ~25 years.  Currently every release zip says "Requires game
data files (data1/pak0.pak, data1/pak1.pak)"; this gives us a "just run it"
download for new users and a one-click web build for hosting.

Tarball already in repo: assets/demo/hexen2demo_nov1997-linux-i586.tgz
(13 MB, sha256 2df15cde0128b7a036e71995e068ca853f13be8e2b591caac140025d66643fc0).
Source: uHexen2 v1.5.8 release — "3-level v1.11 Hexen II Demo from Nov.
1997 running on the Hammer of Thyrion engine".

== Deliverables ==

1. assets/demo/  — tarball + extracted license docs (HEXEN2_DEMO_LICENSE.txt
   from SUBLICENSE.doc, plus DEMO.TXT and ABOUT) + README explaining
   provenance and sha256.

2. flake.nix outputs:
   - demodata     — pure derivation, untar -> data1/ directory
   - demo-linux   — release/linux-x86_64 ∪ data1 → zip
   - demo-windows — release/windows-x86_64 ∪ data1 → zip
   - demo-wasm    — wasm build with -DGAME_DATA_DIR=…/data1, output is
                    index.html + .js + .wasm + .data preload bundle

3. CI: extend .github/workflows/release.yml so push of a v* tag also
   produces hexenwail-<ver>-demo-linux-x86_64.zip,
   hexenwail-<ver>-demo-windows-x86_64.zip, and
   hexenwail-<ver>-demo-web.zip, attached to the GitHub release.

4. Release notes: prepend a "Demo (no data needed)" section above the
   existing "Full version (BYO paks)" section.

5. License: each demo bundle ships with
     licenses/HEXEN2_DEMO_LICENSE.txt   (Raven/Activision sublicense)
     licenses/HEXENWAIL_LICENSE.txt     (engine GPL2+)
     DEMO_README.md                     (provenance + license summary)

== Prereq blocker ==

The WASM build is currently broken on master after some _fp value-tests
landed for OIT, GPU profiling, and compute world cull.  Partial fix on
branch wasm-demo (commit e542a799).  The gl_worldcull.c piece is already
fixed on master via #ifndef __EMSCRIPTEN__ at the top of the file, but
gl_postprocess.c (HW_OIT_HAS_BLEND_FUNCI helper) and gl_rmain.c
(R_ProfileInit guard) are not.

== Open questions ==

- Repo size: 13 MB tarball checked in.  Acceptable, or move to git-lfs?
- Web demo hosting: zip only, or also auto-deploy to GitHub Pages on
  release? (Two extra CI lines, gives a permanent URL.)
- Bundle naming: hexenwail-<ver>-demo-<plat>.zip vs.
  hexenwail-demo-<plat>.zip.  Former matches current scheme.

== Non-goals ==

- Replacing or competing with the existing "BYO paks" release.  Both go
  in the same GitHub release.
- Mac, Android, or Flatpak demo bundles for now — those can come later.
- Modifying demo data (paks travel verbatim).

title: Investigate: how does Ironwail actually do step-mover lerp (re-attempt)
description:
Reverted three rounds of step-mover lerp work (commits 6afc64e67, 532693939, 500879b8a) in the 0.7.1 → 0.7.2 cut because the result didn't hold up under SoT mod testing — Mathuzzz repeatedly reported 'lerping needs smoothing' even after multiple iterations.

What we tried (don't repeat blindly):
1. Phase 1 (6afc64e67): added R_AliasResolveLerp helper, LERP_RESETANIM2 two-stage reset, r_lerpmove cvar, set LERP_MOVESTEP from U_NOLERP. Phase 1 alone left step-movers using msg_origins lerp via cl_main.c.
2. Phase 2 (532693939): capped CL_LerpPoint extrapolation at frac=1 for LERP_MOVESTEP. Didn't help enough.
3. Phase 3 (500879b8a): Ironwail-style render-time fixed-window lerp at 0.1s — separate branch in CL_RelinkEntities using previousorigin/currentorigin/movelerpstart. Didn't help enough.

Hypothesis I had at the time: MOVETYPE_STEP entities think every 0.1s on a 20Hz tick (Hexen II's sys_ticrate = 0.05), so per-tick msg_origins lerp pauses every other tick → visible 'move-pause-move-pause' stutter.

What to investigate before the next attempt:
- Read Ironwail's R_SetupEntityTransform (Quake/r_alias.c line ~169) AND the surrounding scaffolding — particularly how it interacts with cl_main's CL_RelinkEntities. Ironwail uses a 0.1s window assuming Quake's 10Hz physics (sys_ticrate 0.1). At Hexen II's 20Hz our window timing assumption may be wrong.
- Trace the actual server tick rate that SoT mods run at. Some mods change sys_ticrate.
- Compare against how DarkPlaces / FTE handle MOVETYPE_STEP at 20Hz — they have decades of polish here.
- Reproduce the visible bug locally first (set up SoT, find a moving monster Mathuzzz mentioned, capture before/after frames).
- Decide: do we want fixed-window render-time lerp (Ironwail's approach) OR stick with msg_origins-based per-tick lerp + adaptive extrapolation?
- The entity_t fields previousorigin/currentorigin/previousangles/currentangles/movelerpstart still exist in render.h from earlier — we don't need to add them.

Reference Ironwail commits:
- /home/josh/tmp/claudedir/ironwail/Quake/r_alias.c R_SetupEntityTransform
- /home/josh/tmp/claudedir/ironwail/Quake/cl_parse.c LERP_MOVESTEP set on U_STEP bit
- /home/josh/tmp/claudedir/ironwail/Quake/cl_main.c interaction (look for r_lerpmove gating)

Reverted-from commits to consult (in our git history): 6afc64e67, 532693939, 500879b8a.

Don't re-attempt without reproducing the bug locally first.
close_reason: Root cause was a wire-up gap, not a window-timing issue. The LERP_MOVESTEP flag was defined in render.h but never set anywhere — the prior r5 frac-cap (76d1b7e5a) and r9 render-time lerp (1b6bd15fb) both gated on LERP_MOVESTEP and so were dead code at runtime, which is why Mathuzzz couldn't perceive any improvement. Fix: restored the U_NOLERP→LERP_MOVESTEP wire-up in cl_parse.c that was lost in the 0.7.1→0.7.2 cleanup of commit 6afc64e67, plus reinstated the Ironwail-style 0.1s render-time lerp branch in CL_RelinkEntities and the r_lerpmove cvar. Bumps r4→r5. The bead hypothesis (0.1s window vs 20Hz tick mismatch) cannot be evaluated until the gate flag is actually being set; if step-mover stutter persists with this enabled, then the window-timing hypothesis becomes testable.

title: Higher-quality sample resampler in ResampleSfx (snd_mem.c)
description:
Our snd_mem.c:30 ResampleSfx uses linear/nearest-neighbor interpolation (samplefrac approach) when resampling sound assets at load time to shm->speed. For 11025→44100 sounds (most of pak0/pak1), this introduces aliasing and a muddy high-frequency response.

Improvements to consider:
- Cubic / B-spline interpolator (cheap, ~2x cost over linear)
- Windowed-sinc resampler (high quality, more code)
- Cache the resampled output once per sound (already done — sfxcache_t->speed == shm->speed)

This is the load-time analog to Ironwail's paint-time snd_filterquality, but for our different pipeline. Not urgent: the audible improvement is subtle and most users won't notice. Filed as discovered-from from uhexen2-l67u (closed N/A) — that issue's premise didn't fit our architecture.

title: CSHC (not CSQC): evaluate FTE's ftehw.pk3 + Spoike's technical notes
description: Spoike (FTE dev) clarified: Hexen II uses CSHC not CSQC — OP_CALL1+ works differently, progs CRC computed differently. Current Hexenwail has PR_SaveVMState/PR_RestoreVMState which suggests capability to switch between SSHC and CSHC. Spoike questions need for uHexen2's v7 progs format (conflicts with fteqcc v7). FTE compiles entire Quake engine into 65k globals. Reference pk3: https://fte.triptohell.info/moodles/dev/ftehw.pk3 — handles H2 chunk system in gamecode. Stub check for csprogs.dat in cl_csqc.c has no VM behind it — was a no-op check.

title: Per-brush-entity liquid properties
description: Mappers want control over liquid properties per brush entity: transparency, shadows, lighting, warp speed, color, movement speed. Requested by Mathuzzz for SoT-style mapping flexibility.
notes: Requested by Mathuzzz — wants brush entity control for SoT mapping. BLOCKED: needs clarification from Mathuzzz before any work — ambiguous scope (e.g. 'movement speed' = conveyor flow? per-surface vs per-entity? demo-compat plan?). Ask before claiming.

title: Overbright lighting improvements
description: Overbright lighting makes scenes look much more vivid. WhiteMagicRaven and Mathuzzz confirm it's one of the most impactful visual features. Investigate further improvements to light quality — Hexen II has no real sunlight, only shadows, so overbright helps simulate it.
notes: Reported by WhiteMagicRaven and Mathuzzz — overbright is key to vivid lighting
close_reason: Closed

title: MD3 model format support
description: Support MD3 model format (Quake 3 / Quake Remastered). Ironwail has full MD3 support including per-surface textures. Would enable use of higher-poly community models. Reference Ironwail's implementation.
close_reason: Completed: Full MD3 import (all 5 phases) — loader, GPU upload, shader decode. See uhexen2-f2d3 for implementation details.

title: Gamepad second-layer bindings
description: Support a modifier/shift button on gamepad to access a second layer of bindings, doubling the number of available gamepad actions. Reference Ironwail's implementation.
close_reason: Ported from Ironwail: +altmodifier with GP_RTHUMB default, K_GP_*_ALT keycodes, fallback to base binding

title: Fence texture dFdx/dFdy ordering fix
description: When per-fragment dynamic lighting is added, ensure dFdx(in_pos)/dFdy(in_pos) plane computation happens before alpha discard in the world fragment shader. Prevents dark outlines on dynamically-lit fence textures. Reference: Ironwail commit 017fdd2d.
close_reason: N/A for current codebase — the fix only matters with per-fragment dynamic lighting (Ironwail's clustered per-tile path). Hexenwail uses the classic lightmap atlas approach (R_AddDynamicLights writes to the atlas, fragment shader just samples). The world fragment shader has no dFdx/dFdy plane calculation. Re-file when/if we ever port per-fragment dlight clustering.

title: GPU texture compression (BC/DXT)
description: Compress textures on GPU upload using BC1/BC3/BC7 formats to reduce VRAM usage and improve cache performance. Reference Ironwail's texture compression system with quality tuning.

title: Re-enable shadow mapping pipeline
description: New per-pixel shadow mapping system (GL_Shadow_RenderMaps, GL_Shadow_BindForScene) disabled to isolate translucency regression. r_shadow cvar exists, shaders written, just needs GL state hygiene audit before re-enabling.

title: Re-enable soft sprites with proper depth bias
description: Soft sprite shader discards fragments within 0.001 of scene depth, clipping weapon impact sprites that spawn on hit surfaces. Need a larger depth bias tolerance or offset-based approach. Currently disabled.

title: Add remaining notable cvars to menus
description: Cvars missing from menus: r_hdr, r_hdr_exposure, r_oit, sbtrans, hide_hud, gl_coloredlight, gl_colored_dynamic_lights, gl_overbright_models, gl_torch_dlight, m_filter, bgmtype, loadas8bit. in_gamepad already done.
close_reason: Added 7 of the 12 listed cvars to menus: r_hdr, r_hdr_exposure (Rendering), sbtrans, gl_overbright_models, gl_coloredlight, gl_torch_dlight, gl_glow_intensity (Misc/HUD), m_filter (Game). Skipped: r_oit (force-off until OIT translucent path is rewired, see uhexen2-a0hp), bgmtype (already exposed as Music Type), hide_hud (cheat-style), loadas8bit (only takes effect at sound load time, mid-game toggle is misleading UX), gl_colored_dynamic_lights (too similar to gl_coloredlight).

title: WASM: User game data upload (data1 PAK files)
description: Allow users to upload their Hexen II data1 folder for web gameplay. Implement: (1) HTML5 file input for selecting data1 folder/pak files, (2) JavaScript file reader to load into Emscripten VFS, (3) IndexedDB persistence cache so users don't re-upload, (4) Graceful degradation if no data provided (show message). Supports drag-and-drop for better UX.

title: Proper vsync frame pacer
description: The current broken-vsync watchdog (8f08e50fe) is a hack that caps at 500 FPS when vsync silently fails. A proper fix would: query actual display refresh rate, implement a real frame pacer, detect and recover vsync state changes, log frame time diagnostics.
close_reason: Fixed by 31d5ae3b0 — no reports since

title: Parallelize release workflow jobs
description: Release workflow runs flatpak and nix build serially (build needs: flatpak). Refactor to run both in parallel and merge artifacts in a final release job to save ~5-10 minutes.
close_reason: Done: build and flatpak jobs now parallel, publish job depends on both

title: Add dependency/CVE scanning
description: No Dependabot or similar tracking for nix flake input vulnerabilities. Add automated dependency scanning to catch CVEs in upstream packages.

title: Submit to Flathub
description: Submit com.github.bobberb.hexenwail to Flathub so it appears in GNOME Software, KDE Discover, and Steam Deck's app store. Manifest is at flatpak/com.github.bobberb.hexenwail.yml. Requires meeting Flathub quality guidelines and submitting a PR to github.com/flathub. Key motivation: Steam Deck users can install via Discover without workarounds.

title: FFmpeg audio plugin/backend
description: Consider an optional FFmpeg backend for broad audio format support (AC3, AAC, WMA, etc.) without adding individual codec libraries. Low priority — current WAV/FLAC/Vorbis/MP3/Opus covers all practical use cases. Would be useful if community music packs ship exotic formats.

title: Restore module tracker music support (MOD/XM/S3M/IT)
description: HoT supported module tracker formats via CODECTYPE_MOD in bgmusic.c. The format routing table and type bit exist but no codec implementation was ever wired up. Wire up libxmp or mikmod to complete the streamer.
notes: Tracker codec implementation was present in old commits as of 2024 — restore from git history rather than writing from scratch. Take all tracker codec support from upstream sezero (uHexen2 / Hammer of Thyrion).
close_reason: Superseded by uhexen2-5x6 (P1)

title: Cascaded shadow maps
description: High-quality outdoor shadows with distance-based cascade levels.

title: Texture streaming (on-demand hi-res loading)
description: Load high-resolution textures on demand instead of all at startup.

title: Screen-space reflections
description: Water and floor reflections via screen-space raymarching.

title: Depth of field post-process
description: Focal blur for cinematic feel. Toggle.

title: Motion blur post-process
description: Camera and per-object motion blur. Toggle.
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-cut (motion blur), already implemented

title: Light probes for indirect lighting
description: Sample indirect light for models in dark areas. Improves model integration with environment.

title: Volumetric fog
description: Per-area fog volumes instead of global fog. Brush entity fog triggers.

title: PBR material support
description: Metallic/roughness workflow for hi-res texture packs.

title: Dynamic cubemap reflections
description: Real-time cubemap reflections for armor, water, metal surfaces.

title: Skeletal animation support
description: Replace vertex keyframes with bone-based animation. Huge mod potential.

title: Decals system (blood, scorch marks)
description: Projected decals on world surfaces for impacts, blood, explosions.

title: Normal mapping for BSP surfaces
description: Add detail to flat BSP surfaces via generated or supplied normal maps.

title: Screen-space ambient occlusion (SSAO)
description: Post-process depth-based corner/cavity darkening.

title: Shadow mapping for world lights
description: Basic depth shadow maps for static world lights. Toggle.
close_reason: Done in 4ffb3df52..31a91ce62 — shadow mapping system implemented (world + entity, WIP)

title: Audit: verify SV_TouchLinks fix (PR #40) still applied
description: Shanjaq PR #40 fixed SV_TouchLinks reference handling. Verify the fix is still correct in current world.c. Reference: https://github.com/Shanjaq/uhexen2/pull/40
close_reason: Audit complete - all PR fixes verified present in current codebase

title: Audit: verify datagram size fix for connecting clients (PR #29)
description: Shanjaq PR #29 fixed datagram size for connecting clients. Verify fix is still present in net_dgrm.c. Reference: https://github.com/Shanjaq/uhexen2/pull/29
close_reason: Audit complete - all PR fixes verified present in current codebase

title: Audit: autoload fix (PR #24) removed
description: Shanjaq's autoload fix from PR #24 was removed during modernization. Investigate what autoload did and whether it's needed. Reference: https://github.com/Shanjaq/uhexen2/pull/24
close_reason: Audit complete - all PR fixes verified present in current codebase

title: Audit: zdoom-style multiload (PR #9) removed
description: Shanjaq's zdoom-style multiload feature (multiple -game args) from PR #9 was removed during modernization. May not be needed since we have runtime mod switching via Mods menu. Close if confirmed unnecessary. Reference: https://github.com/Shanjaq/uhexen2/pull/9
close_reason: Audit complete - all PR fixes verified present in current codebase

title: Port Ironwail menu system improvements
description:
Port Ironwail's modernized menu system to Hexenwail. Key areas to evaluate and port:

- Mouse-driven menu navigation (click on items, scroll wheel support)
- Slider controls with finer granularity
- Tooltip/description text for menu options
- Menu scaling and layout for widescreen resolutions
- Searchable console command completion
- Key binding UI improvements (conflict detection, unbind support)
- Options organization and categorization
- Visual polish (smooth transitions, hover highlighting)

Reference: https://github.com/andrei-drexler/ironwail
Note: Hexenwail already has custom Display/Sound/Game submenus and BigString rendering — port should adapt Ironwail patterns to existing Hexen II menu aesthetic rather than replacing wholesale.

title: Add sky wind animation (scrolling sky texture)
description: Implement scrolling/wind effect on sky textures for more dynamic skies. Reference Ironwail or other Quake engine forks for implementation.
close_reason: The two-layer scrolling sky IS this feature. Fixed in sky shader update.

title: Add lightmap support for liquid surfaces
description: Liquid surfaces (water, lava, slime) should receive lightmaps instead of being fullbright or uniformly lit. Reference Ironwail/vkQuake implementations.
close_reason: Duplicate of uhexen2-65r

title: Port screenshot code from Ironwail
description: Ironwail has improved screenshot functionality (PNG support, auto-naming, etc). Port relevant screenshot improvements to Hexenwail.
close_reason: Screenshot improved: 4-digit numbering (0-9999), cleaner code, malloc instead of Hunk

title: Port bounding box debug rendering from Ironwail
description: Ironwail has bounding box visualization for entities (r_showbboxes). Useful for debugging collision, visibility, and entity placement. Port to Hexenwail.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add r_oit (order-independent transparency) from Ironwail
description: Ironwail implements OIT for correct alpha blending without requiring geometry sorting. More robust than r_alphasort but heavier on GPU. Should be a toggle (off by default, user opt-in). Port this approach to Hexenwail. Reference Ironwail source for implementation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add r_alphasort (back-to-front alpha sorting) from Ironwail
description: Ironwail sorts transparent/alpha entities back-to-front before rendering to fix alpha blending artifacts. Port this approach to Hexenwail. Should be enabled by default (like Ironwail). Reference Ironwail source for implementation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Menu background dimming
description: Darken/blur the game view behind menus for better readability. Common in modern Quake ports.
close_reason: Already implemented - Draw_FadeScreen() applies amber tint overlay for all menus except Display/Video (skipped for settings preview). See gl_draw.c:1287 and menu.c:5167.

title: Port utils/ and hw_utils/ to CMake
description: The 12+ utility tools (hcc, qbsp, light, vis, etc.) and hw_utils still only have GNU Makefiles. They should get CMake targets so the entire project can be built from a single build system. The utils still depend on scripts/makefile.inc and scripts/detect.sh which are kept for now.

title: Port h2ded dedicated server to CMake
description: The dedicated server (h2ded) only had a GNU Makefile which was removed in the build system cleanup. It needs a CMake target added to engine/CMakeLists.txt to be buildable again.

title: Investigate missing Portal of Praevus ambient sounds (scream1-3.wav)
description: Portal of Praevus maps reference sound/ambience/scream1.wav, scream2.wav, scream3.wav but these files are not in pak3.pak (47MB, 245 files). May be missing from some distributions. Investigate whether these exist in complete retail installs, other paks, or were cut content. Low priority — game plays fine without them.
close_reason: Investigation complete: scream1-3.wav are not referenced in engine or QuakeC source. Portal of Praevus ambient sounds use moan1-3.wav for soundtype 13 (moans/screams). If any maps reference scream files via BSP ambient entities, the engine already handles missing sounds gracefully (console warning, no crash). Not a real bug — likely a documentation artifact or cut content.
